Architectural conflict with open bead ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7), plus the bead materially exceeds the size-check thresholds. Human triage required to resolve the design conflict before any unification work commits. ## Summary The bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector. These are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either: (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned. The bead's "SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make. ## Evidence 1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims). 2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: "Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands." Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`. 3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites). 4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM ("ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. The remaining server-spawn path is via `POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop` (`cli/internal/server/server.go:2390`) and the GraphQL `workerDispatchAdapter` (`cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127`), both of which ADR-022 step 7 wants to delete in favor of subprocess exec. 5. Recent ddx-eccc6efb attempt (20260503T065047-61e4ed20, result.json) was DECLINED for size and decomposed into proposed children 7a/7b/7c — those children may not yet be filed in the main repo per its own bookkeeping note, but the intent stands. ## Size-check verdict (independent of the conflict) The bead also fails the size check on its own: - 17 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (cobra flag binding, client-side validation, HTTP handler, GraphQL adapter, workers.go consumer, on-disk persistence, backward-compat for legacy fields, forward-compat with SpecVersion, custom Duration codec, reflection-driven test, manual end-to-end run). - Touches at minimum: new package `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/` (struct + Validate + ApplyDefaults + custom Duration + tests), `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (cobra rebind + dispatch split), `cli/internal/server/server.go` (handler rewrite + handler tests), `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` + `graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go` + `workers_config_test.go` (parallel-construction deletion), `cli/internal/server/workers.go` (consumer rewrite), and a literal sweep across at least 8 test files (`workers_test.go`, `workers_e2e_test.go`, `workers_prune_test.go`, `workers_stop_test.go`, `workers_stop_propagation_test.go`, `workers_watchdog_test.go`, plus the two GraphQL test files) that all construct `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{}` literals. - File-size signal: workers.go 1959 LOC, server.go 5248 LOC, agent_cmd.go 2245 LOC, workers_test.go 1107 LOC. Estimated change footprint comfortably >500 LOC across >5 files in unrelated packages. - Per execution-bead protocol size check: "More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems" + "More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages" → DECOMPOSE. ## Why I am not decomposing in this attempt The protocol's decomposition path commits the project to a particular shape (in this case: unification rather than deletion). Filing 4–6 child beads under ddx-29058e2a would advance an architectural direction that is in unresolved conflict with ddx-eccc6efb. A clean decomposition only makes sense AFTER the parent-vs-step-7 design choice is made by a human: Option A — Drop ddx-29058e2a in favor of ADR-022 step 7. The parallel-struct smell goes away by deletion, not unification. The 5 currently-dropped flags can be patched as a narrow fix on the existing struct in the meantime if the operator workflow needs them, with a note that step 7 will erase the struct. Option B — Keep ddx-29058e2a and amend / close ddx-eccc6efb. The unified spec becomes the canonical wire format; ADR-022 step 7 reduces in scope (or is closed as superseded) because the server keeps marshalling a spec, just a unified one. Option C — Sequence: land the narrow 5-flag fix now (small bead), then land ADR-022 step 7 (which deletes the struct and obsoletes the parallel-struct smell), then close ddx-29058e2a as superseded. Each option has different downstream implications (ddx-fb290074 audit scope, REACH-PROTO entry roots, where Validate/ApplyDefaults live, whether SpecVersion is needed at all). I do not have authority to pick. ## Recommended next actions for the orchestrator / triage 1. Decide which of A / B / C above is the intended path. This is a 5-minute design call, not a research task. 2. If A: close ddx-29058e2a as superseded; optionally file a small fix bead "add MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec + propagate through handler" (~50 LOC, will be deleted by step 7 anyway). 3. If B: amend ddx-eccc6efb's scope (or close it), then decompose ddx-29058e2a into ~5 children: (i) new spec package + Validate/ApplyDefaults + Duration codec + reflection test, (ii) cobra rebind + parseExecuteLoopSpec + dispatch split, (iii) HTTP handler decode-into-spec rewrite + delete handler-local request struct, (iv) GraphQL adapter unification, (v) workers.go consumer migration + workers_*_test literal sweep + spec.json on-disk format flip + SpecVersion gate. 4. If C: file the narrow 5-flag fix as its own bead now; pause ddx-29058e2a until after step 7 lands, at which point most of its ACs disappear and what's left (if anything) can be re-scoped. ## What this attempt did NOT do (and why) - No code changes. The conflict is upstream of any implementation choice. - No child beads filed. Filing children would commit to the unification path; see "Why I am not decomposing" above. - No partial fix to the 5 dropped flags. The bead explicitly absorbs that narrow fix; doing it here without addressing the unification framing would muddy the bead's status.

acceptance:
1. .ddx/config.yaml documents bead_tracker.backend: axon plus the Axon connection block.
2. TestNewStore_DefaultsToJSONL, TestNewStore_SelectsAxonFromConfig, and TestNewStore_PreservesExternalBackends are added or updated.
3. config selection honors axon without requiring DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL, while jsonl remains the default.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The store factory still treats Axon as an experimental special case and only enables it behind an environment gate. The relevant branch is cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137, which falls back to JSONL unless DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL is truthy, even when config has backend: axon. The checked-in sample config also does not document the bead_tracker backend block yet (.ddx/config.yaml:1-17).

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137 and .ddx/config.yaml:1-17 do not present Axon as a normal selectable backend. The config type already has a Backend field, but the factory still requires an env override and emits a warning instead of honoring configuration directly.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update the store factory so bead_tracker.backend=axon selects the Axon backend from config without requiring the experimental env var.
- Update the checked-in .ddx/config.yaml example to show the backend and Axon connection block.
- Keep the default backend unchanged when backend is unset.
- Preserve bd/br behavior for the existing external backend path.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Axon backend CRUD internals.
- Subscription transport.

acceptance:
1. GraphQL schema includes proposed and cancelled in status docs and status counts, and distinguishes operator-attention, dependency-waiting, and external-blocked derived buckets.
2. needsHuman fields/queries/mutations are removed from canonical GraphQL/API surfaces rather than preserved as runtime aliases.
3. The human resolve mutation is replaced with lifecycle transition mutations for proposed beads: approve/retry to open, close to closed, cancel to cancelled, or block to blocked with external blocker metadata.
4. REST/MCP status filter and update schemas list proposed/open/in_progress/blocked/closed/cancelled and route updates through the transition API.
5. REST/MCP blocked endpoints use external blocker semantics; dependency-waiting has a distinct output path or field.
6. TestGraphQLBeadsNeedsHumanQuery is removed or replaced with proposed/operator-attention behavior.
7. TestGraphQLBeadStatusCountsNeedsHuman is replaced with TestGraphQLBeadStatusCountsLifecycleStates.
8. TestGraphQLBeadHumanResolve* tests are replaced or updated for proposed lifecycle transitions.
9. TestMCPBeadStatusSchemasIncludeSixLifecycleStatuses, TestBeadsBlocked, and TestMCPBeadBlocked assert six-status and external-blocked versus dependency-waiting semantics.
10. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run 'TestGraphQL.*Proposed|TestGraphQL.*StatusCounts|TestGraphQL.*Human|TestMCPBeadStatusSchemasIncludeSixLifecycleStatuses|TestBeadsBlocked|TestMCPBeadBlocked' -count=1` passes.
11. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/bead/...` passes.
12. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Server, REST/MCP, and GraphQL surfaces expose needsHuman as if it were a lifecycle lane and use blocked endpoints for dependency waits. That will keep external clients and the web UI tied to old semantics unless the API contract is updated with the state machine.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:100-140 documents Bead status without proposed/cancelled and includes needsHuman fields.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:177-192 defines BeadStatusCounts.needsHuman and says workerReady excludes needs_human/needs_investigation.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_beads.go:114-125 computes NeedsHuman from labels.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_beads.go:167-238 exposes BeadsNeedsHuman as open plus label.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go:228-273 implements beadHumanResolve by removing needs_human labels or closing.
- cli/internal/server/server.go:3181 and cli/internal/server/server.go:3418 advertise only open/in_progress/closed status filters/updates in MCP schemas.
- cli/internal/server/server.go:3220-3222 describes ddx_bead_blocked as open beads with unmet dependencies.

PROPOSED FIX
Make API contracts use the six-status lifecycle and derived buckets. Remove needsHuman fields/queries/mutations from the canonical schema rather than keeping long-lived aliases. REST/MCP and GraphQL status filters/updates must accept proposed, open, in_progress, blocked, closed, and cancelled, and status mutations must call the transition API. Blocked endpoints must mean external recheckable blockers; dependency-waiting is a distinct derived bucket. Old clients fail clearly after the one-way migration instead of receiving label-based compatibility behavior.

NON-SCOPE
Do not update Svelte UI in this bead except generated schema/types required by the server contract. Frontend has a separate bead.

DEPS
Depends on store/query wiring and migration semantics.
title: server: expose proposed lifecycle over API and GraphQL

acceptance:
AC1. ddx try/ddx work treats success outcome and bead-state propagation as a single recoverable unit: if the finalize step fails after outcome=task_succeeded is written, the next pick of the same bead detects the dangling result_rev and either lands it or surfaces a structured error (not silent no_evidence_produced).
AC2. A regression test exercises the dangling-success scenario by simulating a finalize failure and asserting the bead lands cleanly on retry.
AC3. ddx doctor (or a new diagnostic command) detects executions where result.json claims success but result_rev is unreachable from any branch, and reports them with the recovery command (git merge --no-ff <sha> + ddx bead close --commit <sha> --session <sid>).
AC4. Documentation in the ddx skill or work.md describes this failure mode and how operators can recover manually until AC1 ships.
description:
PROBLEM: A ddx try/ddx work attempt that produces outcome=task_succeeded and a clean result_rev may fail to auto-merge or update the bead state, leaving the success commit unreachable from any branch and the bead stuck in_progress. Operators must manually recover by checking out the dangling SHA, verifying ACs, and merging.

REPRO (observed in axon project, 2026-05-08):
- Bead axon-d323acc9 ("refactor(axon-schema): import named-query parser from axon-cypher-ast")
- Attempt 20260508T190516-8d61a244, session eb-b068c4db
- result.json fields: outcome=task_succeeded, status=success, result_rev=c3386193ab1c18fdf5739901c41d2e69093f1030, base_rev=9194da47097b2d0d05899a51688950084b3995b8, duration_ms=1179708, exit_code=0
- Master HEAD never advanced to include c3386193; bead remained in_progress until manual recovery
- A subsequent attempt 20260508T192511-c4a200de fired 15 seconds later and recorded outcome=task_no_evidence with detail "agent exited without a commit or no_changes_rationale.txt", which suggests the worker re-ran and saw no work to do (the success was already in the dangling commit) but did not connect that to the prior success record.

CURRENT STATE:
- Both attempt result.json files are present and parsable.
- Recovery required: git merge --no-ff <result_rev> + ddx bead close --commit <result_rev> --session <session_id>.
- No evidence in the bead events indicated the attempt had succeeded; the success was discovered by inspecting result.json on disk.

PROPOSED FIX DIRECTION (owned by ddx-loop/finalize maintainer):
1. The post-execute finalize step (merge + bead update with success event) should be transactional with attempt outcome, or at minimum idempotent so a follow-up ddx work cycle can complete it.
2. On bead pick, if status=in_progress and the most-recent attempt outcome=success but no merge commit references the result_rev, attempt to land that result before claiming new work or starting a fresh attempt.
3. A startup self-check could enumerate result.json files where outcome=task_succeeded and ensure the corresponding result_rev is reachable from a branch; surface inconsistencies as recoverable state.

NON-SCOPE: defining the merge strategy (no-ff vs ff-only is already settled by skill guidance); changing result.json schema.
title: bug: ddx try success outcome leaves dangling commit when finalize step fails

description:
PROBLEM
Tasks are still able to leave .ddx/beads.jsonl dirty even after tracker mutations. The pre-commit guard catches later commits that would leave tracker dirt behind, but it cannot catch a task that mutates the tracker and exits before another commit. This repository also does not currently opt into git.auto_commit, so bead-mutating commands only change the JSONL file unless the operator remembers to commit it manually.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- .ddx/config.yaml:1-30 has no git.auto_commit setting, so cli/cmd/bead.go:78-97 returns without committing tracker mutations by default.
- cli/cmd/bead.go:74-97 documents that beadAutoCommit errors are silently ignored and drops the error returned by internal/git.AutoCommit.
- cli/internal/git/autocommit.go:58-72 stages the target file and runs git commit without path limiting, so if auto-commit is enabled it can include unrelated staged changes instead of being a tracker-only safety action.

PROPOSED FIX
- Set this repo's .ddx/config.yaml git.auto_commit to always with a chore commit prefix so bead commands create tracker commits by default.
- Change beadAutoCommit to return errors and make mutating bead commands fail loudly when the configured tracker auto-commit fails.
- Restrict AutoCommit's git commit to the target file so auto-commit cannot accidentally include unrelated staged work.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove the pre-commit beads-tracker-health guard; it remains the fallback for manual commits.
- Do not require all .ddx runtime/evidence artifacts to be auto-committed.
- Do not change the bead storage format.
title: bead: hard-fail tracker auto-commit misses

description:
PROBLEM
ddx work currently continues scanning and claiming ready beads after setup/infrastructure failures. During the May 6 ENOSPC incident, ddx work failed to create one isolated worktree, then immediately claimed another production-reachability bead and failed again. Resource exhaustion must stop the drain because continuing expands the blast radius.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:437-530 initializes loop events and exitReason but does not include resource_exhausted as a recognized loop-fatal exit reason.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:529 onward continues iterating after non-success reports unless existing retry/no-progress/once logic stops it; there is no host-resource circuit breaker.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go:263-283 defines status taxonomy around execution_failed/no_evidence/etc., but there is no distinct resource_exhausted status/reason for loop policy to inspect.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:434-440 and docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:281-284 require ddx work to stop claiming new beads after resource_exhausted.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a stable resource_exhausted attempt/loop classification that can be returned by ddx try/resource preflight and recognized by ExecuteBeadWorker.
- On resource_exhausted, release any non-terminal claim, emit cleanup/resource events, set loop.end exit_reason=resource_exhausted, and return without scanning the next ready bead.
- Print an operator-visible message such as resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop.
- Ensure resource_exhausted is not counted as model no-progress, capability retry, or review/quota retry.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the resource checker itself here; this bead consumes the outcome from the try/resource-preflight bead.
- Do not change queue ranking or production-reachability bead priority.
- Do not change provider 429 in-attempt retry behavior.

acceptance:
1. TestExecutionCleanup_RemovesStaleDDXScratchDirs creates stale allowlisted DDx scratch prefixes outside the execution worktree root and verifies cleanup removes them only after the configured age/liveness checks pass.
2. TestExecutionCleanup_PreservesActiveDDXScratchDirs verifies fresh mtime, live PID/liveness, and non-DDx prefixes are preserved.
3. TestExecutionCleanup_ReclaimsExpiredTestOwnedWorktrees verifies stale DDx metadata from temp test project roots can be reclaimed when no active git worktree/liveness remains, while active foreign project worktrees remain preserved.
4. TestCleanupCommand_ReportsScratchReclamation verifies ddx cleanup --json and human output include scratch dir counts plus bytes/inodes reclaimed.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run "TestExecutionCleanup_RemovesStaleDDXScratchDirs|TestExecutionCleanup_PreservesActiveDDXScratchDirs|TestExecutionCleanup_ReclaimsExpiredTestOwnedWorktrees|TestCleanupCommand_ReportsScratchReclamation" -count=1 passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The production cleanup manager is too narrow for the resource failure we just had to repair by hand. ddx cleanup reported little or no reclaimable space because it only owns execution worktree directories, while DDx-generated e2e/test scratch directories and generated test binaries can consume many GiB in the same temp filesystem.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:202-212 reads entries only under the execution temp root and skips anything without ExecuteBeadWtPrefix. This excludes DDx-owned scratch prefixes created by command/e2e tests and generated binaries.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:234-240 preserves ownership mismatches unconditionally, which is correct for foreign projects but means test-owned DDx execution worktrees are never reclaimed by an operator running cleanup from the main project.
- cli/cmd/cleanup.go:64-67 constructs the cleanup manager with default scope only; there is no CLI or manager option to include safe DDx scratch prefixes.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:69-88 summary fields only count execution temp dirs, registered worktrees, and run-state files, so broader scratch cleanup would not be observable without new fields.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend ExecutionCleanupManager with an explicit DDx scratch cleanup scope driven by allowlisted prefixes/metadata, minimum age, and liveness/process checks.
- Include DDx-created e2e/test scratch roots and generated test binary directories in that scope without touching arbitrary temp directories.
- Keep foreign-project execution worktree preservation by default, but add a safe test-owned/expired-DDx metadata path when liveness is stale and the directory is not registered active.
- Extend cleanup summaries and CLI JSON/human output with scratch dirs removed, scratch bytes/inodes reclaimed, preserved active scratch count, and warnings.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not delete non-DDx temp directories or user-managed caches.
- Do not remove complete .ddx/executions, .ddx/runs, refs/ddx/iterations/*, active registered worktrees, or live liveness-marked paths.
- Do not change work-loop stop policy; ddx-fc6b42d2 wires cleanup into the claim cycle after this bead.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-cf37253d for the updated spec contract. Builds on closed beads ddx-5d154c5d (cleanup manager) and ddx-ef2a7638 (operator cleanup command); those are contextual predecessors, not active blocking deps.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx has a partial tier-label mechanism, but durable bead-level execution hints are underspecified. Agents can currently copy route-shaped settings into bead metadata without a clear lint/audit contract, and smart-tier usage is not tied to a required justification.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-037-bead-execution-hints.md:23-39 documents that `ddx try` infers tier from labels today, but arbitrary custom fields are preserved without a routing contract. docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-037-bead-execution-hints.md:62-92 requires smart justification, and lines 112-161 require durable route-pin rejection plus execution-routing-intent evidence.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: This bead defines and implements the durable execution-hint contract that later prose beads rely on. Weak implementation would let agents persist concrete model or harness pins into queue metadata, directly violating ADR-024.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add typed parsing for `tier:cheap`, `tier:standard`, and `tier:smart` labels.
- Extract and validate a `SMART JUSTIFICATION:` section for smart-tier beads.
- Add bead lifecycle lint findings for missing smart justification and durable route-pin fields/labels.
- Record `execution-routing-intent` evidence with source, requested tier/power, smart justification, actual route facts, and degradation notes.
- Extend agent metrics projection enough to audit smart-hint usage and rejected durable route pins.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add durable bead-level harness/provider/model/model-ref pins.
- Do not change Fizeau routing internals.
- Do not implement a materialized metrics rollup store.

DEPS
No deps. This must land before the Vale/prose child beads rely on tier hints.

description:
CONTEXT
DDx needs deterministic default prose rules and vocabulary so the CLI can flag common AI-writing tropes without relying on subjective model judgment. The rules must be tuned for DDx technical documents and configurable by projects.

RULE REQUIREMENTS

- Detect repeated formulaic openings or filler transitions where practical.
- Support project vocabulary accept/reject lists.
- Support at least technical and planning modes; public mode may share defaults if TD-036 says so.
- Keep findings advisory metadata-ready: rule id, severity, rationale, suggested edit.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- ddx doc prose command wiring.
- Review integration.
- Any AI-authorship score.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx pre-close review and review repair currently exist as partially overlapping ideas instead of one candidate-cycle pipeline. Recent tracker evidence shows review errors and review_malfunction outcomes can be recorded after implementation commits without a reliable same-worktree repair loop, which makes it unclear when to land, preserve, retry, or close.

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:668-680 defines ExecuteBeadWithConfig as a worker that creates an isolated worktree, returns a task result, and leaves merge/preserve/recovery to the parent.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:765-767 removes the implementation worktree via a defer before any later post-merge review path could reuse it.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1086-1104 invokes RunPostMergeReview only after the loop has a success report from the implementation/landing path.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:53-77 describes the review state machine as post-merge/pre-close and explicitly notes repair-cycle extraction is still future work.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:337-356 already wants adversarial review and repair-cycle behavior, but does not yet define the same-worktree candidate-cycle pipeline in implementable detail.

PROPOSED FIX
Create a decomposed implementation epic for the candidate-cycle refactor. The child beads will specify and then implement: candidate vocabulary, read-only reviewer enforcement, attempt-cycle coordinator interfaces, project-root candidate refs, checks-before-review, same-worktree pre-land review, finding classification, same-worktree repair, power-floor routing, two-slot review, retirement of post-land review semantics, and cleanup/recovery hardening.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not directly edit implementation code in this epic bead.
- Do not manually edit .ddx/beads.jsonl; all child work must be created and managed through ddx bead commands.
- Do not remove existing related beads such as ddx-639af4ef or ddx-9228a484; this epic coordinates the refined replacement/dependency path.
title: agent: refactor review repair into candidate-cycle pipeline

description:
Hand-curated sub-agent prompts have been outperforming ddx work / ddx try in this session. Two competing hypotheses for why:
H1: ddx execution machinery (the 9 fail-prone layers) is unreliable — failure is in the workers/server code.
H2: Bead authoring quality is insufficient — beads punt investigation; hand-curation papers over the gap.

Both are plausible. Determining the relative contribution requires a 3-path study, NOT the original 2-path proposal.

THREE PATHS

Path A: ddx try / ddx work with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (current production path).
Path B: Sub-agent dispatched with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (NOT hand-curated). Same prompt as A; different executor. Isolates ddx machinery from prompt quality.
Path C: Sub-agent dispatched with operator hand-curated prompt (file:line cites, named tests, explicit non-scope, etc.). Establishes the ceiling.

INTERPRETATION
- A vs B differ → ddx machinery is the failure (file follow-ups against the 9 layers).
- B vs C differ → bead authoring quality is the failure (already covered by bead-quality-audit + retrofit).
- A == B << C → both are real, with bead quality being the dominant gap.

METHOD
Pick 5 representative CLOSED beads as fixtures: one trivial fix, one refactor child, one test addition, one multi-file feature, one documentation update. For each fixture, run all 3 paths on a branch from the bead's base-rev (parent of the original work commit). Throwaway commits — the original work is already in main. We're measuring path properties, not producing mergeable code.

MEASUREMENTS
Per path per bead:
- Time-to-first-claim (A) / time-to-first-output (B,C)
- Time-to-merge-eligible-commit (whether or not we merge)
- Attempts (A) / iterations (B,C)
- Status outcomes: success / no_changes / preserved / blocked / errored
- Cost: tokens, USD
- Manual interventions
- Failure mode classified per the 9-layer machinery list

OUTPUT
.ddx/executions/<run-id>/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md with:
- Per-bead per-path table
- Aggregate stats (median + p99)
- Failure-mode distribution
- Hypothesis verdict (H1 vs H2 vs both)
- Recommendations for which path is appropriate for which bead-shape

TIMING
This study runs AFTER the executeloop refactor (C0-C13) lands so the ddx machinery is its post-refactor shape. Running pre-refactor measures behavior we're about to change. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES + bead-quality-audit + retrofit + refactor must all complete first. This bead is filed now and EXECUTION DEFERRED.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Fixing whatever the study reveals (separate bug beads filed from the study's findings)
- Running against pre-refactor ddx machinery (data we won't act on)
- Comparing different sub-agent harnesses (different concern)

EVIDENCE
- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and is partial Path B-vs-C data favoring the bead-quality hypothesis.
notes: Partial Path B-vs-C evidence: ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome, supporting the bead-quality hypothesis side of the study.
title: study: 3-path execution comparison (ddx work vs sub-agent + verbatim bead vs sub-agent + hand-curated)

description:
PROBLEM
The bead lifecycle is currently controlled by a mix of status values, lifecycle labels, ad hoc events, retry metadata, and command-specific conditionals. That makes ddx work unreliable: execution-ready work, operator-required work, dependency waits, and terminal outcomes can drift apart depending on which surface is queried.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:38 defines the six upstream status values, but later sections still make labels such as needs_human and triage:needs-investigation lifecycle-driving states.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1450 filters needs-investigation and needs_human labels in readiness logic, while cli/internal/bead/store.go:1548 treats ordinary dependency waiting as Blocked().
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1961 parks intake rejections by adding a needs_human label instead of moving the bead lifecycle into a single status field.
- cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:60 parses status: needs_investigation even though that value is not an upstream bead status.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:177 exposes needsHuman counts as lifecycle output, and the Svelte bead page hard-codes derived ready/incomplete status options.

PROPOSED FIX
Replace conflicting lifecycle behavior with one DDx-owned status state machine. Status is the sole lifecycle field. The accepted lifecycle values are proposed, open, in_progress, blocked, closed, and cancelled. proposed means operator decision required; open means accepted active work and may be dependency-waiting; in_progress means claimed/running; blocked means accepted work paused by an external recheckable blocker; closed means terminal satisfied and satisfies dependents; cancelled means terminal not-doing and does not satisfy dependents. Dependency waiting is derived from dependency closure, not status=blocked. needs_human and triage:needs-investigation stop being queue labels and become legacy metadata/events only where needed for migration or compatibility.

NON-SCOPE
Do not execute the refactor in this epic. Child beads own specs, pure state machine code, store/query wiring, agent outcome mapping, API/UI surfaces, skill contracts, and final conformance.

CHILD WORK
The children under this epic must keep the same invariant: lifecycle decisions call the pure state-machine contract rather than adding new one-off conditionals.
title: bead: replace lifecycle labels with status-owned state machine

description:
PROBLEM
  The conflict-recovery state machine (auto-merge + focused-resolve + close-or-park) is partially extracted but the description cites only the pre-extraction loop lines, leaving the executor without a clear picture of the current API boundary.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go:118 defines RunConflictRecovery(ctx, ConflictRecoveryInput) ConflictRecoveryOutput — the C4 extraction already landed. The bead description still refers to the old inline branch. The caller site is cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 (loop branches on ShouldAttemptConflictRecovery, then calls RunConflictRecovery). The try.Attempt API (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:67, func Attempt(ctx, store, beadID, AttemptOpts) (Outcome, error)) does not yet call RunConflictRecovery; conflict recovery remains loop-owned.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Migrate RunConflictRecovery invocation from execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 into try.Attempt (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go) so Attempt's return Outcome already encodes conflict-recovery disposition.
  - Add try/conflict_recovery.go: thin adapter from ConflictRecoveryOutput to Outcome (Disposition=Merged → OutcomeSuccess, Park/NeedsHuman → OutcomePark).
  - Loop caller (execute_bead_loop.go:847) delegates to try.Attempt instead of calling RunConflictRecovery directly; removes the inline branch.
  - Add TestAttempt_ConflictRecovered_ReturnsMergedOutcome and TestAttempt_ConflictUnresolvable_ReturnsPark to cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Deleting the attempted/hookFailed maps (C7, ddx-06eb05d8).
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: fail-open at the layer boundary when conflict recovery is not applicable or cannot complete cleanly.
- P2: conflict recovery owns only its own conditions; the loop should delegate rather than infer recovery policy.
- P3: the worker panel should surface conflict-recovery transitions and any degraded path.
- P4: a bad bead X must not spill into bead Y; conflict recovery stays per-bead.

acceptance:
1. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentPreClaimIntakeRunsOncePerBead` starts two workers against one ready bead with a blocking `PreClaimIntakeHook` and proves only one worker enters the model-backed intake for that bead while the other observes it as unavailable or moves on.
2. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentPreDispatchLintDoesNotDuplicateEvents` proves two workers cannot append duplicate `bead-quality.lint` events for the same readiness attempt before ownership is established.
3. `TestPreClaimIntakeRewriteRequiresOwnedReservation` proves `actionable_but_rewritten` cannot update description/acceptance unless the worker owns the claim/readiness lease, and concurrent losers leave the bead unchanged.
4. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerReadinessRejectReleasesOrParksClaim` covers intake error, ambiguous/needs-human, and lint-block outcomes: retryable errors release the bead or set a bounded cooldown; terminal readiness outcomes park with explicit labels/metadata; actionable beads proceed to implementation.
5. Existing `TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentWorkersDoNotDoubleExecuteSameBead` and `TestExecuteLoop_TwoWorkersBothClaimP0sBeforeP2s` still pass, preserving duplicate-implementation protection and priority ordering.
6. WIRED-IN: production `ExecuteBeadWorker.Run` uses the new claim/reservation boundary before invoking `PreClaimIntakeHook`, `applyPreClaimIntakeRewrite`, or `appendPreDispatchLintEvent`.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run "TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentPreClaim|TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentPreDispatch|TestPreClaimIntakeRewriteRequiresOwnedReservation|TestExecuteBeadWorkerReadinessReject|TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentWorkersDoNotDoubleExecuteSameBead|TestExecuteLoop_TwoWorkersBothClaimP0sBeforeP2s" -count=1 passes.

9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work` now runs model-backed bead readiness intake and bead-lifecycle lint after selecting a ready bead but before atomically claiming it. With two workers on the same project, both workers can select the same bead, both spend model calls on intake/lint, and both can append pre-dispatch events before one loses `Store.Claim`. If intake returns `actionable_but_rewritten`, the loser can also mutate bead description/acceptance before owning the bead. Atomic `Store.Claim` still prevents duplicate implementation, but it no longer protects the expensive and stateful pre-claim phase.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:686-765 calls `runtime.PreClaimIntakeHook` and handles rewrite/block outcomes before `w.Store.Claim` is attempted.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:768-807 calls `runtime.PreDispatchLintHook` and `appendPreDispatchLintEvent` before the claim, so concurrent workers can append duplicate lint events for the same open bead.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:809-823 only detects the ownership race after intake/lint have already run.
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go:39-58 loads and updates bead description/acceptance from the intake rewrite path without a prior worker claim/ownership check.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:588-635 makes `ClaimWithOptions` the atomic ownership boundary, but `ReadyExecution` at cli/internal/bead/store.go:1352-1355 only lists candidates and does not reserve them.

PROPOSED FIX
- Move the expensive/model-backed readiness and lint gates behind an atomic worker reservation, or add an equivalent atomic `ReserveForReadiness` lease that removes the bead from other workers while intake/lint runs.
- If the chosen design uses ordinary claim before readiness: claim first, start heartbeat, run intake and lint, then unclaim/release on retryable intake errors, park with `needs_human`/triage metadata on terminal readiness outcomes, and continue to implementation only for actionable beads.
- If the chosen design uses a separate readiness lease: make `ReadyExecution` exclude fresh readiness leases, make stale leases reclaimable, and require rewrite/lint event mutations to verify lease ownership.
- Ensure `actionable_but_rewritten` applies metadata under the same ownership/lease used by the worker, so concurrent workers cannot double-rewrite or append conflicting events.
- Preserve the existing atomic `Store.Claim` duplicate-implementation guarantee and existing `picker.claim_race` telemetry for true claim races.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing the bead-lifecycle rubric or model prompt content.
- Removing readiness/lint checks entirely.
- Changing final implementation landing/review semantics.

DEPS
No deps. This is a queue safety bug introduced by the current pre-claim intake ordering and can be fixed independently of the parked-bead triage work.

description:
PROBLEM
Even after a pure lifecycle state machine exists, DDx can still drift if status writers bypass it. Today commands and store helpers assign statuses directly, so invalid or semantically wrong transitions can keep entering the tracker from CLI, GraphQL, REST/MCP, claim/unclaim, close/reopen, or operator-prompt paths.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:535-562 exposes Store.Update, where callers can mutate b.Status directly before generic validation.
- cli/cmd/bead.go:525-526 assigns `b.Status = v` for `ddx bead update --status`.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:580-612 implements ClaimWithOptions with direct status checks and assignment.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:730-736 makes Unclaim set in_progress back to open directly.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1024-1027 and cli/internal/bead/store.go:1192-1209 close/reopen by assigning statuses directly.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go:228-256 currently resolves human-review state by mutating labels/closing rather than lifecycle transitions.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a status-transition write API, for example Store.TransitionLifecycle or Store.SetLifecycleStatus, that delegates to the pure lifecycle transition function and records transition reason/evidence. Route every status-writing path through it: update --status, claim, unclaim, close, close-with-evidence, reopen, operator prompt approve/cancel, GraphQL/REST/MCP update, migration application, and agent parking paths. Leave Store.Update for non-lifecycle fields but prevent arbitrary status assignment outside the transition API.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change ready/blocked query derivation in this bead. Do not add broad backcompat for legacy labels; old lifecycle labels are handled only by the one-way migrator.

DEPS
Depends on the pure lifecycle core and storage status preservation.
title: bead: enforce lifecycle transitions on every status write

description:
PROBLEM
DDx does not have a written contract that separates broad interactive queue stewardship from worker execution. An agent opened in the repo can interpret queue questions as permission to start edits, while ddx work is meant to pick up execution-ready beads.

ROOT CAUSE
AGENTS.md:1-18 enters bead policy without declaring a default interactive queue-steward mode. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:18-29 defines ddx run/try/work layers but not the interactive mode boundary around them. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:28-32 routes phrases like "do work", "what's on the queue", and "what's ready" through the ddx skill without specifying whether those prompts mean human intervention triage or worker execution.

PROPOSED FIX
Update AGENTS.md, FEAT-010, and FEAT-011 with an explicit mode taxonomy: queue_steward for broad interactive DDx questions, bead_execution for ddx work/try/execute-bead worker prompts, direct_user_implementation for explicit human requests to edit code/docs, and review for read-only review asks. Define precedence so bead_execution supersedes only the queue-steward default, while tracker, merge-policy, and safety instructions remain load-bearing.

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing CLI behavior beyond static guidance tests.
- Changing tracker status enums or storage.
- Starting or managing workers automatically from interactive sessions.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-09d7d787. Dependencies: none.

description:
PROBLEM
  The execute-bead subsystem files use execute_bead_* naming that conflates the orchestrator, the attempt, the review, and the loop under one prefix, making package boundaries and import graphs ambiguous. The refactor proposal was stored at /tmp/ and is unreadable post-session.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (drain loop), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (attempt), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go (land coordinator), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go (landing), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (review), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go (review triage), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go (disposition), and cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go (mirror) all share a flat package with unrelated concerns. The ddx try CLI command named in FEAT-010 does not exist (cli/cmd/try.go absent).

PROPOSED FIX
  Rename per the following table (no behavior change):
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go             -> cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go (already exists; merge or verify)
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/land.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go         -> cli/internal/agent/try/coordinator.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go       -> cli/internal/agent/try/review.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/review_triage.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go       -> cli/internal/agent/try/disposition.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go         -> cli/internal/agent/work/drain.go
    cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go         -> cli/internal/agent/try/mirror.go

  Rename identifiers: ExecuteBeadWorker -> work.Worker, ExecuteBeadWorker.Run -> work.Worker.Drain, ExecuteBead -> try.Attempt (FEAT-006 + FEAT-010).

  Add cli/cmd/try.go with cobra command "ddx try <bead-id>" invoking try.Attempt directly (FEAT-010 surface).

  Update FEAT-006 and FEAT-010 spec docs to reflect new package paths. Keep ExecuteBead* shims as typedefs; deletion is C14.

NON-SCOPE
  - Behavior changes to the drain loop or attempt logic.
  - Deletion of ExecuteBead* typedefs (C14).
  - ddx-dc157075 file-path reference migration if that bead has not yet landed.

INTERSECTIONS
- P2: file and identifier names should reflect the subsystem boundary and keep responsibilities separated.
- P5: ddx try as a first-class CLI surface makes the worker/attempt state easier for operators to see.
- P7: the refactor aligns names and prompt surface with the bead-as-prompt contract.

acceptance:
1. Worker prompt text no longer asks for `status: needs_investigation`; it asks only for valid lifecycle outcomes proposed/open/blocked/closed/cancelled as appropriate.
2. The live no-changes parser rejects `status: needs_investigation` with a clear error or no-op outcome that points to the lifecycle migrator; it does not convert it as runtime compatibility.
3. NoChangesLabelNeedsInvestigation and needs_human worker-exclusion behavior are removed from live code; any remaining names are confined to migration-only fixtures.
4. TestNoChangesAutonomousInvestigationRemainsOpen proves smart-runnable investigation outcomes remain status=open and record smart-retry metadata.
5. TestNoChangesOperatorRequiredBecomesProposed proves genuine operator-only outcomes transition to status=proposed.
6. TestNoChangesRejectsLegacyNeedsInvestigationStatus proves live parser output does not add active needs_human/triage labels.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestNoChanges|TestPostAttemptTriage|TestExecuteBeadNoChangesVerify' -count=1` passes.
8. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...` passes.
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The no-changes contract and parser still accept a pseudo-status needs_investigation, then park the bead via labels and cooldown metadata. That bypasses the upstream status set and prevents ddx work from rerunning investigation-quality work with a smart agent when autonomous progress is possible.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:16-23 defines NoChangesKindNeedsInvestigation.
- cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:25-35 defines NoChangesLabelNeedsInvestigation and related events/extra keys.
- cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:60-64 parses `status: needs_investigation`.
- cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:136-140 defines NoChangesActionNeedsHumanNoCooldown.
- cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:203-215 maps needs-investigation to a needs-human action and label.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1786-1796 instructs workers to output `status: needs_investigation` for ambiguous no-change cases.

PROPOSED FIX
Replace the no-changes pseudo-status contract with status-owned outcomes. If the bead is already satisfied, close it. If no change happened but autonomous smart-agent work remains possible, leave status=open with explanatory no-changes metadata and route the next retry with smart power. If operator decision is required, transition to status=proposed with explicit reason/suggested action metadata. Reserve status=blocked only for external recheckable blockers. `status: needs_investigation` is not accepted in new live no-changes output; stored historical values are handled by the one-way lifecycle migrator.

NON-SCOPE
Do not rewrite preclaim intake, terminal review, GraphQL, or frontend surfaces in this bead. Those have separate integration beads.

DEPS
Depends on store/query semantics because no-changes outcomes must be visible in the new worker-ready/operator-attention buckets.
title: agent: map no-changes outcomes to status-owned lifecycle states

acceptance:
1. ddx work focus is read-only and exits successfully on an empty queue.
2. Human-readable output has separate sections for Requires human, Blocked/planning, Worker-ready summary, Worker recommendation, and Unknowns; ready implementation beads are not listed as primary intervention work by default.
3. --json returns stable keys human_required, blocked_or_planning, ready_summary, worker_recommendation, and unknowns.
4. Worker recommendation logic is conservative: it can suggest another worker when ready depth is high and active/in-progress capacity is observable, and it reports unknown capacity instead of inventing a blocker when signals are absent.
5. TestWorkFocusReportsInterventionQueue covers needs_human/review-blocked and blocked/planning rows.
6. TestWorkFocusOmitsWorkerReadyBeadsByDefault covers the ready-versus-intervention distinction.
7. TestWorkFocusJSONIncludesUnknownHazards covers JSON unknown capacity/resource signals.
8. TestWorkFocusRecommendsWorkerForDeepReadyQueue covers the conservative worker-start suggestion.
9. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run "TestWorkFocusReportsInterventionQueue|TestWorkFocusOmitsWorkerReadyBeadsByDefault|TestWorkFocusJSONIncludesUnknownHazards|TestWorkFocusRecommendsWorkerForDeepReadyQueue" -count=1 passes.
10. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... passes.
11. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Interactive queue stewardship needs a higher-level surface than ddx bead ready. Ready beads are worker-actionable and should usually be left to ddx work; operators need a command that exposes work ddx ready/work will not pick up plus a conservative signal when the ready queue is deep enough to justify starting another worker.

ROOT CAUSE
.agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:7-16 lists ready, blocked, status, and list as queue inspection commands but has no human-intervention view. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:18-23 answers "What should I work on next?" with ddx bead ready. cli/cmd/work_plan.go:119-160 prints execution plan rows and skipped epics, but not a first-class intervention queue or worker-capacity recommendation.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a read-only command surface, proposed as ddx work focus, that summarizes human-required intervention separately from worker-ready work. It should show needs_human/review-blocked beads, blocked beads that require planning or dependency decisions, malformed or underspecified beads if lifecycle signals are available, ready-depth summary, active-worker/in-progress signals when observable, and unknown capacity hazards when not observable. It may recommend starting another worker only from observable queue depth/capacity signals.

NON-SCOPE
- Automatically starting workers.
- Closing, updating, or splitting beads.
- Replacing ddx work plan or ddx bead ready.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-09d7d787. Dependencies: ddx-377ebf92.
title: work focus: expose human-required queue intervention

description:
PROBLEM
The public DDx execution vocabulary is supposed to be `ddx run`, `ddx try`, and `ddx work`, but large parts of the codebase still use legacy `execute-bead` / `execute-loop` names in public help, comments, server worker kinds, GraphQL models, tests, docs, tracker metadata helpers, and internal symbols. Operators now see mixed terms and developers keep adding new code under the old names.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1485-1563 still defines the legacy `ddx agent execute-loop` cobra surface as if it were primary: help says "execute-loop is the primary queue-driven execution surface", examples use `ddx agent execute-loop`, and deprecation text still names execute-loop rather than pointing to `ddx work`.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1590-1940 keeps the main work implementation named `runAgentExecuteLoopImpl`, `writeExecuteLoopResult`, and `ExecuteBeadLoopResult`, so new work-path changes naturally copy the old terminology.
- cli/cmd/try.go:213-333 implements the new `ddx try` command by constructing `ExecuteBeadExecutor`, `ExecuteBeadRuntime`, `ExecuteBeadWorker`, and `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`, so the new public command still routes through legacy names.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/models.go:2233-2281 and cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/workers.spec.ts:343-397 expose worker kind/comment strings as `execute-loop` / `execute-bead`, which leaks legacy naming through API and UI fixtures.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:215-262 still describes attempt results and retry metadata as `execute-bead` / `execute-loop-*`, even while FEAT-010 and ADR-023 name the lifecycle `try` / `work`.
- Existing related beads are partially stale or narrower than this repo-wide cleanup: ddx-387a0178 owns C13 file/identifier renames and still says `ddx try` does not exist, while ddx-29058e2a / ddx-1a9cc01f still propose `ExecuteLoopSpec` naming for a work-side spec.

WHY THE NAMES DIVERGED
The codebase grew from the historical `ddx agent execute-bead` and `ddx agent execute-loop` commands. FEAT-010 introduced the clearer layered vocabulary (`run` = invocation atom, `try` = one bead attempt, `work` = queue drain), and `ddx try` / `ddx work` now exist as the intended operator surface. The implementation retained old symbols and compatibility aliases, so old names persisted as the easiest local pattern to copy.

PROPOSED FIX
- Perform a repo-wide terminology audit using `rg -n "execute-loop|ExecuteLoop|execute loop|execute-bead|ExecuteBead|execute bead"` and classify each hit as: public/user-facing rename, internal compatibility alias to retain, durable metadata key to retain with documentation, historical docs intentionally retained, or generated fixture/test data.
- Update public/user-facing CLI help, comments, API/GraphQL descriptions, server/frontend labels, tests, and docs to prefer `work` and `try`.
- Rename non-compatibility internal types/functions introduced by the FEAT-010 path from ExecuteLoop/ExecuteBead vocabulary to Work/Try vocabulary. Coordinate with ddx-387a0178 C13; if C13 already performs a rename, fold this cleanup into or after it rather than duplicating work.
- For compatibility aliases and durable data keys that must remain (`ddx agent execute-loop`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, `execute-loop-*` Extra keys, historical event kinds), add concise comments documenting them as legacy/back-compat names and point readers at `ddx work` / `ddx try`.
- Re-scope or update related open beads whose titles/descriptions still establish new `ExecuteLoop*` names (notably ddx-29058e2a and ddx-1a9cc01f) so future work does not add fresh legacy terminology.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove the legacy `ddx agent execute-loop` or `ddx agent execute-bead` command aliases in this bead; they are compatibility surfaces unless a governing doc explicitly authorizes removal.
- Do not rename durable tracker keys or historical event kinds if doing so would break existing `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, `.ddx/executions`, worker records, or archive reads. Document retained keys instead.
- Do not change work/try behavior, queue ordering, claim semantics, routing policy, or Fizeau integration.
- Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`; update any related bead metadata only with `ddx bead update` / `ddx bead dep`.

INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-387a0178: structural C13 file/identifier rename; this bead depends on it or should be folded into it if it is re-authored.
- ddx-29058e2a and ddx-1a9cc01f: ExecuteLoopSpec naming conflicts with the intended WorkSpec/WorkRequest vocabulary; update or supersede as part of this cleanup.
- ddx-eccc6efb: server-spawn migration may delete some `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` paths; if it lands first, shrink this bead to remaining public/API/doc names.

title: agent: replace legacy execute terminology with work/try nomenclature

acceptance:
1. FEAT-001 removes the `ddx agent` structural mount and defines only `ddx run`, `ddx try`, `ddx work`, and any Fizeau diagnostics namespace that remains, with no aliases.
2. FEAT-006 is renamed or rewritten as the DDx consumer spec for Fizeau, and no longer presents `agentlib.DdxAgent` / `ddx-agent` as the desired contract name.
3. FEAT-010 names DDx-owned packages/roles using task execution vocabulary (`run`, `try`, `work`, `taskexec`, `fizeauadapter`) rather than `internal/agent`.
4. ADR-024 says escalation changes only MinPower/MaxPower/request facts and Fizeau chooses the concrete route.
5. TP-020 is replaced or superseded with a DDx boundary test plan proving DDx does not normalize, fuzzy-match, or route models.
6. docs/migrations/routing-config.md documents removal of `agent.*` defaults and the split between Fizeau routing config and DDx execution config.
7. DDx skill references explain explicit passthrough and Fizeau-owned model matching using examples such as `--model qwen36`, while stating DDx passes the raw string unchanged.
8. `rg -n "ddx agent|ddx-agent|agentlib.DdxAgent|Agent Service|cli/internal/agent" docs .agents/skills .claude/skills skills` returns only audited historical or literal-external-agent hits documented in the change.
9. `ddx doc validate` passes or any unrelated pre-existing failures are listed in the bead notes.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Implementation agents need durable specs before removing `ddx agent`, renaming DDx internals, or consuming Fizeau contract changes. Today the specs still mix the old `agent` vocabulary with the desired DDx/Fizeau boundary, so agents can reasonably follow stale instructions.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
FEAT-001 still describes an `Agent Passthrough` structural mount for `ddx agent` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-001-cli.md:70-84. FEAT-006 still describes the upstream invocation boundary as `agentlib.DdxAgent` and `DDx Agent Service` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md:1-23. FEAT-010 still says layer 1 consumes upstream `ddx-agent` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:16-19. TP-020 remains a historical DDx-side routing test plan even though Fizeau owns routing. docs/migrations/routing-config.md:1-13 removes some old routing keys but does not yet document the full `agent.*` removal / Fizeau-vs-execution config split.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the specs before implementation. Required docs: FEAT-001, FEAT-006 or its renamed replacement, FEAT-010, ADR-024, TP-020, docs/migrations/routing-config.md, docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md, and DDx skill references. The specs must state: DDx owns task execution (`run`, `try`, `work`); Fizeau owns routing, provider/model discovery, native transcript/session rendering, and fuzzy model constraint resolution; `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are explicit operator passthrough only; DDx passes raw strings unchanged; `ddx agent` is removed without deprecation; and `agent` is allowed only for actual external AI agent concepts.

NON-SCOPE
No production code removal in this bead. No Fizeau implementation work. No compatibility alias design. No generated CLI docs rebuild unless the docs generator is part of the spec-doc workflow.

UPSTREAM REFERENCES
- fizeau-61668fd3 for native harness `agent` -> `fiz` contract identity
- fizeau-400b57fe for Fizeau-owned model alias/fuzzy matching

PARENT AND DEPENDENCIES
Parent: ddx-f088a3fd. Dependencies: none; this bead comes first.

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go implements NewPostAttemptTriageHook or an equivalently named constructor returning func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error), with explicit injected AgentRunner/service/config inputs and no dependency on cli/cmd.
2. TestTriageHook_UsesRunnerLibrary asserts the hook reaches an injected AgentRunner.Run path and a static grep/AST assertion proves triage_hook.go does not import os/exec or shell out to ddx agent run.
3. TestTriageHook_PromptIncludesOutcomeAndLogExcerpt verifies the prompt starts with MODE: triage, names bead-lifecycle, includes bead content, ExecuteBeadReport JSON, and a bounded session-log excerpt when available.
4. TestLoop_TriageHook_FiresPostOutcome proves PostAttemptTriageHook runs after report status/detail are populated and before no-progress cooldown is applied.
5. TestTriageHook_RecordsButDoesNotMutateOutcome proves valid triage classification populates OutcomeReason and/or a triage event without changing ExecuteBeadReport.Status.
6. TestTriageHook_HookError_FallsThroughToLegacyCooldown proves hook error leaves OutcomeReason empty and preserves existing cooldown behavior.
7. The triage prompt requests structured JSON fields classification, recommended_action, rationale, suggested_amendments, and suggested_followup_beads.
8. WIRED-IN: git grep PostAttemptTriageHook cli/internal/agent shows the execute-loop invocation site, not just the runtime field declaration.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  ddx try needs post-attempt triage after the loop has adjudicated an attempt outcome and before cooldown decisions, so transient failures can be classified without mutating the underlying status. Today the loop records status/detail/rationale but has no triage hook invocation path that can populate OutcomeReason.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:940-1090 contains the outcome adjudication and cooldown branch chain. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:976 and 1084 call shouldSuppressNoProgress before applying no-progress cooldown. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1099 appends the report to the loop result after cooldown handling. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:30 now exposes ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PostAttemptTriageHook and cli/internal/agent/quality_hooks.go defines TriageResult, but no cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go exists and the loop never calls the hook.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go with a constructor, e.g. NewPostAttemptTriageHook(projectRoot string, store BeadReader, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, svc agentlib.FizeauService, runner AgentRunner, sessionLogReader SessionLogExcerptReader) func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error). If package boundaries require a different name, preserve the explicit injected inputs.
  - The hook builds a prompt whose first line is MODE: triage, names bead-lifecycle, includes the bead body, ExecuteBeadReport as JSON, the most relevant recent bead events, and a bounded session-log excerpt when report.SessionID is non-empty and a log can be found under the project agent log dir.
  - Invoke dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx, projectRoot, svc, runner, rcfg, AgentRunRuntime{Prompt: prompt, WorkDir: projectRoot, PromptSource: "bead-lifecycle-triage"}) or an equivalent package-local wrapper that still uses AgentRunner/ResolvedConfig and not os/exec.
  - In execute_bead_loop.go, call runtime.PostAttemptTriageHook after report.Status/Detail/Rationale/BaseRev/ResultRev are final for the attempt and before any shouldSuppressNoProgress or SetExecutionCooldown branch. A valid classification sets report.OutcomeReason = TriageResult.Classification and records triage details in the execute-bead event body or a dedicated bead-quality.triage event.
  - On hook failure, fail open: leave OutcomeReason empty and keep legacy cooldown behavior.

NON-SCOPE
  - Defining OutcomeReason and TriageResult; closed in ddx-c8dc1146.
  - CLI wiring for ddx try; covered by sibling/parent wiring work.
  - GraphQL exposure of OutcomeReason.
  - Automatically creating SuggestedFollowupBeads; record/surface only unless a later bead opts in.

PARENT
  ddx-e1a576a7.

DEPS
  No deps. ddx-c8dc1146 is closed and the OutcomeReason/TriageResult contract exists.

description: Add a CI-enforced static lint over GraphQL mutation resolvers in cli/internal/server/graphql that flags any resolver missing the requireTrusted gate (the route-level wrapper) or omitting CSRF/Origin checks for prompt-driven endpoints. Lint runs as a Go test (go test ./cli/internal/server/graphql -run TestADRMutationConformance) so it is wired into the existing CI test suite without new tooling. Detection should walk the schema.graphql Mutation type and confirm: (a) the /graphql HTTP route is wrapped in requireTrusted (verified by reading server.go); (b) each resolver function for a mutation that takes operator/prompt-style input invokes the CSRF/Origin check helper (e.g. validateCSRF / Origin header check). Allowlist exceptions explicitly. See /tmp/story-15-final.md §Tests 'ADR conformance test' and §Risks 'Multi-node authorization gap'. Parent: ddx-256af8b5.
title: S15-7a: ADR-conformance lint for mutation resolvers (requireTrusted + CSRF/Origin)

acceptance:
1. AttemptCycleCoordinator and the pass/check/review/repair/land interfaces (or equivalent names documented in code comments) exist in cli/internal/agent.
2. ExecuteBeadWithConfig or an extracted ImplementationPass can return a live worktree candidate result without owning terminal cleanup.
3. TestAttemptCycleCoordinator_SinglePassSuccessStillLands verifies the coordinator can preserve current success behavior without review/repair enabled.
4. TestAttemptCycleCoordinator_NoChangesBehaviorUnchanged verifies no_changes adjudication remains unchanged.
5. TestAttemptCycleCoordinator_LiveWorktreeProtected verifies active cycle metadata prevents cleanup from deleting the worktree mid-cycle.

description:
PROBLEM
The current execute-bead implementation conflates worktree lifetime, implementation dispatch, artifact writing, landing, and post-merge review across ExecuteBeadWithConfig and the execute loop. Candidate-cycle review/repair needs a coordinator that owns the full live-worktree lifecycle before final land.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:668-680 documents ExecuteBeadWithConfig as the thin worker and says merge/update-ref/gate/preserve are parent responsibilities.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:765-767 removes the worktree via defer inside ExecuteBeadWithConfig, preventing same-worktree review/repair after the function returns.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:888-900 calls agenttry.Attempt around the executor, then handles review later in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1086-1104.
- cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:146-299 owns generic attempt outcome classification, but not candidate-cycle review/repair.

PROPOSED FIX
- Introduce narrow interfaces for AttemptCycleCoordinator, ImplementationPass, CandidateRefStore, CandidateCheckRunner, CandidateReviewer, RepairPass, and CandidateLander or equivalent names.
- Split the implementation pass from terminal worktree cleanup: implementation produces a candidate result inside a live worktree; the coordinator owns cleanup after terminal disposition.
- Preserve existing no_changes, no_evidence, and execution_failed behavior while creating seams for candidate checks, review, repair, and final land.
- Add basic cleanup liveness protection for active candidate-cycle worktrees before the deferred removal is moved.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not yet run pre-land review.
- Do not yet implement repair classification or repair loops.
- Do not enable two-slot review.

DEPS
Depends on the spec bead for vocabulary and on the read-only reviewer profile only for interface shape; this bead may define the coordinator before reviewer execution is moved.

5. TestDocumentsResolver_Cache_HitOnSameRevision verifies warm request does not rebuild.
6. TestDocumentsResolver_Cache_MissOnRevisionChange verifies cache miss triggers rebuild.
7. TestDocumentsResolver_Search_FiltersFromCache verifies search uses cached data.

9. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... green.
10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The server documents resolver rebuilds the full artifact graph on every page request, even when the underlying graph has not changed. On a 2000-artifact corpus, this produces visible latency on every load and makes concurrent requests redundantly expensive.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/graphql_resolver.go (or equivalent resolver file): the documents resolver calls the full graph-build pipeline on every GraphQL request without checking if the graph revision has changed since the last build.
- No normalized artifact cache (by graph revision) exists — every request starts cold.
- The search path in the documents resolver also rebuilds the full artifact list on every search call.
- ddx-5681cc57 (2k fixture) is a dep: cache design must be validated against the 2k scale fixture.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a cache keyed by graph revision (mtime-based, as recommended in the perf epic): store normalized artifact list (lowercased title/path/id/mediaType) keyed by revision string.
- Add search to documents resolver: filter the cached normalized list by search query.
- Add invalidation on file change: detect mtime changes and invalidate cache entry.
- Ensure concurrent requests don't double-build (single-flight pattern or sync.Once per revision).

NON-SCOPE
- Caching for resolvers other than documents.
- CI performance gate (that is a later bead).
title: perf: cache graph/artifact metadata + add search to documents resolver + invalidation

title: checks: backfill production-reachability — internal/server/graphql (5 unreached)

acceptance:
1. Evidence tab appears in runs/[runId] tabbed page alongside Overview/Prompt/Response/Tools/Session.
2. Tab lists every file in run's bundle with path + size + mimeType.
3. Whitelisted text files < 64KB render inline as raw text.
4. Non-whitelisted or > 64KB files show download link only.
5. Evidence tab lazy-loads on tab activation (not on page load).
6. test('evidence tab: whitelisted small file inlines', ...) Playwright e2e verifies inline rendering.
7. test('evidence tab: large file download-only', ...) Playwright e2e verifies download link for > 64KB.
8. test('evidence tab: non-whitelisted extension download-only', ...) Playwright e2e verifies .go / .wasm etc. show download only.
9. test('evidence tab: missing path 404', ...) verifies bundleFile(path) returns 404 for out-of-bundle paths.
10. p95 dataset (30 evidence files) renders without crash.
11. bun run test:e2e green.
12. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The runs/[runId] page (built in S16.1) has no Evidence tab. Users cannot browse or download execution evidence files (manifest.json, result.json, stderr logs, etc.) from the run-detail view. Whitelisting logic for safe inline rendering must be enforced to avoid serving binary or executable content inline.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/runs/[runId]/+page.svelte (added by ddx-f04faa86) has no Evidence tab.
- No bundleFiles[] or bundleFile(path) resolver exists yet for the run-detail page (that resolver is ddx-6d132f62, a dep).
- No whitelist enforcement is implemented; any file could be rendered inline without size or extension checks.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add Evidence tab to runs/[runId]/+page.svelte (as a sibling tab to Overview/Prompt/Response/Tools/Session).
- List every file in run's bundle with: path, size, mimeType.
- Whitelist: *.txt, *.md, manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json, files < 64KB — render inline as raw text.
- Non-whitelisted or > 64KB: download link only. No Markdown rendering, no syntax highlighting, no hex preview.
- Evidence tab lazy-loads on activation.
- Large files (> 64KB): show size + download button only.
- Bundle-root confinement enforced server-side on bundleFile(path) resolver.

NON-SCOPE
- Markdown rendering (deliberate: whitelist is raw-text only).
- Syntax highlighting.
- Hex preview of binary files.
title: Story 16.2: Evidence tab — bundle-file list + download + whitelist inline view

acceptance:
1. `TestTryDocsChanged_AttachesProseEvidence` covers docs-changing attempts.
2. `TestTryNoDocsChanged_SkipsProseCheck` covers non-doc attempts.
3. `TestTryProseFindings_AdvisoryByDefault` proves findings do not block closure.
4. `TestTryProseFindings_FedBackBeforeFinalization` covers editable feedback timing.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
DDx should run prose checks during normal docs-changing attempts without users reminding the agent. Findings need to be captured as evidence and fed back while the attempt is still editable.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:264-283 requires `ddx try` / `ddx work` attempts that change Markdown under `docs/` to run `ddx doc prose --changed`, attach evidence, and keep prose advisory. No workflow hook currently implements that contract.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Workflow-hook policy is high-judgment because it touches execution finalization and evidence. A weak implementation could block correct work on advisory prose findings or run too late for agents to repair obvious prose issues.

PROPOSED FIX
- Detect docs Markdown changes in `ddx try` / `ddx work` attempts.
- Run `ddx doc prose --changed` before finalization when docs changed.
- Attach prose-check evidence to the attempt.
- Feed findings back to the agent before final response when editable.
- Keep prose findings advisory by default and separate from correctness review.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not make prose checks mandatory blockers.
- Do not run full-doc cleanup.
- Do not alter Fizeau routing.

DEPS
Depends on the Vale-backed command and the upgraded writing skill.
title: try: attach prose-check evidence for docs-changing attempts

description:
PROBLEM
Cleanup must never remove published DDx evidence bundles, but the current command tests only prove a subset of that contract. cli/cmd/cleanup_test.go:129-159 proves stale temp dirs are removed while .ddx/executions and .ddx/runs examples survive, yet the acceptance requires explicit coverage for complete .ddx/executions bundles, complete .ddx/runs bundles, and refs/ddx/iterations/* remaining intact after cleanup.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/cmd/cleanup.go:64-134 delegates to the cleanup manager and prints the preservation summary, but there is no end-to-end test that seeds all three published-evidence forms and verifies cleanup leaves them untouched.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend cli/cmd/cleanup_test.go or add a sibling end-to-end test that seeds complete .ddx/executions/<attempt-id> and .ddx/runs/<run-id> bundles plus refs/ddx/iterations/* under a temp git project.
- Run ddx cleanup --apply and assert the stale DDx temp dirs are removed while the published evidence remains on disk / in git refs.
- Verify the output or JSON report includes the preservation summary for the complete evidence bundles.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not require live provider/model execution.
- Do not remove or rewrite the published evidence artifacts.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestCleanupEndToEnd_PreservesPublishedEvidence exists and seeds complete .ddx/executions, .ddx/runs, and refs/ddx/iterations/* evidence.
2. Cleanup leaves every published evidence artifact intact while still removing stale DDx temp resources.
3. The cleanup output reports the preserved evidence bundles.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestCleanupEndToEnd_PreservesPublishedEvidence" -count=1 passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: test: preserve published execution evidence during cleanup end-to-end

acceptance:
1. ArtifactTypePanel.svelte exists at cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/ArtifactTypePanel.svelte.
2. Artifact detail view shows ArtifactTypePanel for artifacts with matching type definitions from typeDefinitions resolver.
3. Tabbed layout: Reference Prompt / Template / Examples tabs.
4. Selector dropdown appears on prefix collision; ?typeDef= URL round-trip preserves selected type on refresh.
5. ArtifactTypePanel imports prefixOf from artifacts/grouping.ts.
6. vitest tests cover: single type definition, multi-match collision selector, ?typeDef= URL state.
7. bun run test green (vitest).
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
No frontend component renders the ArtifactType definitions for an artifact. When an artifact matches a type definition (by prefix), the operator has no UI surface to view the associated template, prompt, and examples. The detail-view of any artifact is missing the type-aware panel.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/ contains artifact detail pages but no ArtifactTypePanel.svelte component.
- The typeDefinitions resolver (ddx-9ca4b5bf, a dep) provides the data, but nothing in the frontend consumes it.
- On prefix collision (multiple type definitions matching the same prefix), no selector dropdown or ?typeDef= URL state exists.
- prefixOf utility at cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/artifacts/grouping.ts (from AC #10 of parent) is the expected import source.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/ArtifactTypePanel.svelte:
  - Tabbed layout: Reference Prompt | Template | Examples tabs (labels neutral, not 'Generate from').
  - On prefix collision: selector dropdown with ?typeDef= URL round-trip.
  - Imports prefixOf from artifacts/grouping.ts (single source of truth).
- Integrate ArtifactTypePanel into the artifact detail-view page.
- Add vitest unit tests for the component.

NON-SCOPE
- The typeDefinitions GraphQL resolver (that's ddx-9ca4b5bf, a dep).
- Generating artifacts from a prompt (not in this bead; labels must say 'Reference Prompt').

IN-SCOPE FILES

- review tests using prose fixtures
- docs updates for review integration

REQUIRED BEHAVIOR
- Add an opt-in review surface such as `ddx bead review <id> --prose` or the exact command selected by TD-036.
- Findings remain advisory by default.
- Review output must distinguish prose-quality findings from acceptance/correctness findings.
- The integration must reuse the same checker/rule/config path as `ddx doc prose`.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Making prose findings globally blocking.
- Changing execute-loop selection or closure semantics based only on prose findings.
