description:
PROBLEM
The parent Fizeau-boundary bead originally referenced the old TP-020 routing planner test plan. That filename has since moved to docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-020-fizeau-boundary-and-pass-through.md, but the plan still needs a focused verification pass so it is clearly a DDx boundary plan rather than a DDx-side routing planner plan.

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-020-fizeau-boundary-and-pass-through.md:8-14 says the plan supersedes DDx-side routing planner coverage and now tests raw passthrough/no local routing guarantees.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-020-fizeau-boundary-and-pass-through.md:24-49 lists named tests for raw constraint passthrough, no DDx-side normalization, no DDx-side routing, and config boundary behavior.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-020-fizeau-boundary-and-pass-through.md:58-64 defines exit criteria that DDx forwards strings unchanged and leaves Fizeau routing to Fizeau-owned tests.

PROPOSED FIX
- Audit TP-020 so it no longer asks DDx to test model catalog ranking, fuzzy matching, provider fallback, or concrete route selection.
- Ensure every listed test name maps to a DDx boundary invariant: parse/forward unchanged, do not normalize locally, do not route locally, and reject old DDx routing defaults.
- Update references to the retired TP-020 filename if any nearby docs still point at the old agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution path.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the tests in this bead; this is the plan-contract bead.
- Do not edit Fizeau-owned routing tests.
- Do not update generated website command pages.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-424fdac4. No deps; this is one decomposition child of the broad Fizeau-boundary ratification bead.

description:
PROBLEM
The `ddx work` stdout sequence is assembled by several call sites instead of one renderer, so timestamp shape, bead scoping, route/model display, and phase wording cannot be enforced consistently.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:722-737 writes lifecycle/header lines directly with `fmt.Fprintf`.
- cli/internal/agent/session_log_format.go:135-144 formats service progress rows without timestamp, phase, or scoped bead awareness.
- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:185-195 writes route/progress entries directly to the same writer.

PROPOSED FIX
Introduce a reusable work stdout renderer for human `ddx work` output. It should own line timestamping (`HH:MM:SS`), current-bead scoping, phase labels, route/model formatting, and header/title formatting. Keep it small and local to agent/work stdout behavior so JSONL/event persistence remains untouched.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/work_log_renderer.go
- cli/internal/agent/work_log_renderer_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/session_log_format.go only for narrow helper integration if needed

NON-SCOPE
- Do not rewrite all logging in the repository.
- Do not alter structured event timestamps or storage schemas.
- Do not change model routing decisions.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-eeced112. Depends on the stdout spec bead.

description:
PROBLEM
The triage policy layer still exposes needs_human as a terminal action. That action name leaks into config, retry ladders, execution events, and review routing, so agent code can keep producing old lifecycle labels even after status=proposed is introduced.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/triage/triage.go:31-43 defines ActionNeedsHuman.
- cli/internal/triage/triage.go:53-77 ends default ladders in needs_human.
- cli/internal/triage/triage.go:86-100 falls unknown modes back to needs_human.
- cli/internal/triage/triage.go:103-115 parses needs_human from config/text.
- cli/internal/config/types.go:76-104 resolves triage policy actions from config, including existing needs_human fixtures.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go:9-13 defines TriageNeedsHumanLabel.

PROPOSED FIX
Replace needs_human with a canonical proposed/operator-required action whose lifecycle effect is status=proposed and no active needs_human label. Old needs_human config/action values are not normal compatibility aliases; after lifecycle migration they should fail validation with a clear upgrade/migration error. Default ladders must bias toward autonomous repair/investigation before status=proposed where smart-agent work is possible.

NON-SCOPE
Do not wire preclaim intake or terminal review parking in this bead except where they compile against the renamed triage action. Dedicated agent integration beads own those paths.

DEPS
Depends on store/query semantics because triage outcomes must target the new lifecycle buckets.
title: agent: replace needs-human triage action with proposed lifecycle action

- Create a synthetic 2000-artifact fixture under cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/scale/.
- Measure: first paint cold/warm, scroll smoothness (frame rate under scroll), search latency (50ms goal from parent perf epic).

- Do NOT add a CI gate (baseline only — gate added in a later bead after caching lands).

NON-SCOPE
- Caching implementation (ddx-4a7eed8c).
- CI gate (deferred).

acceptance:
1. `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --dry-run` reports counts for needs_human labels, triage:needs-investigation labels, needs_investigation pseudo-statuses, rows that will become proposed/open/blocked/closed/cancelled, and whether the schema marker is missing.
2. `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --apply` performs the one-way conversion through the lifecycle transition API, removes active legacy labels, records explanatory events/Extra, writes the lifecycle schema marker, and auto-commits tracker changes.
3. The migration is idempotent: a second apply reports no changes and preserves the schema marker.
4. TestLifecycleMigrate_NeedsHumanLabelBecomesProposed covers open+needs_human rows.
5. TestLifecycleMigrate_NeedsInvestigationSmartRunnableRemainsOpen covers investigation labels that should be rerun by a smart agent rather than parked.
6. TestLifecycleMigrate_NeedsInvestigationOperatorRequiredBecomesProposed covers investigation labels that genuinely require operator input.
7. TestLifecycleMigrate_ExternalBlockerBecomesBlocked covers rare external recheckable blockers and proves dependency-only waits do not become blocked.
8. TestLifecycleMigrate_ApplyWritesSchemaMarkerAndIsIdempotent verifies apply+second apply behavior.
9. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/... -run 'TestLifecycleMigrate|TestBeadMigrateLifecycle|TestReconcile' -count=1` passes.
10. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/...` passes.
11. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Existing beads and lifecycle metadata can carry needs_human, triage:needs-investigation, no_changes_needs_investigation, cooldowns, and lifecycle_reconciled labels. Under the new state machine those labels cannot remain active runtime controls, and DDx startup must refuse to run until old queues are migrated.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/reconcile.go:8-27 defines lifecycle labels and extra keys including LabelNeedsInvestigation and LabelNeedsHuman.
- cli/internal/bead/reconcile.go:156-223 adds or clears needs-investigation labels/cooldown metadata during lifecycle reconciliation.
- cli/internal/bead/reconcile.go:281-284 includes triage labels in currentLifecycleFields.
- cli/internal/bead/types.go:206-216 defines BlockerKindNeedsInvestigation and BlockerKindNeedsHuman as first-class blocker kinds.
- cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go:10-108 and cli/internal/bead/migrate.go:23-40 implement archive/event/backend migration but no one-way lifecycle migration.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a one-way lifecycle migration command/path, for example `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --dry-run|--apply`, that converts legacy lifecycle labels/metadata into status-owned state. open+needs_human and equivalent human-required outcomes become status=proposed with reason/suggested-action metadata. triage:needs-investigation is removed as an active lane; each row becomes status=proposed when operator input is required, remains status=open when autonomous smart-agent work should proceed, becomes status=blocked only for external recheckable blockers, or terminal status where evidence already proves closed/cancelled. After apply, record a lifecycle schema marker used by the startup gate. This is not backwards compatibility: old labels are migration input only.

NON-SCOPE
Do not preserve needs_human or triage:needs-investigation as worker-exclusion labels. Do not auto-apply migration at startup. Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` outside the migration command.

DEPS
Depends on store/query wiring and transition write API so migration output validates against the new queues.
title: bead: migrate legacy lifecycle labels to status-owned states

title: artifacts: e2e for grouping + Story 6 composition check

acceptance:
1. TD-031 states that DDx startup refuses normal operation when the active bead queue contains legacy lifecycle labels or pseudo-statuses that have not been migrated.
2. FEAT-004 and FEAT-010 state that `ddx work`, server workers, GraphQL worker starts, REST/MCP worker starts, and queue status/readiness commands fail closed on unmigrated lifecycle queues.
3. The docs define the allowed bypass commands: help, version, doctor/read-only diagnostics that do not mutate lifecycle, and `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --apply` / dry-run.
4. The docs explicitly say migration is one-way and git rollback is the recovery strategy; normal runtime does not maintain needs_human or triage:needs-investigation compatibility lanes.
5. The docs require startup error output to include counts of legacy labels/pseudo-statuses and the exact migration command to run.
6. `rg -n "one-way|startup|refuse|migrate --lifecycle|git rollback|needs_human|triage:needs-investigation" docs/helix docs/triage` finds the new migration-gate contract text and no active legacy queue-state wording.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/... ./internal/server/... -run 'TestLifecycle|TestBeadMigrate|Test.*Startup|Test.*Gate'` passes as a regression gate.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The lifecycle contract must specify how existing repositories move from label-owned lifecycle state to status-owned lifecycle state. Without a hard startup gate, DDx can start against an old queue, reinterpret stale needs_human / triage:needs-investigation labels under new code, and either run beads that were parked under old semantics or keep old queue lanes alive indefinitely.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/command_factory.go:110-120 constructs the root command without any project-local bead queue format preflight.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:67-92 chooses store backend/prefix but does not identify legacy lifecycle queue state before callers read or mutate beads.
- cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go:10-108 currently defines `ddx bead migrate` only for event externalization/archive and axon backend migration.
- cli/internal/bead/migrate.go:23-40 implements archive/event migration, not lifecycle status migration.
- The live tracker currently contains open beads with needs_human and triage:needs-investigation labels, so this is not a hypothetical upgrade path.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend the governing docs with a one-way lifecycle migration contract. DDx startup must detect unmigrated lifecycle queues and refuse to run normal commands until migration is applied. The allowed escape hatches are only diagnostic/help/version commands and the lifecycle migration command itself. The migration is one-way: old labels and pseudo-statuses are input to the migrator, not a compatibility surface for normal runtime. Because beads are git-tracked, rollback is git rollback rather than maintaining dual semantics.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement the startup gate or migrator in this docs bead. Do not preserve needs_human or triage:needs-investigation as active runtime compatibility aliases.

DEPS
Depends on the base lifecycle contract bead so this amendment lands after the six-status state machine spec edit.
title: docs: require one-way lifecycle migration and startup gate

acceptance:
1. All 15 children closed.
2. ExecuteBeadWorker.Run replaced by work.Worker.Drain (~30 lines).
3. 18 store-error-as-loop-exit smells eliminated; transient store error during outcome-commit logs and continues.
4. attempted/hookFailed maps deleted; pick derives exclusion from drain.result.
5. Routing preflight runs once at Drain startup, never per iteration.
6. ddx work mode semantics preserved: default is drain-until-no-ready-work; --once runs one ready bead then exits; --watch stays alive and sleeps for the idle interval when no execution-ready beads exist; --idle-interval without --watch is a usage error; no public compatibility alias remains for the removed polling flag.
7. ddx try <bead-id> CLI command exists.
8. File/identifier rename per package split complete; ExecuteBead* shims deleted.
9. JSONL event shapes (bead.result, execute-bead, review, routing, triage-decision, loop-error) unchanged - verified by C0 fixture baseline.
10. Server worker integration (cli/internal/server/) compiles and behaves identically except for the explicit once/drain/watch mode contract; tests green at every phase.
11. ddx-c6e3db02, ddx-b24e9630, ddx-cfedee8e, ddx-a8718bec, ddx-dc157075, ddx-f9acb86d, ddx-7dcbd40b, ddx-8b7ed960, and ddx-dc300be9 behaviors preserved per C5/C9/C10 absorption ACs.
12. FEAT-006 + FEAT-010 cross-refs updated to reflect new package paths and the mode + idle_interval worker-loop vocabulary.
description:
ExecuteBeadWorker.Run() is a 700-line function with 24 returns: 4 legitimate terminal exits, 1 legacy idle-loop exit, 1 routing-preflight-kills-worker exit, and 18 store-error escalations from the same handleOutcomeStoreError pattern repeated across outcome paths. The implicit state machine in the loop is real and coherent (Pick -> Gate -> Claim -> Execute -> Classify -> Reviewing -> Terminal) but written as conditional soup instead of explicit state.

## Three-package split aligned to FEAT-010

  cli/internal/agent/run/   - Layer 1: invocation atom (existing dispatch + runner)
  cli/internal/agent/try/   - Layer 2: bead attempt (worktree, prompt, single Execute, Land, conflict-recovery, post-merge review, triage)
  cli/internal/agent/work/  - Layer 3: queue drain (pick, claim, heartbeat, drain-loop control, stop conditions)

try.Attempt(beadID) returns sealed Outcome { Disposition (enum), Cooldown, ParkReason, RecordEvents }. Loop becomes approximately 30 lines: pick -> guard -> claim -> attemptWithHeartbeat -> applyOutcome. Single commitOutcome helper deletes the 18 store-error returns.

## Ordering: refactor-LAST per user decision

In-flight Group A beads (c6e3db02 rate-limit, b24e9630 no_changes, cfedee8e ladder wire, a8718bec cost-cap reviewer) land first against current code. dc157075 tactical worker-stay-alive lands first as operational bridge. The stdout/watch-mode beads (ddx-7d9e1e82, ddx-f9acb86d, ddx-7dcbd40b, ddx-8b7ed960, ddx-dc300be9) define the public work-loop contract this refactor must preserve. THIS refactor lands last and absorbs all intervening behavior into the new architecture. Per-child ACs explicitly call out which in-flight bead's behavior each child must preserve.

## Naming and public semantics

Package = try (not try_bead - Go idiom). Function = try.Attempt(beadID). Loop = work.Worker.Drain. CLI surface is ddx run / ddx try / ddx work. ddx work has three modes: once (one ready bead then exit), drain (default: run ready work until no execution-ready beads remain, then exit), and watch (stay alive indefinitely, sleep for idle interval when no execution-ready beads exist, and poll for newly arriving work). The removed polling flag is not preserved as a public compatibility alias; the durable vocabulary is mode plus idle_interval.

## Children

  C0  Pre-refactor JSONL fixture baseline for event-shape regression detection
  C1  try.Outcome sealed sum type + Disposition enum + ParkReason vocabulary enumeration
  C2  try.Attempt function shell (wraps existing executor; no behavior change)
  C3  Move review verdict handling into try.Attempt (180 lines from loop body)
  C4  Move conflict recovery into try.Attempt
  C5  Move no_changes adjudication, decomposition, push-failed, push-conflict (absorbs ddx-b24e9630, ddx-c6e3db02 behavior)
  C6  Single commitOutcome helper; deletes 18 store-error exit paths + handleOutcomeStoreError
  C7  Uniform Guard contract; delete attempted/hookFailed maps; pick() derives from drain.result
  C8  Routing preflight moves to Drain startup (runs once, not per iteration)
  C9  First-class StopCondition enum + cost-cap relocation + downstream-consumer audit for execution_failed:max-cost grep before relocation. Includes queue snapshot plumbing and human-review blocker pressure from ddx-7dcbd40b.
  C10 ddx work once/drain/watch modes + transient idle substate. Watch mode owns idle interval sleeps, per-idle queue status, and next-ready transition messaging from ddx-f9acb86d and ddx-8b7ed960.
  C11 Heartbeat extracted to withHeartbeat helper
  C12 Single phase-event emission (replaces 4 sites)
  C13 File and identifier rename per package split + ADD ddx try CLI command + handle dc157075 file-path migration if it landed first
  C14 Delete ExecuteBead* shims; verify all callers ported off (grep audit)

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Spec-first development · Executable specifications · Audit trail required · Context is king · Drift is debt · Inspect and adapt</principles>
<concerns>go-std | security-owasp | testing</concerns>
<practices>Use ddx bead commands for tracker changes · Preserve audit evidence · Keep prompts fit for execution and self-contained · Go implementation follow-up must use real/stubbed domain interfaces and go test plus lefthook gates</practices>
<governing>FEAT-010 / ADR-023 readiness policy currently permits safe intent-preserving updates before claim; this evolve pass specifies the stronger validated replacement rewrite contract so readiness can improve prompt fitness while preserving explicit commitments.</governing>
</context-digest>

PROBLEM
Safe rewrite policy is currently ambiguous. The append-only preservation guard prevents deletion of stale or noisy wording, but appending more text can make future bead prompts longer and more confusing. DDx needs specs that define validated replacement rewrites as a first-class readiness capability, including both expansion of underspecified beads and compression of noisy/stale beads.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:176-196 defines actionable_but_rewritten but does not say replacement rewrites are allowed or how they are validated.
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md:136-144 says readiness may update beads but does not distinguish append-only amendments from validated replacement rewrites.
- docs/triage/decomposition.md:17-26 describes safe rewrites but does not require prompt fitness or audit evidence for replaced text.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:392-407 names readiness transitions but does not model successful replacement rewrite as an open-to-open readiness mutation with evidence.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the governing specs to define safe rewrites as validated replacement rewrites: replacement bead text is allowed when a deterministic validator preserves intent, acceptance criteria, non-scope, durable evidence, and unresolved product choices; original text is retained in audit evidence, not duplicated into the prompt body. Replacement can expand an underspecified bead or compress a noisy one when prompt fitness requires it.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement code changes in this evolve pass.
- Do not relax acceptance criteria preservation.
- Do not permit rewrites that choose missing product semantics or delete unresolved conflicts.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: none. No deps.
notes:
<measure-results>
- FEAT-010 updated to define validated replacement safe rewrites, prompt fitness, durable-anchor validation, and audit evidence.
- ADR-023 updated to prefer fit-for-purpose replacement over append-only amendment when it improves the implementation prompt.
- docs/triage/decomposition.md updated with operational intake contract for replacement rewrites.
- TD-031 updated with open-to-open readiness rewrite transition and evidence requirements.
- Downstream beads created: ddx-a41e6fbb (validated replacement validator/prompt/evidence) and ddx-d5ae126c (park rejected rewrite attempts).
- Verification: lefthook run pre-commit passed for staged docs; commit ef22894a1.
</measure-results>
title: evolve: specify validated safe rewrites

description:
PROBLEM
DDx-created temporary execution resources do not have one shared cleanup owner. Inline attempt cleanup, orphan recovery, and liveness cleanup are spread across attempt code and ad hoc test helpers, which let stale /tmp/ddx-exec-wt directories and registered orphan worktrees accumulate until /tmp ran out of inodes.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:257-279 defines the DDx temp worktree root and naming convention, but there is no cleanup manager that owns that root.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:296-324 exposes WorktreeAdd/Remove/List/Prune primitives, but no production code uses them to reconcile stale registered vs unregistered DDx worktrees before attempts.
- cli/internal/agent/run_state.go:16-29 records one live attempt path, but it does not model cleanup ownership, preserved state, stale PID/session liveness, or partial setup directories.
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:202-266 defines the cleanup manager roots, ownership metadata, entry points, and conservative deletion rules now missing in code.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a focused cleanup manager under cli/internal/agent, e.g. execution_cleanup.go, with injectable filesystem/git/liveness probes for tests.
- Teach it to inspect the configured DDx temp worktree root, project git worktree list, run-state/liveness files, and legacy .ddx/executions/.ddx/runs evidence roots.
- Implement conservative deletion: remove only DDx-owned stale unregistered dirs, stale registered DDx worktrees, stale liveness files, and partial setup dirs; preserve published evidence, preserve refs, and active liveness.
- Return a structured cleanup summary with removed path counts, bytes/inodes where measurable, warnings, and blocked errors.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not wire the manager into ddx try/work loops here; that is covered by sibling beads.
- Do not add the operator CLI command here; that is covered by a sibling bead.
- Do not implement .ddx/executions to .ddx/runs migration.

The current cleanup tests in cli/cmd/cleanup_test.go:55-159 cover dry-run/apply and evidence preservation, and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_worktree_cleanup_test.go:48-65 only proves failed worktree setup removes a partial directory. cli/cmd/cleanup.go:53-134 runs the production cleanup manager against the filesystem, but there is no command-level end-to-end test that seeds real git-tracked stale worktrees plus stale unregistered dirs and then proves the cleanup pass removes only the stale DDx-owned resources.

acceptance:
1. `PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks()` (or equivalent code path) writes a `pre_merge_check_failed` event onto the bead with check name, exit code, and output excerpt.
2. The event surfaces in `ddx bead evidence list <bead-id>` and `ddx bead show <bead-id>` events list.
3. After preservation, result.json carries either an updated status or a new `orchestrator_status` field that distinguishes worker success from final landing failure.
4. Unit/integration test covers: worker returns task_succeeded → pre-merge check blocks → evidence event present, iteration ref written, bead unclaimed for retry.
description:
Context:
When `SubmitWithPreMergeChecks()` (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:131-178) calls `RunPreMergeChecks()` and the outcome is Blocked, `PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks()` writes the result under `refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<timestamp>-<basesha>` and skips the merge. The bead's evidence list never receives a `pre_merge_check_failed` event with the check name, output, or rationale.

This made a real incident on 2026-05-08 invisible: bead `fizeau-bd5193ca` (in /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau) was redriven 4 times across ~50 minutes / ~$9 of agent cost. Each attempt's result.json reported `outcome: task_succeeded, status: success` (worker classification, written before the gate) and the orchestrator silently preserved the result. `ddx bead evidence list fizeau-bd5193ca` only showed routing + cost events; `ddx bead show` had no hint that pre-merge checks had blocked anything. Operator had no way to discover the rejection without reading orchestrator source.

Reproduction:
1. Claim any bead via `ddx work` whose worker output trips a pre-merge check.
2. Observe result.json status=success, iteration ref preserved, bead remains open, evidence list devoid of pre-merge-check info.

Proposed fix:
- Emit a structured `pre_merge_check_failed` evidence event from `PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks()` (or its caller) with at least: check_id/name, exit_code, stdout/stderr tail, attempt_id, base_rev, result_rev. Append via the same evidence path used by routing/cost events so it shows up in `ddx bead evidence list` and `ddx bead show`.
- The orchestrator's terminal status (post worker + gate) should also be reflected back into result.json's status field — currently it stays `success` regardless of the gate outcome, which is misleading. Either rewrite result.json with the orchestrator's verdict, or add a separate `orchestrator_status` field.

description:
PROBLEM

Prefer Fizeau typed decoders such as agentlib.DecodeServiceEvent where decoding is necessary; remove local stringly-typed event constants and custom partial structs that duplicate Fizeau schema.

1. TestPreCloseReview_FixableGapSchedulesRepair verifies review_fixable_gap prevents close and the next implementation cycle receives repair_context_from_review_group.
2. TestPreCloseReview_TerminalClassificationsBlockOperator verifies spec_gap, missing_acceptance, too_large at depth cap, unsafe_or_out_of_scope, and exhausted review_error block with needs_human/review-manual-required.
3. TestPreCloseReview_ReviewErrorRetryBudgetPerSlot verifies retry accounting is keyed by result_rev and reviewer_index.
4. TestPreCloseReview_ReviewOutcomesDoNotConsumeNoProgress verifies review_error and operator-blocking review outcomes do not increment implementation no-progress counters.
5. TestExecutionTrace_RecordsReviewAndRepairCycles verifies cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, review_group_id, reviewer_index, and final decision are appended for both initial and repair cycles.

description:
PROBLEM
The pre-close review gate also needs bounded repair and terminal blocking policy. Without this slice, REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK can either loop forever or require manual handling even when a fixable implementation gap is clear.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:273 has OutcomeReason but no review classification taxonomy for repair cycles.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1576 suppresses no-progress for a narrow transient set and does not yet model review_error / operator-blocking classifications.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:780 classifies parse errors but does not tie review errors to per-result/per-slot retry caps in loop state.

PROPOSED FIX
- Classify non-approve review outcomes as review_fixable_gap, review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large, review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope, or review_error.
- For review_fixable_gap, schedule one bounded repair cycle carrying repair_context_from_review_group.
- For terminal classes and exhausted review_error retries, block with needs_human/review-manual-required and do not consume no-progress budget.
- Record append-only cycle trace for initial and repair cycles.

NON-SCOPE
- Initial review-group dispatch primitives.
- Basic pre-close close/prevent-close integration.

2. TestCheckGitRepoHealthFixUnsetsRedirectedCoreWorktree verifies ddx doctor --fix unsets the bad core.worktree value and subsequent doctor passes the Git Repo Health check.
3. TestPreCommitDDXValidateFailsOnCoreWorktreeRedirect or an equivalent lefthook validation test proves the ddx-validate hook fails before expensive hooks when core.worktree redirects the checkout.
4. Existing TestCheckGitRepoHealthDetectsStrayCoreWorktree and TestCheckGitRepoHealthCleanRepoStillPasses continue to pass.

description:
PROBLEM
The May 7 incident cleanup exposed a local Git configuration corruption: the primary checkout's .git/config had core.worktree pointing at /tmp/ddx-c2a66f45-wt. After that, git status and git commit run from /Users/erik/Projects/ddx evaluated a detached temp worktree instead of the real checkout. ddx doctor and pre-commit validation should fail fast on this class before hooks or commits operate on the wrong worktree.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/doctor.go:111 runs Git Repo Health, and cli/cmd/doctor.go:721-839 documents checks for core.bare, stray core.worktree, and extensions.worktreeConfig.
- lefthook.yml:291-302 runs ddx doctor in the ddx-validate pre-commit hook, but the hook did not protect the operator from a primary checkout whose local core.worktree redirected Git operations to /tmp/ddx-c2a66f45-wt.
- cli/internal/git/exec.go:20-48 scrubs inherited Git environment variables, but local .git/config core.worktree is persistent repo state, not an inherited environment variable.

PROPOSED FIX
- Harden ddx doctor so it detects a primary checkout .git/config core.worktree value that points anywhere other than the physical current checkout root, even when Git itself reports the redirected worktree as top-level.
- Add a pre-commit-facing fast check or ensure ddx-validate surfaces the doctor failure before other hooks run expensive tests.
- Keep ddx doctor --fix behavior for safely unsetting stray core.worktree.
- Add regression tests that create a repo with .git/config core.worktree pointing at a different temp worktree and prove doctor reports git_stray_core_worktree from the physical invocation root.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change execute-bead worktree creation policy.
- Do not change normal linked worktree support when extensions.worktreeConfig is enabled and the command is actually running inside that linked worktree.
- Do not remove existing Git environment scrubbing.

DEPS
Depends on the incident evidence containment bead so the repo is clean enough to validate hook behavior.
title: doctor: fail on primary checkout core.worktree redirects

description:
PROBLEM
  The pre-dispatch lint hook needs a production implementation that invokes the bead-lifecycle skill through the internal runner library, not by spawning ddx agent run as a subprocess.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go:35-63 centralizes agent invocation in dispatchViaResolvedConfig. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:168 defines the existing AgentRunner injection seam as Run(opts RunArgs) (*Result, error). cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1110-1112 routes execute-bead calls through dispatchViaResolvedConfig. cli/internal/agent/quality_hooks.go defines LintResult, but no cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go exists to compose a bead-lifecycle lint-mode prompt and parse its structured output.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go with a small constructor, e.g. NewPreDispatchLintHook(projectRoot string, store BeadReader, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, svc agentlib.FizeauService, runner AgentRunner) func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error). If a narrower signature fits existing package boundaries better, keep the same explicit inputs: projectRoot, bead lookup, sealed resolved config, optional service, optional AgentRunner.
  - The hook reads the bead by ID and builds a prompt whose first line is MODE: lint, names the bead-lifecycle skill, includes the bead title/type/labels/parent/deps/description/acceptance/custom fields, and instructs JSON-only output matching LintResult.
  - Invoke dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx, projectRoot, svc, runner, rcfg, AgentRunRuntime{Prompt: prompt, WorkDir: projectRoot, PromptSource: "bead-lifecycle-lint"}) or an equivalent package-local wrapper that still uses AgentRunner/ResolvedConfig and not os/exec.
  - Parse Result.Output or Result.CondensedOutput as JSON into LintResult. Return typed/sentinel errors for missing skill/harness, bad JSON, timeout/context cancellation, and empty model output so ddx-d1dae2dd can append warnings and fail open.
  - Keep hook code independent of cli/cmd; CLI wiring belongs in a separate bead so ddx work can reuse this package-level hook later.

NON-SCOPE
  - Execute-loop warn/block policy; covered by ddx-d1dae2dd.
  - Config schema for block threshold beyond whatever ddx-d1dae2dd needs.
  - Triage hook implementation; covered by ddx-4375292b.
  - CLI flags for disabling lint.

PARENT
  ddx-e1a576a7.

DEPS
  No deps. ddx-c8dc1146 is closed and the LintResult/runtime hook contract exists.
title: agent: implement runner-backed bead lifecycle lint hook

description:
PROBLEM
Lifecycle status changes drive process metrics and cycle-time reports, but the metrics parser only understands open, in_progress, and closed. Proposed, blocked, and cancelled will be invisible or miscounted after the state-machine refactor.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:936 tracks only openMS, inProgressMS, and closedMS.
- cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:946-952 and 981-989 only accumulate time for open, in_progress, and closed.
- cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:1321-1370 parses event status tokens for open/in_progress/closed/reopened only.

PROPOSED FIX
Update process metrics to parse and account for all six lifecycle statuses. Reports should preserve existing fields where needed and add proposed/blocked/cancelled timing and transition counts. Cancelled should be terminal not-done, closed should remain terminal done, and reopen/revision logic should use the state-machine transition definitions.

NON-SCOPE
Do not redesign all metrics output. Keep the change scoped to lifecycle status parsing, timing, and tests.

DEPS
Depends on lifecycle core and store/query semantics.
title: metrics: account for all six lifecycle statuses

acceptance:
1. `TestProjectRuntimePreflight_MissingBeadLifecycleReportsCheckedPathsAndRemediation` verifies a project with `.agents/skills/ddx/SKILL.md` but no nested `bead-lifecycle` reports the resolved project root, both checked nested skill paths, `ddx update --force`, and `ddx doctor`.
2. `TestWorkPreflight_WarnsOnceForMissingLifecycleSkill` verifies `ddx work --once` emits one preflight warning before dispatch and does not repeat the warning across loop iterations in the same process.
3. `TestTryPreflight_WarnsBeforeClaimForMissingLifecycleSkill` verifies `ddx try <id>` runs the preflight before claim/worktree setup and includes the remediation text.
4. `TestServerPreflight_MissingLifecycleSkillStartsDegraded` verifies `ddx server` surfaces degraded startup diagnostics without blocking server construction/startup solely because `bead-lifecycle` is missing.
5. `TestProjectRuntimePreflight_LegacySkillSymlinkSuggestsUpdateForce` verifies legacy project-local skill symlinks reuse the FEAT-015 remediation path: `ddx update --force`, then `ddx doctor`.
6. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... -run 'Test(ProjectRuntimePreflight|WorkPreflight|TryPreflight|ServerPreflight)'` green.
7. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/...` green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Long-running DDx entrypoints can start work even when the project-local skill install is broken. In /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau, post-attempt triage logged `triage hook: skill missing: bead-lifecycle (continuing)` only after an execution failed. The operator had to infer that the project still had a pre-migration skill layout and needed `ddx update --force` / `ddx doctor`.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/cmd/work.go:106` delegates directly to `runAgentExecuteLoopImpl` without a lightweight runtime preflight. `cli/cmd/try.go:102-120` resolves execution flags and proceeds toward store/runtime setup without checking project-local skill prerequisites. `cli/cmd/server.go:98-146` starts the server without surfacing degraded project-local skill layout. `cli/cmd/doctor.go:281-287` already detects legacy skill symlinks and prints `run: ddx update --force`, but that diagnostic is only available when the operator explicitly runs `ddx doctor`.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a shared lightweight project-runtime preflight helper for long-running entrypoints. It should reuse existing doctor/install helper logic where practical, but must not run the full `ddx doctor` checks.
- Wire the helper into `ddx work`, `ddx try`, compatibility `ddx agent execute-loop`, and `ddx server` before execution begins.
- For work/try/execute-loop: fail on missing required project/tracker state, warn once per process for optional lifecycle-hook degradation in WARN-ONLY mode, and include remediation.
- For server: fail on required server project state, but log degraded startup diagnostics for missing lifecycle skills or legacy skill symlink layout.
- Checked paths for `bead-lifecycle` must be `.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` and `.claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` under the resolved project root.
- Remediation text must include `ddx update --force` first, then `ddx doctor`.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add network, package-manager, route/provider, stale-doc, or Fizeau health probes to this preflight.
- Do not change bead-lifecycle hook classification semantics.
- Do not make `ddx server` refuse to start solely because `bead-lifecycle` is missing.
- Do not edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` manually; tracker mutation is handled by this bead record only.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-a9d130d0. No deps.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx lacks a first-class operator-attention lane for beads that need human review. A Codex/Claude TUI or the web GUI cannot reliably answer “show me beads needing my attention” and then capture the operator review decision.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:39-41 defines ready/status as open/ready/blocked counts only. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md:223-229 defines Ready queue view but no Needs review filter. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md:243-263 defines review actions but not needs_human review intake/resolution in the bead GUI. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:343-352 and docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md:156-170 say terminal review cases block with needs_human but do not define status, metadata, CLI/TUI discovery, GUI expression, or resolution semantics.

PROPOSED FIX
Amend FEAT-004, FEAT-008, FEAT-010, and ADR-024 to define needs_human as status=open plus needs_human label and structured reason metadata; skipped by workers; discoverable through CLI/TUI and a Beads list Needs review filter; resolvable through explicit retry/split/obsolete/defer review actions with required notes in CLI and web detail UI.

NON-SCOPE
Implementation of CLI, GraphQL, web, or worker changes. New bd/br status values. Full artifact-review chat UI.

DEPS
No deps; this is the prerequisite spec contract.
title: spec: define needs-human operator review lane

description:
PROBLEM
DDx cannot safely assume Fizeau native harness identity `fiz` or Fizeau-owned `--model qwen36` fuzzy matching until those upstream contract changes land in a Fizeau release consumed by cli/go.mod.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
DDx currently imports github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau from cli/go.mod:7. The consumed Fizeau v0.10.9 public interface already has ServiceExecuteRequest.Model/Provider/Harness/ModelRef/MinPower/MaxPower fields, but its contract still names native behavior around `agent` and its current matching only partially normalizes model IDs. Upstream work is tracked in ../fizeau: fizeau-61668fd3 for native harness `agent` -> `fiz`, and fizeau-400b57fe for fuzzy model constraint resolution.

PROPOSED FIX
After upstream Fizeau closes fizeau-61668fd3 and fizeau-400b57fe and cuts a release, bump cli/go.mod/go.sum to that release. Update DDx Fizeau adapter tests and any test fixtures that assert native harness `agent` so they use `fiz`. Add DDx-side tests proving DDx passes raw model strings such as `qwen36` through to ServiceExecuteRequest.Model unchanged and records the actual model returned by Fizeau without implementing matching.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement fuzzy matching in DDx. Do not add `agent` compatibility aliases. Do not remove the `ddx agent` CLI in this bead; that is ddx-d8474e0e. Do not perform broad internal package renames; that is the internal-rename child.

UPSTREAM REFERENCES
- fizeau-61668fd3 must be closed in ../fizeau before this bead can complete.
- fizeau-400b57fe must be closed in ../fizeau before this bead can complete.

description:
PROBLEM
The candidate-cycle review/repair pipeline is not specified with enough precision for independent implementation beads. The current docs say review and repair should happen, but do not define the vocabulary, same-worktree lifecycle, candidate ref retention, read-only reviewer capability, transitional single-slot review allowance, or terminal dispositions.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:337-356 describes adversarial pre-close review and repair outcomes, but it does not state that review should run pre-land in the still-live attempt worktree.
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md:96-185 defines pre-close review policy, but still allows interpretation as post-land review and does not define read-only tool reviewer mechanics.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:231 mentions pre-close review as a close prerequisite, but does not define candidate-cycle events such as candidate-pinned, repair-cycle-started, approved-land-conflict, or repair-cycle-exhausted.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-022-prompt-evidence-assembly.md:310-354 discusses review evidence assembly, but does not clarify canonical bounded evidence versus supplemental same-worktree read-only access.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update FEAT-010 to define the layer-2 candidate-cycle pipeline: create worktree, implementation pass, project-root candidate ref, candidate checks, same-worktree read-only review, repair loop, final land, close/preserve/park, cleanup.
- Update ADR-024 to choose read-only tool reviewer as the review capability model and define same-worktree repair as append-only.
- Update FEAT-022/TD-033 references so bounded evidence remains canonical even when reviewer workdir is the attempt worktree.
- Update TD-031 with candidate-cycle event names, terminal dispositions, budgets, and claim/cooldown behavior.
- Add an explicit transitional allowance for single-slot review until the two-slot bead lands, with a removal condition.

NON-SCOPE
- No Go implementation changes.
- No new CLI flags.
- No manual edits to .ddx/beads.jsonl.

INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-639af4ef currently covers repair-cycle policy at a high level; this bead supersedes its ambiguous post-land framing by specifying the candidate-cycle pipeline.
- ddx-9228a484 covers retry budgets; this bead names the budget axes so implementation can be decomposed safely.

DEPS
None. ddx-639af4ef and ddx-9228a484 are informational cross-references/intersections, not blocking dependencies for this spec-definition bead.

description:
PROBLEM
Candidate commits created in a temp worktree must be resolvable from the project repository before checks, review, repair, landing, or crash recovery can rely on them. Today the worker returns a raw result_rev from the temp worktree, but no dedicated project-root candidate ref lifecycle exists for the FEAT-010 candidate-cycle pipeline.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:32-103 defines ExecuteBeadResult with base_rev/result_rev/preserve_ref fields but no candidate_ref or cycle_index metadata.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:397-410 in FEAT-010's sequence says candidate refs must be pinned before candidate checks and read-only review, but the loop currently goes from implementation result toward checks/review/land without a project-root candidate ref record.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:696-708 creates land-time preserve refs under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>, but that only covers failed/deferred landing; it is not the per-cycle candidate ref required before checks/review.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:333-365 defines each cycle as recording cycle_index, attempt_id, base_rev, result_rev, and a candidate ref under refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index>.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add CandidateRefStore or equivalent agent package helper that pins each close-eligible candidate commit in the project repo under refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index> before candidate checks and read-only review run.
- Use cycle_index starting at 0 for the first implementation cycle in an attempt and increment for each append-only repair cycle.
- Record candidate_ref and cycle_index alongside existing attempt metadata. The concrete storage target is ExecuteBeadResult/result.json (add JSON fields) plus the append-only bead event emitted for the candidate-cycle step; include base_rev and result_rev so the cycle can be reconstructed after worktree cleanup.
- Retention policy: landed temporary candidate refs may be deleted only after result.json and the bead event/landed evidence containing candidate_ref, base_rev, result_rev, attempt_id, and cycle_index are durable in the project branch. Preserved/manual/parked/conflicted candidates retain the candidate ref for operator inspection and recovery.
- Ensure git -C <projectRoot> rev-parse <candidate_ref> resolves even after the temp worktree is removed.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not move review or repair into the worktree yet.
- Do not implement stale ref pruning beyond the retention behavior above.
- Do not rename existing land-time preserve refs in execute_bead_land.go except where tests need to distinguish preserve_ref from candidate_ref.

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work` does not have a crisp human-facing contract for run modes or stdout. The current docs and code use `--poll-interval` as if it were the mode selector, but every queue worker scans the queue between attempts. Operators need explicit semantics for one-shot work, drain-and-exit work, and long-running watch work, plus output that answers whether the worker exited, why it exited, what queue state remains, and why an idle watcher is sleeping.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:685-702 currently describes the behavior as a `--poll-interval` default, conflating idle sleep tuning with the operator's chosen work mode.
- cli/cmd/work.go:37-50 presents `ddx work --poll-interval 30s` as the continuous-worker example rather than naming the mode directly.
- cli/cmd/work.go:75 exposes `--poll-interval` as the primary stay-alive control, even though `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:649-682` treats any positive interval as idle/watch behavior.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1885-1937 prints terminal summaries without stop reason, already-satisfied breakdown, remaining queue-state context, or blocked-behind-human-review explanation.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:656-688 emits idle progress internally but does not define the human stdout contract for idle or next-ready-bead transitions.

PROPOSED FIX
Update FEAT-010 and FEAT-006 with an explicit stdout and mode contract:
- `once`: process at most one execution-ready bead and exit. Public flag remains `--once`.
- `drain`: process the currently execution-ready queue until a terminal stop condition, then exit. Direct CLI `ddx work` uses this unless `--watch` is supplied.
- `watch`: process ready work, then remain idle and rescan for newly arriving execution-ready work until signal, fatal config, budget/resource stop, or operator stop. Public flag is `--watch`.
- `idle interval`: the sleep duration between empty-queue scans while in watch mode. Public flag is `--idle-interval <duration>` with a 30s default in watch mode.
- `--poll-interval` is removed from the public CLI and generated docs. There is no compatibility alias. New server/API inputs use `mode` + `idle_interval`, not `poll_interval`.
- Server-managed workers default to watch mode with a 30s idle interval unless the request explicitly asks for `once` or drain mode.

Also define the human stdout contract:
- Terminal summaries state attempts, closed count, changed/already-satisfied breakdown, failures, stop reason, and remaining queue state.
- Remaining queue state distinguishes execution-ready, blocked, needs-human, cooldown/deferred, not-eligible, superseded, and epic/closure-candidate buckets where DDx can compute them.
- Queue summaries explain dependency pressure from human-review blockers: if 30 unique open beads are transitively blocked behind 3 open `needs_human` / needs-investigation blockers, say so and list the blocker IDs/titles with blocked counts.
- Watch idle output says the worker is still running, gives the idle interval, includes queue-state summary, and uses direct wording such as `idle: no execution-ready beads; sleeping 30s`.
- Every idle poll prints a compact queue-state line. The full blocker list may be suppressed on unchanged repeated polls, but counts and sleep duration remain visible every poll.
- When watch mode wakes up and claims new work, stdout separates the new bead from the prior block and says `taking next ready bead from queue: <id> — <title>` before the bead block starts.
- Fizeau typed service events remain opaque; DDx may project route/model/tool summaries into phase-labeled human stdout without mutating the event schema.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement CLI flags or renderers in this docs bead.
- Do not change Fizeau event schemas, routing policy, structured JSONL timestamps, or stored event names.
- Do not create or modify Go tests in this docs bead; the Go command below is a regression gate only.

PARENT + DEPS

description:
Context:
On 2026-05-08, bead `fizeau-bd5193ca` ("Fix fiz version JSON output") in the fizeau repo went through 4 successive `ddx work` execute-bead attempts:
- 20260508T175640-b12741e5 (15m, $2.59, sonnet) → task_succeeded → result preserved at refs/ddx/iterations/fizeau-bd5193ca/…/2c3f896a
- 20260508T181204-20d01e6d (18m, $2.68, sonnet) → task_succeeded → preserved as b673f48a
- 20260508T183001-483c8a6e (15m, $1.74, sonnet) → task_succeeded → preserved as ee719835
- 20260508T184500-34ccc16f (~10m, sonnet) → task_succeeded → preserved as ebbeb0ad

None merged onto master; only `chore: update tracker (execute-bead …)` commits landed. Bead stayed in_progress until the loop was killed manually.

Operator manually cherry-picked attempt 3 (ee719835) onto fizeau master as 44d85287 and verified all acceptance criteria pass:
- `go test ./cmd/fiz ./agentcli` → both packages OK
- `fiz --version` → human-readable with non-empty commit
- `fiz version --json` → valid JSON with non-empty version, non-empty commit, boolean dirty

So the implementations themselves were correct. Some pre-merge check blocked them anyway. We don't yet know which check or why — see ddx-62edcb10 for the orthogonal observability bug that hid the rejection.

Investigation goals:
1. Read `RunPreMergeChecks` (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:148-area) and enumerate every check it runs by default for a fizeau-style repo.
2. Replay one of the preserved iteration refs (e.g. `refs/ddx/iterations/fizeau-bd5193ca/20260508T183001-483c8a6e-e9e33bba16bb`) through the pre-merge check pipeline against the matching base_rev to reproduce the block locally.
3. Identify which check returned Blocked=true and why. Determine whether it was a real defect in the implementation (and the cherry-pick happened to skip the check) or a false positive in the check itself.
4. If false positive, file a follow-up bead to fix the check; if real defect, document why the cherry-pick still passed go test.

Useful artifacts (in /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau):
- `.ddx/executions/20260508T17{5640,1204},18{3001,4500}-*/result.json` — worker outputs
- `refs/ddx/iterations/fizeau-bd5193ca/*` — preserved result trees
- `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-2026-05.jsonl` — session metadata
- `.ddx/attachments/fizeau-bd5193ca/events.jsonl` — overflow events

Depends on: ddx-62edcb10 only insofar as fixing that bug would have made this investigation a one-line lookup instead of a code dive. They can be tackled independently.

8. `TestQueueSnapshot_HumanReviewBlockersAggregateTransitiveBlockedBeads` builds a fixture with 30 open beads blocked behind 3 needs-human blockers and asserts the total and per-blocker counts/IDs/titles are present.
9. `TestExecuteBeadLoopResult_PreservesLoopEndExitReason` proves the structured event still carries the same exit reason.
10. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestExecuteBeadLoopResult|TestQueueSnapshot_HumanReviewBlockers|TestLoop_DrainExitsOnNoReadyWork|TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoReadyWork|TestExecuteBeadWorkerEmitsLoopEventsWithNoReadyWork' -count=1` passes.
11. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Human and JSON summaries cannot reliably answer why `ddx work` stopped or what queue state remained when it stopped. Idle watch output also cannot explain why the worker is sleeping beyond a flat "no ready work" signal. The loop emits `exit_reason` only in the structured `loop.end` event, while `ExecuteBeadLoopResult` exposes attempts/successes/failures and a partial `NoReadyWorkDetail` only for the zero-attempt no-ready path.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:531-542 keeps `exitReason` as a local variable and writes it only through the deferred `loop.end` event at lines 577-581.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:399-407 defines `ExecuteBeadLoopResult` without stop condition, exit reason, or a reusable queue-state summary.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:632-647 populates `NoReadyWorkDetail` only when `result.Attempts == 0`, so a drain that completed work and then emptied the queue cannot summarize the remaining queue state.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1576-1715 exposes `BlockedAll` with dependency blockers, retry cooldowns, needs-human/investigation blockers, execution-ineligible beads, superseded beads, and epic-only blockers, but the work loop does not aggregate those into a queue snapshot.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1885-1937 can only print attempts/successes/failures and bare route lines because the loop result does not carry stop and queue-state fields.

PROPOSED FIX
Expose stop and queue state as first-class loop-result data:
- Add stable fields to `ExecuteBeadLoopResult` for stop condition/exit reason and a compact queue snapshot.
- Populate stop condition/exit reason whenever `applyStop` classifies a terminal stop, and preserve existing `loop.end.exit_reason` behavior.
- Populate queue snapshot whenever the picker reaches the no-candidate path, including after successful attempts, not just the zero-attempt case.
- The queue snapshot should distinguish at least: execution-ready count, blocked count, needs-human/investigation count, cooldown/deferred count and next retry time, execution-ineligible count, superseded count, skipped epics, and epic closure candidates where the store can compute them.
- Add a transitive dependency-pressure summary for human-review blockers. A human blocker is an open bead that carries `needs_human`, `needs_investigation`, or the existing no-changes triage labels. For each such blocker, compute how many open downstream beads are transitively blocked by it. Expose total blocked-by-human count plus blocker entries with ID, title, priority, and downstream count.
- Keep existing JSON field names for `no_ready_work` and `no_ready_work_detail` compatible; add new fields rather than removing old ones.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the human renderer in this bead; the idle and terminal summary renderers consume the new fields.
- Do not change bead picker ordering, retry policy, or readiness classification.
- Do not implement watch/drain CLI flag naming; ddx-f9acb86d owns that.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-eeced112. Depends on ddx-7d9e1e82 for the documented summary contract.

1. Execution records include append-only cycle trace data for both initial implementation cycles and repair cycles with fields: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer_index values, review verdicts, review result, and final decision.

3. TestExecutionTrace_AdapterPreservesCycleFields proves cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_try_adapter.go preserves all cycle trace fields in both conversion directions.
4. Existing bead.result/review event consumers remain compatible; if durable field names are retained for compatibility, the test documents those retained names explicitly.
5. No trace writer overwrites or rewrites an older cycle entry for the same result_rev/review_group_id.

description:
PROBLEM
Once work supports initial and repair cycles, operators need a durable append-only trace for every implementation and repair cycle. Today the loop emits bead.result and review events, but there is no single cycle trace schema that connects cycle_index, attempt_id, implementer route facts, review group slots, review verdicts, and final decision.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:31-67 defines the try.Report shape without cycle_index, review_group_id, reviewer slot, or final cycle decision fields.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_try_adapter.go:9-46 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_try_adapter.go:49-86 copy report fields between legacy and try shapes, so any new trace fields must be wired through both directions while the compatibility layer exists.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1339-1349 emits bead.result without cycle trace fields.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_group.go:56-94 builds review_group_id and reviewer_index slot data, but that data remains review-local rather than being attached to a durable cycle trace.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:200-227 records the reduced review verdict and closes on APPROVE without appending a unified initial/repair cycle record.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an append-only execution cycle trace data shape for initial implementation cycles and review repair cycles.
- Required fields: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer_index values, review verdicts, review result, and final decision.
- Persist the trace in execution evidence and/or bead events using an append-only format that can survive retries and process restarts.
- Wire the trace through the try.Report/legacy ExecuteBeadReport adapter while both shapes exist.
- Cover both the first implementation cycle and the bounded repair cycle introduced by ddx-ece97605.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement repair-cycle scheduling; this bead consumes the repair behavior from ddx-ece97605.
- Do not build frontend UI for cycle traces.
- Do not prune, compact, or rewrite older execution evidence.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-ece97605 so the trace test can cover both initial and repair cycles.
title: evidence: record append-only implementation and repair cycle trace

description:
Fizeau v0.10.10 is published at https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau/releases/tag/v0.10.10. DDx currently depends on github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau v0.10.9 in cli/go.mod, so service progress rendering still shows duplicate tool rows and uses turn_index as the visible counter. Fizeau v0.10.10 changes normal tool progress to emit only the completed progress row and adds ServiceProgressData.tool_call_id plus ServiceProgressData.tool_call_index. Update DDx to consume v0.10.10 and render tool progress counters from tool_call_index when present. Keep start/finish semantics available for any future delayed-running display, but normal fast tool calls should appear as one row containing the command/action and output summary.

In-scope files:
- cli/go.mod and cli/go.sum
- cli/internal/agent/session_log_format.go
- cli/internal/agent/session_log_format_test.go or existing progress formatter tests
- service progress fixtures under cli/internal/agent/testdata/progress_corpus if needed

Out-of-scope:
- Changing Fizeau event production; released upstream in v0.10.10
- Broad worker UI redesign
- Changing unrelated execute-bead or cleanup behavior already dirty in this worktree

acceptance:
AC1. After a successful ddx try/ddx work attempt merge, git status -s in the operators main worktree shows ONLY the changes to .ddx/beads.jsonl (tracker close event) and any genuinely untracked files. The beads merged file changes do NOT appear staged in the index.
AC2. A regression Go test exercises: prepare main worktree at HEAD, simulate a bead worktree branch with a code change, run the merge + evidence-stage sequence, then assert git ls-files --stage and git diff --cached --name-only contain only expected paths (no pre-merge revert entries).
AC3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes.
AC4. cd cli && golangci-lint run passes.
description:
PROBLEM: After ddx work auto-merges a beads worktree branch into master (cb300d7-style merge commit), the main worktrees git INDEX ends up with the OLD pre-merge content of the beads modified files staged. Verified twice in axon project, 2026-05-08.

SYMPTOM: git status shows M in column 1 (staged-vs-HEAD) for the very files the bead just modified; working tree matches index; both differ from HEAD. Any subsequent operator git add of an unrelated file followed by git commit then sweeps in those staged reverts, undoing the beads work.

REPRO (axon, 2026-05-08):
- 333d1a1 [axon-044a5b5b] modified ui/src/.../audit/+page.svelte and ui/tests/e2e/intent-audit-lineage.spec.ts
- cb300d7 merged into master; master HEAD content is correct
- After merge, git status -s in main worktree:
  M  ui/src/routes/.../audit/+page.svelte
  M  ui/tests/e2e/intent-audit-lineage.spec.ts
  D  .ddx/executions/20260508T204742-afe71867/manifest.json (and others)
- git diff HEAD reports the index differs from HEAD by reverting the bead changes and deleting the exec dir files
- Operator recovery: git checkout HEAD -- <file paths> resets both index and working tree

LIKELY ROOT CAUSE: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go around RealLandingGitOps.MergeInto and landEvidence/StageDir. The merge updates working tree but the index appears to be left at the pre-merge state for the touched files. Then StageDir runs git add on the exec dir, which flips the index entries for those files to "staged for deletion" (since the worktree was deleted) but leaves the audit-page entries alone in their pre-merge state.

PROPOSED FIX DIRECTION (owned by ddx maintainers):
- After MergeInto succeeds, ensure the index in the main worktree is fully synchronized with HEAD before any subsequent StageDir or add operations.
- Or: verify that git merge --no-ff is being run with the main worktree as cwd (not a different worktree) so the index updates correctly.
- Or: explicit git reset HEAD or git read-tree HEAD between merge and staging to defensively reset the index.

NON-SCOPE: changing merge strategy (--no-ff vs ff-only is fixed by skill guidance).
title: bug: ddx merge leaks pre-merge file content into operator's git index

description:
PROBLEM
CLI queue surfaces present needs-human and blocked lanes that are derived from labels or ordinary dependency waits. After the status-owned state machine lands, operators need commands that show proposed, open/ready, dependency-waiting, in-progress, external-blocked, closed, and cancelled consistently.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/work_focus.go:57-83 builds a human_required list from store.NeedsHuman.
- cli/cmd/work_focus.go:86-115 excludes needs_human from blocked output using old label semantics.
- cli/cmd/work_focus.go:181-198 documents needs_human/review-blocked help text.
- cli/cmd/bead.go:916,957,979 consumes ReadyExecution/BlockedAll/BlockerKindNeedsInvestigation/BlockerKindNeedsHuman for bead ready/status output.
- cli/cmd/try_test.go currently accepts only selected status update values, while the help text for bead update is stale versus the six upstream statuses.

PROPOSED FIX
Update CLI commands and help to use status-owned lifecycle vocabulary. `ddx bead ready` and `ddx work focus` should show execution-ready open beads separately from dependency-waiting open beads and proposed operator-attention beads. `ddx bead blocked` should mean external recheckable blockers, not ordinary dependency waits. Status commands should report the six statuses and derived buckets. Any old needs-human command surface must either be removed, renamed, or explicitly deprecated as an alias for proposed/operator-attention.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change server GraphQL/API or frontend UI in this bead. Do not mutate live tracker rows; migration has a separate bead.

DEPS
Depends on store/query wiring and legacy migration behavior.
title: cli: update bead and work-focus commands for proposed lifecycle

2. TestWorkCleanup_RunsPeriodicallyWhilePolling uses a fake clock/ticker to verify long-lived polling triggers cleanup without busy looping.

description:
PROBLEM
Cleanup cannot rely only on the next ddx try setup path. Long-lived ddx work processes and server-managed workers can sit idle or run many attempts while stale DDx temp worktrees, heartbeat files, and partial setup directories accumulate in the background.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:437-530 starts the work loop and emits loop.start/loop.end, but there is no periodic cleanup worker tied to long-lived polling or graceful shutdown.
- cli/cmd/work.go:101 and related command plumbing start ddx work without a cleanup cadence or cleanup lock.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:49 documents server/FEAT-010 worker state, but server-managed worker paths do not share a background cleanup contract.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:476-497 and docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:232-240 require startup, periodic, shutdown, and background cleanup with jitter and a cleanup lock.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add background cleanup orchestration for long-lived ddx work/server worker processes using the cleanup manager from the base cleanup bead.
- Run cleanup at ddx work startup, periodically while polling, after setup/finalization failures before the next claim, and during graceful signal shutdown.
- Use jitter plus a project cleanup lock so multiple workers do not prune simultaneously.
- Emit structured cleanup events and operator-visible summaries only when cleanup reclaims meaningful resources or blocks progress.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the cleanup manager primitives here.
- Do not replace server-spawn migration work in ddx-eccc6efb; if server workers exec ddx work, they may inherit this behavior instead of duplicating it.
- Do not delete complete evidence or preserved worktrees.
title: work: run periodic background cleanup for long-lived workers

acceptance:
1. TestBeadsTrackerHookAllowsCleanTracker verifies the new guard exits 0 when .ddx/beads.jsonl has no changes.
2. TestBeadsTrackerHookAllowsFullyStagedTrackerChange verifies the guard exits 0 when .ddx/beads.jsonl changes are staged and there are no unstaged tracker changes.
3. TestBeadsTrackerHookFailsOnUnstagedTrackerChange verifies the guard exits non-zero and prints git add .ddx/beads.jsonl when the tracker has unstaged changes.
4. TestBeadsTrackerHookFailsOnPartiallyStagedTrackerChange verifies staged plus unstaged tracker changes are rejected so partial tracker commits cannot leave state behind.

description:
PROBLEM
Tracker mutations are still easy to leave behind in the operator checkout. Several ddx task paths either rely on best-effort auto-commit or create implementation commits that do not include an already-dirty .ddx/beads.jsonl, leaving the queue state uncommitted after work appears done.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- AGENTS.md:35-39 says tracker mutations must be committed by default, but this is policy guidance rather than a mechanical guard.
- cli/cmd/bead.go:74-97 makes bead auto-commit best-effort and silently ignores AutoCommit errors, so failed tracker commits can leave .ddx/beads.jsonl dirty.
- lefthook.yml:28-50 has fast pre-commit guards for conflicts and local git config, but no guard that fails a commit when .ddx/beads.jsonl has unstaged or partially staged changes.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a fast pre-commit guard that inspects .ddx/beads.jsonl directly with git diff.
- Fail when .ddx/beads.jsonl has unstaged changes, or when it has both staged and unstaged changes, so commits cannot leave tracker mutations behind accidentally.
- Allow a commit when .ddx/beads.jsonl is clean, or when all tracker changes are staged in the commit.
- Keep the guard narrow to .ddx/beads.jsonl; do not require all .ddx evidence artifacts to be staged.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the bead storage format.
- Do not make every .ddx/ path mandatory in every commit.
- Do not remove existing beadAutoCommit behavior; this is a commit-time safety net.

DEPS
No deps. This is a direct prevention follow-up from the May 7 worker-collision cleanup.
title: hooks: fail commits that leave beads tracker dirty

description:
Once REACH-PROTO + REACH-GO have landed and ddx self-installs the check, run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current cli/ tree and resolve every violation. This is the systematic application phase the user mandated: "go back through the codebase systematically applying it."

ABSORBS
- ddx-09d2990c (WIRE) — the 4 specific unwired functions become a subset of this sweep. Close WIRE as superseded by this bead at file time.
- Any clusters from /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md not already covered.
- Codex audit additions: jsonlFallbackForCollection (cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go:90), Store.breakStaleLock (lock.go:70), NewStoreWithBackend (store.go:119), WithSpokeHTTPClient (federation_spoke.go:103), Server.execStore (server.go:2028), GraphQL NewResolver (resolver.go:14).

EXPECTED WORKFLOW
1. Run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current worktree. Capture violation list.
2. For each violation, decide: WIRE (add to production code path) or DELETE (function is genuinely obsolete).
3. File one child bead per violation cluster (group by package or by originating story for traceability).
4. Drain the children. Each closes either via wiring PR or deletion PR.
5. Final pass: ddx ac run returns status=pass with no violations and no // wiring:pending annotations remaining (or all annotations cite still-open future beads).

DECISION RULE per violation
- If the symbol's originating bead's AC describes desired runtime behavior → WIRE.
- If the symbol was added speculatively or the design has moved on → DELETE; reopen the originating bead with obsolescence rationale, or close as obsolete.
- If neither is clear within 15 min of investigation → annotate // wiring:pending <follow-up-bead> and file the follow-up.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744), REACH-GO (ddx-ea76e1b8)

NOT IN SCOPE
- The protocol or check implementation itself (separate beads).
- Other languages (this project is Go-only at the check layer).
- Auto-run as recurring sweep (could be a future /schedule offer; not built into this bead).

description:
PROBLEM
The try command needs an end-to-end regression that proves a resource_exhausted failure leaves the target bead open and reclaimable instead of parking it behind execute-loop-retry-after or consuming the claim. Existing tests in cli/cmd/try_test.go exercise not-found, closed, unmet-deps, happy-path, and interruption, but they do not cover the command-level resource exhaustion path.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/cmd/try.go:324-333 checks the resource checker before the worker run and returns an ExitError on failure. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:695-715 turns a ResourceExhaustedError into a structured result, but there is no command-level test that verifies the failing bead remains open/unclaimed and does not receive a retry cooldown marker.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add TestTryResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_Reclaimable in cli/cmd/try_test.go or a sibling e2e test file.
- Seed one ready bead in a temp project, inject a resource checker failure through the command factory, and run ddx try on that bead.
- Assert the command exits non-zero, prints the resource_exhausted stop message, leaves the bead open with no owner, and does not write execute-loop-retry-after.
- Use temp dirs and fakes only; do not fill real disk or inodes.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not test unrelated claim-approval or review flows.
- Do not add new retry-policy behavior.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestTryResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_Reclaimable exists and invokes ddx try with an injected resource_exhausted failure.
2. The command exits non-zero, prints resource_exhausted, and leaves the bead open and unclaimed.
3. The test verifies no execute-loop-retry-after marker is written.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestTryResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_Reclaimable" -count=1 passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: test: keep ddx try resource exhaustion reclaimable

description:
PROBLEM
The ddx skill is the gateway most interactive agents read before deciding what to do. Without explicit mode guidance there, the agent can still interpret broad queue questions as implementation requests even after AGENTS.md and specs are updated.

ROOT CAUSE
.agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:18-23 maps "What should I work on next?" directly to ddx bead ready, which is the worker-ready queue. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:1-16 defines doing work as draining the ready queue and recommends ddx work, but it does not say broad interactive sessions should manage intervention/planning instead of manually executing ready beads. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:43-49 tells agents to pick the next ready bead by default without distinguishing direct worker mode from broad interactive queue management.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the canonical ddx skill references and shipped copies so broad interactive DDx prompts enter queue_steward mode. The skill should tell agents to inspect intervention/focus status, summarize blockers, propose bead/spec changes, and suggest a worker when ready depth warrants it; explicit ddx work/try/execute-bead prompts remain bead_execution and may implement bead AC. Keep review prompts read-only unless the user asks to fix findings.

NON-SCOPE
- Editing SKILL.md frontmatter unless required by existing schema.
- Changing skill install/discovery mechanics.
- Implementing ddx work focus itself; this bead consumes that command surface.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-09d7d787. Dependencies: ddx-377ebf92 and ddx-3c6ca63f.
notes: SUPERSEDED by ddx-c2a66f45-wt-6bee7238. The interactive-steward routing bead now owns the first-stop interactive.md work, existing status/work/review reference cleanup, route fixtures, eval-driver schema update, and shipped-copy verification. Do not dispatch this bead independently.
title: ddx skill: route broad prompts to queue stewardship

description:
execute_bead_loop.go:483-510 routing preflight runs per-iteration today AND treats its rejection as a worker-terminal exit (the user's original bug #2). Move to Drain startup: run once before the for-loop. If it fails, return one worker-level execution_failed record and exit (legitimate startup failure). Never run again per iteration.

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: routing preflight should fail open when its own check rejects.
- P2: preflight only owns startup advisory checks, not eligibility or provider availability.
- P3: skipped preflight should surface as an explicit event.
- P4: a preflight failure on one bead must not affect later beads.

description:
PROBLEM
After pre-land candidate review is stable, the legacy post-land RunPostMergeReview path must be retired or made unreachable. Keeping both paths risks divergent review semantics, double review, and future fixes landing in only one state machine.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1086-1104 still calls RunPostMergeReview from the old success branch.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:53-77 documents the extracted state machine as post-merge/pre-close.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:715-812 ReviewBead still assumes project-root git show review rather than candidate-cycle review in a live worktree.

PROPOSED FIX
- Remove or explicitly gate off the old post-land review call path once candidate-cycle review owns close eligibility.
- Keep any reusable parsing/prompt/artifact helpers, but move policy decisions to the candidate-cycle coordinator.
- Update tests and docs so there is a single review/repair policy path.
- Preserve a manual/operator bead review command if still needed, but make it separate from automated candidate-cycle review.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove reusable review prompt assembly, verdict parsing, or artifact-writing helpers.
- Do not change two-slot aggregation behavior beyond eliminating the legacy policy path.

DEPS
Depends on two-slot candidate review so the replacement path satisfies ADR-024 before the old path is retired.
title: agent: retire legacy post-land review path

acceptance:

description:
PROBLEM
Governing specs mix "complexity gate," "pre-claim intake," "pre-dispatch lint," and readiness language, so DDx has no stable product concept for the pre-claim decision about whether a bead is tractable and actionable.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md:50-67` defines the hook as "Pre-claim intake" and describes actionability, scope, and decomposition without naming bead readiness as the canonical concept.
- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:112-145` repeats the intake terminology inside task execution lifecycle text.
- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:142-143` describes bead-lifecycle as lint/triage, omitting readiness.

PROPOSED FIX
Amend ADR-023, FEAT-010, FEAT-004, FEAT-011, and TD-031 review notes so "bead readiness assessment" is the canonical product concept. Keep `MODE: intake` and existing intake event names only as compatibility details. Distinguish readiness, lint/rubric scoring, and post-attempt triage.

NON-SCOPE
Do not edit skills, Go code, generated docs, or tests in this bead.
title: docs: align bead readiness terminology across governing specs
