description:
Add a language-agnostic check protocol to ddx so projects can declare mechanical pre-merge gates that ddx try evaluates before any worktree is merged back. Solves the recurring "implemented but not wired" failure mode at the platform layer rather than per-project.

CONTEXT
- Audit at /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md surfaced 4 functions with tests but no production caller; reviewer didn't catch it because the diff "looked right" and tests passed.
- ac_structural_check.go (cli/internal/agent/) is the existing seed: parses claims from AC prose for test_name/file_deleted/field_removed. It can't catch the unwired case because a unit test naming the function satisfies the claim vacuously.
- Prior plan considered embedding Go-specific call-graph analysis directly in cli/internal/agent/. Rejected as a boundary violation (CLAUDE.md: "DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions"). Each language ecosystem has its own dead-code tooling (Go: deadcode/callgraph; Python: vulture; TS: ts-prune/knip; Rust: cargo-udeps).

DESIGN — protocol (declarative, plugin-based)
- Discovery: .ddx/checks/*.yaml each declares one check: { name, command, when: pre_merge, applies_to: { paths: [globs] | always, labels: [...] } }
- Inputs to check command (env vars): BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR, RUN_ID
- Output contract: check writes ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json with schema { status: pass|block|error, message: string, violations: [{file, line, symbol, kind, detail}] }
- Exit code 0 = ran-to-completion (status field is authoritative); non-zero = check itself errored (treated as BLOCK with status=error)
- Hook point: pre_merge — runs in the parent (after worker's worktree work is done, BEFORE the merge-back into integration branch). BLOCK aborts the merge; the worktree stays preserved per existing conventions for human resolution.
- Bypass: bead-level annotation checks_bypass: [{name: <check>, reason: <text>, bead: <bead-id>}] recorded in events. No CLI flag — bypass must be tracked.
- Standalone: ddx ac run <bead-id> [--check <name>] runs the same checks locally so devs can iterate without spawning a full try attempt.

WHY pre_merge (NOT pre_close in the worker)
- User directive: "We can't afford to merge this kind of dead code." Worker-side checks could be bypassed by a worker that ignores BLOCK; parent-side gate cannot.
- Worker still gets feedback via ddx ac run for local iteration.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Multiple lifecycle hook points beyond pre_merge (add later if needed).
- Check dependencies / ordering (independent + parallel until proven insufficient).
- Plugin manifests, capability flags, version negotiation.
- Auto-discovery beyond .ddx/checks/*.yaml.
- Retry / timeout policy (project owns; checks should be fast).
- Any specific language check (separate beads).

OUT OF SCOPE: REACH-GO (the Go-specific deadcode-based check) and REACH-BACKFILL (the systematic application across cli/) are separate beads that depend on this one.

FILES (anticipated)
- cli/internal/checks/protocol.go — config schema + JSON I/O types
- cli/internal/checks/loader.go — read .ddx/checks/*.yaml
- cli/internal/checks/runner.go — subprocess invocation, env injection, output parsing, evidence dir mgmt
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go — wire pre_merge call before merge step; BLOCK aborts merge
- cli/cmd/ac.go — ddx ac run subcommand
- cli/internal/checks/*_test.go — table-driven tests for protocol contract

description:
PROBLEM
Per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES bead ddx-06b77652 P7 ('bead = sufficient sub-agent prompt'), bead authoring quality is a dominant factor in execution success. Audit-1 (commit 86848d21) covered 20 beads; audit-2 (commit b2eb77d0) covered 108 beads; 88 of 108 scored ≤5 against the rubric. Evidence bead ddx-f339c399 documents one observed no_changes outcome (ddx-29058e2a, 2026-05-04) traced to bead-quality gap.

ROOT CAUSE
Today nothing mechanically prevents authoring a bead that's insufficient as a sub-agent prompt OR catches a non-clean execution outcome before it triggers cooldown. The 9 fail-prone layers in ddx try / ddx work (per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES) are mostly fail-closed; bead authoring quality has no observability or enforcement layer at all.

PROPOSED FRAMEWORK
A SKILL-based bead-lifecycle quality framework, layered onto the existing ddx skill (matching the bead-breakdown/ sub-skill pattern at .agents/skills/ddx/bead-breakdown/SKILL.md). Three modes within ONE sub-skill: lint (pre-dispatch), triage (post-attempt outcome classification), refine (suggest amendments). ddx try integrates via small Go hooks that compose the existing layer-1 runner library to invoke the skill.

NO new top-level skills. NO new ddx CLI verbs. NO LLM judgment baked into Go code.

CHILD BEADS (4 sequential phases)
1. ADR-023 + FEAT amendments (governance + policy)
2. Sub-skill ships to all 5 paths + curated examples
3. ddx try hook integration (warn-only mode) + OutcomeReason field
4. Migration: close stale dups + retrofit 40 HIGH beads (during warn-only; collects baseline)

After child #4 confirms baseline acceptable false-positive rate, BLOCK mode is enabled via config (no separate bead).

NON-SCOPE
- ddx work plan quality column (defer; informational, not blocking)
- ddx bead refine standalone command (defer; refine mode in skill is invocable via ddx run)
- Cross-project skill packaging (DDx-only first; package later per FEAT-015 project-local model)
- in_progress-as-eligible bug (separate concern; queue correctness, not bead quality)

GOVERNANCE
- ADR-023 at docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md (codex correction: not 03-decide/)
- FEAT-004 amend: bead validation hooks + waiver storage + tracker metadata (FEAT-004:150 already owns bead validation hooks)
- FEAT-010 amend: ddx try / ddx work hook ordering + retry classification + outcome records
- FEAT-011 amend: nested workflow-skill packaging (FEAT-011:180 stale — says DDx ships exactly one skill, but bead-breakdown/ exists)
- FEAT-001 amend ONLY if --force --reason CLI flag added (defer to child #3 if needed)

description:
PROBLEM
DDx currently has a worker queue and an in-progress claim state, but no first-class operator-attention lane for beads that require human review. When review triage decides needs_human, the tracker can leave beads in in_progress while worker execution skips needs_human labels, making them neither worker-actionable nor clearly operator-actionable.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:39-41 defines ready/status as open/ready/blocked counts only. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:343-352 and docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md:156-170 say review/spec gaps block with needs_human, but they do not define the operator lane, CLI discovery surface, resolution semantics, or claim transition. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1060-1090 counts a failed pre-close review but does not unclaim the bead.

PROPOSED FIX
Create a spec-first implementation queue that defines and implements needs_human as a first-class operator-attention lane: open + needs_human + structured reason metadata, excluded from ddx work, discoverable by agents/TUIs via ddx bead needs-human, and resolvable through explicit supported actions.

NON-SCOPE
- Full interactive artifact review chat UI.
- Changing the bd/br canonical status enum.
- Removing existing retry/escalate review-block behavior for fixable gaps.

CHILD QUEUE
- Spec update bead defines state model and CLI contract.
- Store/count helpers support the lane.
- CLI list/show/status/ready surfaces make it agent-friendly.
- Resolve command performs explicit retry/split/obsolete/defer actions.
- Execute loop transitions terminal needs_human review blocks to open+needs_human.
- Reconcile repairs historical stale rows.
title: operator lane: define needs-human review workflow

acceptance:
1. Guardrail tests fail on a fixture that adds a new `ddx agent` command or a new DDx-owned `cli/internal/agent` package.
2. Guardrail tests fail on a fixture that normalizes or fuzzy-matches `ServiceExecuteRequest.Model` in DDx run/try/work code.
3. Guardrail tests fail on config-driven default `Harness`, `Provider`, or `Model` injection into Fizeau Execute requests, while allowing explicit operator passthrough flags.
4. Guardrail tests allow audited literal external-agent terms and durable legacy storage keys through an explicit allowlist.
5. Existing ddx-0ca4ffda is updated, closed as superseded, or cross-referenced so there is one authoritative guardrail plan.
6. `cd cli && go test ./tools/lint/...` passes.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` passes.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
After specs and implementation remove DDx-owned `agent` vocabulary and routing defaults, the repo needs mechanical guardrails so future changes do not reintroduce DDx-side routing, fuzzy model matching, `agent.*` defaults, or new `ddx agent`/`cli/internal/agent` surfaces.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
Existing guardrail bead ddx-0ca4ffda only targets Profile/Harness/Model assignment on Runtime structs. The finalized plan is broader: DDx must not normalize model strings such as `qwen36`, must not inspect Fizeau catalogs for fuzzy matching, must not set model/provider/harness defaults from config, and must not add new DDx-owned `agent` packages or public commands. FEAT-006/FEAT-010 will define those constraints after ddx-424fdac4.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend routinglint/runtimelint or add a terminology lint so it catches: `ddx agent` public command additions, new `cli/internal/agent` package paths, DDx-side model normalization/fuzzy matching in run/try/work, DDx-side default Model/Provider/Harness config injection, and non-allowlisted `agent` terminology in DDx-owned execution packages/docs. Merge or supersede ddx-0ca4ffda as appropriate; keep explicit operator passthrough allowed.

NON-SCOPE
Do not block literal external-agent references, agentskills.io standard language, historical audit docs, or durable legacy keys that specs intentionally allowlist. Do not forbid explicit CLI passthrough flags. Do not implement Fizeau matching.

PARENT AND DEPENDENCIES
Parent: ddx-f088a3fd. Depends on spec update, Fizeau release integration, public CLI removal, and internal rename.
title: Add DDx guardrails for Fizeau routing boundary and agent-term removal

acceptance:
1. ExecuteBeadLoopResult and no-ready detail use lifecycle-derived queue snapshot fields and no longer expose SkippedNeedsInvestigation as a live bucket.
2. Work stop/idle summaries distinguish drained empty queue, proposed operator-attention, dependency-waiting open work, external-blocked work, cooldown, ineligible, superseded, and epics.
3. Proposed operator-attention beads stop autonomous work with a message naming the proposed blockers and downstream pressure.
4. Dependency-waiting open beads are reported as waiting on deps, not blocked status.
5. TestExecuteBeadLoopResult_LifecycleQueueSnapshot covers a run that completes one bead then reports remaining proposed/dependency-waiting/external-blocked/cooldown buckets.
6. TestWorkStopSummary_ProposedVsDependencyWaiting proves proposed and dependency-waiting are rendered differently.
7. TestNoReadyWorkDetail_DoesNotExposeNeedsInvestigation proves old investigation buckets are gone from runtime output.
8. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TestExecuteBeadLoopResult_LifecycleQueueSnapshot|TestWorkStopSummary|TestNoReadyWorkDetail' -count=1` passes.
9. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/...` passes.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work` stop and no-ready reporting still uses old bucket names such as skipped needs-investigation and a generic blocked stop condition. After lifecycle state becomes status-owned, worker idle/stop output must distinguish execution-ready, dependency-waiting, proposed operator-attention, cooldown, external blocked, ineligible, superseded, and epic-only states through the lifecycle derivation.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:632-645 only fills NoReadyWorkDetail on the zero-attempt no-ready path and includes SkippedNeedsInvestigation.
- cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go:21-30 has a generic Blocked stop input/condition without tying it to external blockers versus dependency waiting or proposed operator-attention.
- cli/cmd/work_focus.go:57-115 and cli/cmd/bead.go:950-958 currently rely on old needs-human/blocked store buckets for operator guidance.

PROPOSED FIX
Refactor the worker no-candidate path and stop summary to consume lifecycle-derived queue snapshots from the store. Empty-queue, dependency-waiting, proposed, external-blocked, cooldown, and ineligible states must be reported separately. Proposed beads stop autonomous work with an operator-attention summary; dependency-waiting does not become status=blocked; external blocked is rare and recheckable. Remove SkippedNeedsInvestigation from runtime stop logic.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change actual routing/power selection in this bead; smart investigation routing has a separate bead.

DEPS
Depends on lifecycle query derivation and agent parking updates.
title: work: derive no-ready and stop output from lifecycle buckets

acceptance:
1. `skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` documents `MODE: readiness`.
2. It states `MODE: intake` is compatibility for readiness.
3. It includes reason codes from ADR-023.
4. It says infra failures must return system readiness findings, not bead-quality findings.
5. It preserves the current intake JSON compatibility contract or explicitly references the parser-migration bead.
6. `skills/ddx/SKILL.md` routes “assess bead readiness” to bead-lifecycle.
7. `.agents`, `.claude`, and `cli/internal/skills` copies are synchronized.
8. `ddx skills check skills/ddx/SKILL.md skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md .agents/skills/ddx/SKILL.md .agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md .claude/skills/ddx/SKILL.md .claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md cli/internal/skills/ddx/SKILL.md cli/internal/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` validates changed skill files.
9. `make skill-schema` passes.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
CLI prompts can use `MODE: intake`, but the bead-lifecycle skill only documents lint, triage, and refine, so readiness/intake behavior is under-specified and can return empty or parser-incompatible output.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md:16-22` lists only `MODE: lint`, `MODE: triage`, and `MODE: refine`.
- `.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md:16-22`, `.claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md:16-22`, and `cli/internal/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md:16-22` mirror the same missing readiness contract.
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:179` builds prompts beginning with `MODE: intake`, which the skill does not define.

PROPOSED FIX
Add canonical `MODE: readiness`, define `MODE: intake` as compatibility, add structured readiness reason codes from ADR-023, and preserve existing parser compatibility expectations or explicitly point to the parser-migration bead.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change Go hook parsing or CLI output.

acceptance:
1. DDx-owned production code no longer lives under `cli/internal/agent`; remaining `agent` package/path references are audited as literal external-agent, generated historical data, or compatibility storage.
2. FEAT-010 package/path references match the implemented package names.
3. All new or renamed DDx task-execution identifiers use run/try/work/taskexec/fizeauadapter vocabulary rather than Agent/ExecuteBead/ExecuteLoop vocabulary, except audited durable compatibility names.
4. Existing stale beads ddx-387a0178 and ddx-3ee4ddcf are updated, closed as superseded, or otherwise made non-dispatchable so they no longer instruct workers to preserve `ddx agent` aliases or create new `cli/internal/agent` packages.
5. `rg -n "cli/internal/agent|AgentRunRuntime|AgentRunner|ExecuteBead|ExecuteLoop|ddx agent" cli docs .agents/skills .claude/skills skills` returns only audited retained hits documented in the implementation notes.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx internals still use `cli/internal/agent`, AgentRunRuntime, AgentRunner, ExecuteBead*, and related names for DDx-owned task execution. After the spec update, those names will be wrong: DDx owns task execution, while Fizeau owns routing/execution internals. Keeping `agent` as the DDx package name invites future routing-policy drift and confusion with real external AI agents.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/ contains DDx-owned queue drain, bead attempt, review, worktree, session evidence, and Fizeau adapter code. Existing refactor bead ddx-387a0178 still targets cli/internal/agent/try and cli/internal/agent/work and acceptance #7 keeps deprecated `ddx agent` aliases. Existing terminology bead ddx-3ee4ddcf still says not to remove legacy aliases. Those plans conflict with the no-deprecation decision and with FEAT-010 task-execution vocabulary.

PROPOSED FIX
After specs are ratified and the Fizeau contract cleanup release is integrated, split/rename DDx-owned internals to names matching responsibilities, for example `cli/internal/taskexec`, `cli/internal/taskexec/run`, `cli/internal/taskexec/try`, `cli/internal/taskexec/work`, `cli/internal/fizeauadapter`, and `cli/internal/runlog` or equivalent repo-consistent names. Rename exported/internal identifiers accordingly: AgentRunRuntime -> TaskRunRuntime or FizeauRunRuntime as appropriate; AgentRunner -> TaskExecutor/FizeauExecutor; ExecuteBead* -> Try/Attempt/Work terms. Keep `agent` only in literal external-agent concepts, historical event keys that cannot be migrated safely, or third-party standards names.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove the public `ddx agent` CLI in this bead unless it is already covered by ddx-d8474e0e. Do not change queue behavior, retry policy, review gates, or routing semantics. Do not implement Fizeau fuzzy matching. Do not rename durable tracker keys unless a migration plan is included in a separate bead.

PARENT AND DEPENDENCIES
Parent: ddx-f088a3fd. Depends on: ddx-424fdac4 and ddx-7065edd1.
title: Rename DDx execution internals away from agent vocabulary

acceptance:
1. Budget stops produced by --max-cost flow through the StopCondition Budget helper rather than being represented only as a synthetic executor failure report.
2. Reviewer group cost is charged to the same budget accumulator before close eligibility; TestStopCondition_BudgetAfterReviewCostPreventsClose covers an APPROVE review whose reviewer cost trips the cap and proves CloseWithEvidence is not called.
3. TestStopCondition_BudgetAfterImplementerCostStopsBeforeNextClaim covers implementer cost tripping the cap and proves the next bead is not claimed.
4. Existing TestExecuteLoopCostCap_ShortCircuitsAfterCap and TestExecuteLoopCostCap_LocalProvidersDoNotCount still pass.
5. The audit report .ddx/executions/<run-id>/cost-cap-consumers.md is committed and lists every rg hit for "cost cap", "max-cost", "review-cost-deferred", and "execution_failed:max-cost" under cli/, docs/, .agents/skills/, and .claude/skills/.
6. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestStopCondition_Budget|TestExecuteLoopCostCap|TestRunPostMergeReviewChargesReviewCost" -count=1 passes.
7. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
C9 requires budget/cost-cap stops to use the same durable stop policy as other drain exits. Today implementer cost and reviewer cost are accumulated in related but separate places, and the cost-cap stop is represented as a synthetic execution_failed report returned from the executor closure instead of a Budget StopCondition.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1659-1694 builds costCap, accumulateBilledCost, and costCapTripped as local closures in runAgentExecuteLoopImpl.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1789-1813 checks the cap inside the ExecuteBeadWorker executor closure and returns a fabricated ExecuteBeadReport when the cap is tripped.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:116-139 charges reviewer slot cost to ReviewCostCap and emits review-cost-deferred, but that path does not feed a first-class Budget stop decision before close eligibility.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:214-227 charges review cost immediately before the APPROVE close path, leaving no shared work-layer decision point between cost accumulation and CloseWithEvidence.

PROPOSED FIX
- Route max-cost decisions through the StopCondition Budget path introduced by ddx-89ab3fda.
- Keep local and subscription provider exclusion behavior from cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1663-1681.
- Charge implementer cost and reviewer group cost to the same budget accumulator before evaluating close eligibility.
- If reviewer cost trips the cap before close, record the existing review-cost-deferred evidence and leave the bead open instead of closing through APPROVE.
- Preserve the existing operator-facing max-cost message text unless tests intentionally update it.
- Commit a downstream-consumer audit at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/cost-cap-consumers.md listing cost-cap/maximum-cost event and detail-string consumers found with rg.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not alter no-progress accounting.
- Do not implement review repair cycles or MinPower repair escalation.
- Do not change Fizeau pricing/model catalog behavior.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-89ab3fda so Budget is a typed StopCondition before cost-cap behavior is rewired.

description:
PROBLEM
The background cleanup worker has separate unit coverage for periodic ticks and project-lock serialization, but no end-to-end regression that drives the worker deterministically with fake time and proves concurrent destructive passes do not happen. The current evidence lives in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go:203-351, while the production worker lives in cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_loop.go:25-180. That gap leaves room for a scheduling or locking regression that only appears when a long-lived process is polling cleanup in the background.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_loop.go:25-180 owns runExecutionCleanupPass, jitteredCleanupDelay, and startExecutionCleanupWorker. Existing tests verify pieces of that behavior, but they do not drive the worker end-to-end with fake time/ticks while asserting the cleanup lock prevents overlapping destructive passes.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add TestBackgroundCleanupEndToEnd_UsesLockAndJitter in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go or a sibling agent e2e test file.
- Use deterministic tick/fake-time control so the worker can be advanced without waiting on real time.
- Run two cleanup workers or overlapping passes against the same temp project and assert at most one destructive pass runs at a time.
- Verify the worker still performs cleanup on the scheduled cadence and emits the expected cleanup observations without touching real disk.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add new scheduling policy beyond the existing jitter/lock contract.
- Do not consume real bytes or inodes.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestBackgroundCleanupEndToEnd_UsesLockAndJitter exists and drives the cleanup worker with fake time or deterministic ticks.
2. The test proves concurrent destructive cleanup passes do not overlap while the lock is held.
3. The test proves periodic cleanup still fires on the expected cadence under the deterministic clock.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestBackgroundCleanupEndToEnd_UsesLockAndJitter" -count=1 passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: test: drive background cleanup with fake time and lock serialization

4. DDx-owned worker lifecycle events remain distinct from Fizeau inner progress events in tests and event names.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx worker and live-output paths still derive inner agent progress from Fizeau-adjacent session logs or harness-native records. After SD-011, canonical progress belongs to Fizeau ServiceEvents; DDx should consume those fields for display and keep DDx-owned worker lifecycle events separate.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/session_log_format.go:83-85 formats Fizeau-style session JSONL records into DDx transcript/progress lines.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:1655-1704 reads latest agent-*.jsonl logs and calls agent.FormatSessionLogLines for live worker output.
- cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:451-456 still writes Fizeau-style progress/session records from a direct parser.
- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:243-355 drains service events and reconstructs progress/tool-call entries rather than treating canonical progress as the source of truth.

PROPOSED FIX
Use Fizeau canonical progress ServiceEvent payloads for DDx worker/live output. DDx may format DDx-owned worker lifecycle events, but inner agent action/target/timing/output excerpts must come from canonical Fizeau progress fields when present. Historical fallback handling must be explicit and not used when canonical events exist.

NON-SCOPE
- Deleting the legacy formatter/tailer; ddx-f948b7a4 handles deletion after this bridge lands.
- Parsing Claude stream-json directly.
- Fizeau schema changes.

acceptance: 1. Execute-bead prompt requires verification_command OR explicit needs-investigation marker in any no_changes_rationale.txt. 2. Loop verifies the verification_command before closing as already_satisfied. 3. Implementation aligns exactly with TD-031 NoChangesContract (per ddx-673833f4) — uses LABELS not new statuses (e.g., 'triage:needs-investigation', 'triage:no-changes-unverified', 'triage:no-changes-unjustified'). **bd/br round-trip via ddx bead export must produce a bd-importable record after this bead lands.** 4. New events use canonical names from TD: no_changes_unverified, no_changes_unjustified, no_changes_verified. 5. Tests cover all paths: verified→close, unverified→stay-open+label, unjustified→stay-open+label, needs-investigation→stay-open+label. 6. Existing beads currently in 'closed via no_changes' state are not retroactively reopened. 7. Auto-triage (ddx-3c154349) consumes the triage:* labels per TriageContract. 8. **Verify schema_compat_test.go passes.**
description:
Today execute-bead's no_changes outcome conflates two cases:
1. Legitimate already_satisfied: the AC is currently met by prior commits; agent correctly says 'nothing to do'
2. Silent failure: agent didn't know how to do the work, gave up, wrote no_changes_rationale.txt; loop closes the bead anyway

Both result in the same artifact (no_changes_rationale.txt) and the same close. The second case is a serious failure mode masquerading as success.

**Constraint (per ADR-004)**: bd/br compatibility forbids adding new persisted status values. The bead's status stays in the canonical 6 (open/in_progress/closed/blocked/proposed/cancelled). DDx-specific execution semantics live in LABELS or EVENTS, NOT in new statuses.

Fix has two halves:

A) Execute-bead PROMPT change (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText / AgentText):
- When the agent decides 'no changes', it MUST include in the no_changes_rationale.txt one of:
  - verification_command: <shell command that proves the AC is currently met> AND its output showing pass
  - status: needs_investigation + reason: <why I cannot make progress>
- Bare 'no changes' without either is rejected by the loop

B) LOOP-side verification (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go):
- Parse no_changes_rationale.txt
- If verification_command present: run it; if exit 0, legitimate already_satisfied → close. If non-zero, fail to verify → bead STAYS OPEN with label 'triage:no-changes-unverified' added + emit no_changes_unverified event
- If status: needs_investigation: bead STAYS OPEN with label 'triage:needs-investigation' added (NOT a status change), event captures the agent's reason
- If neither present: reject — bead stays open with label 'triage:no-changes-unjustified' added + emit no_changes_unjustified event

Status NEVER changes to needs_investigation (that would violate bd/br compatibility). Auto-triage (ddx-3c154349) consumes the labels + events to drive its triage policy.

description:
PROBLEM
The parent Fizeau-boundary bead's search scope includes generated/bundled website docs under `.agents/skills/website` and `.claude/skills/website`. These pages still advertise retired `ddx agent` commands, quorum flags, and `ddx work` as an alias for `ddx agent execute-loop`, which conflicts with the no-compatibility-alias direction.

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
- .agents/skills/website/content/docs/cli/_index.md:53-57 still lists `ddx agent run`, quorum usage, list, usage, and capabilities examples.
- .agents/skills/website/content/docs/cli/commands/ddx_agent.md:6-77 still documents a `ddx agent` command tree and examples.
- .agents/skills/website/content/docs/cli/commands/ddx_work.md:13-21 describes `ddx work` as an alias for `ddx agent execute-loop` and tells readers to reach for execute-loop.
- .claude/skills/website/content/docs/skills.md:15-84 still documents a `/ddx-agent` guided dispatch skill and `ddx agent run --quorum` examples.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update generated/bundled website docs in both `.agents/skills/website` and `.claude/skills/website` so public workflow examples use `ddx run`, `ddx try`, and `ddx work` directly.
- Remove or clearly retire pages that present `ddx agent` as an active workflow namespace or compatibility alias.
- Preserve legitimate external-agent terminology only where it describes AI agents or historical docs with explicit retired-context wording.
- If these pages are generated from another source, update the source and regenerate the copies rather than patching only one output copy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change production CLI behavior in this bead.
- Do not edit core FEAT/ADR docs; sibling children own those surfaces.
- Do not remove examples of `--harness`, `--provider`, or `--model` when they are explicitly described as raw passthrough constraints.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-424fdac4. No deps; this is one decomposition child of the broad Fizeau-boundary ratification bead.

description:
PROBLEM
Candidate-cycle worktrees will live longer than today because implementation, checks, review, and repair can run in one attempt worktree. Cleanup and crash recovery need a hardening pass after the new pipeline exists so stale resources are reclaimed without deleting active review/repair state.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

PROPOSED FIX

- Recover or preserve latest pinned candidate after crash before cleanup removes the worktree.

description:
PROBLEM
The execute-bead loop body (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:875-1036) has 18 store-error exit paths — every bead outcome write can hard-exit the drain loop. This violates the loop's contract: a single bead's store failure should never kill the entire drain worker.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:875-1036 contains ~14 direct error returns triggered by store write failures, all routing through handleOutcomeStoreError. Each returns a hard error to Drain instead of scheduling a cooldown and continuing. The helper handleOutcomeStoreError itself is also a terminal-exit path. The 18 exit paths (14 store-error sites + handleOutcomeStoreError variants) are the dominant smell: 18 of 24 returns in the loop body are store-error paths.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add cli/internal/agent/work/commit.go defining commitOutcome(ctx, store, beadID, op func() error).
- commitOutcome calls op(); on error, calls SetExecutionCooldown with 'loop-error' ParkReason and returns nil (never hard-errors to Drain).
- Delete handleOutcomeStoreError.
- Replace all 14 callsites in execute_bead_loop.go (pre-refactor coordinates: 628, 740, 763, 800, 834, 850, 869, 877, 905, 918, 963, 979, 1014, 1026) with commitOutcome.

NON-SCOPE
- Logic changes to what's stored (only how store errors are handled).
- execute_bead_loop.go file rename (separate bead ddx-1d867ec1).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: store-write failure must not terminate the whole drain loop.
- P3: loop-error fallback should be observable as a structured degradation event.
- P4: a write failure on one bead must not affect later beads.

3. `lefthook run pre-push --commands full-tests` no longer fails with `go: -race requires cgo` on a host whose Go toolchain supports cgo.

description:
PROBLEM
The pre-push full-tests hook can fail before running tests because the Go race detector requires cgo, but the hook invokes `go test -race` without ensuring `CGO_ENABLED=1` or producing a clear skip when cgo is unavailable. This blocks otherwise valid tracker/agent work at push time with a host-environment error.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- lefthook.yml:545-550 defines the pre-push `full-tests` command as `go test -race -cover ./...` under `cli/`.
- Recent push attempts failed with `go: -race requires cgo; enable cgo by setting CGO_ENABLED=1` because the hook inherited an environment with cgo disabled.
- cli/internal/server/perf/perf_test.go:32-37 already accounts for race builds by disabling perf percentile budgets under `-race`, so the intended gate is to run the race build, not to fail before test startup.

PROPOSED FIX
- Make the pre-push full-tests hook explicitly set `CGO_ENABLED=1` before `go test -race -cover ./...` on platforms where the Go toolchain supports cgo.
- If cgo is genuinely unavailable, fail with an actionable message or skip only the race flag while still running a non-race full package gate. Pick one policy and document it in the hook/test so operators understand the behavior.
- Add a regression test that parses lefthook.yml and proves the full-tests command cannot regress to a bare `go test -race` without CGO handling.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not weaken the pre-commit hook or merge-policy hook.
- Do not change package-level race/perf test behavior except as required to make the hook invoke the intended gate.
- Do not make local pushes depend on network access.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-a9d130d0. No deps.

description:
PROBLEM
In the coordinator→client mutation path (Story 14/15), the originating peer identity must be forwarded from coordinator to spoke unchanged, and the spoke must authorize on both the origin AND the coordinator identity. Today cli/internal/federation/fanout.go supports only query fan-out with no identity forwarding; the mutation envelope from S15-7b carries headers but no production caller enforces dual authorization on the spoke side.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/federation/fanout.go: query fan-out exists but no requireTrustedForwarded middleware or augmentation of requireTrusted handles forwarded mutations.
- The spoke's requireTrusted (cli/internal/server/server.go) checks only the immediate transport peer — it does not parse X-DDx-Origin-Identity or X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity headers.
- No dual-authorization path exists: a forwarded mutation that passes coordinator trust but carries an untrusted origin is not rejected.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add requireTrustedForwarded middleware (or augment requireTrusted) in cli/internal/server/server.go:
  - Parse X-DDx-Origin-Identity and X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity from incoming request.
  - Validate origin identity against tailnet WhoIs / loopback policy.
  - Validate coordinator identity against registered federation peers.
  - Return 403 if either check fails or either header is missing.
- Coordinator forwarding path (built in S15-7b): when forwarding a prompt-driven mutation, set X-DDx-Origin-Identity = verified incoming peer identity (from WhoIs at coordinator edge); set X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity = hub's own attested identity. Identity MUST NOT be rewritten in transit.

NON-SCOPE
- Identity signing/attestation structure (that's ddx-e2c217d1).
- Mutation routing envelope (that's S15-7b / ddx-eb75a32d, a dep).
- Non-mutation endpoints.
title: S15-7c: Origin identity attestation forwarding + dual authorization (origin + coordinator)

description:
PROBLEM
No chaos or integration test suite validates federation behavior under failure conditions. Unit tests cover individual components but no test exercises multi-node failure scenarios (spoke crash, hub crash, network partition, version skew, duplicate node_id). Shipping federation without chaos coverage risks silent data loss or incorrect routing under common failure modes.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/federation/: unit tests cover fanout and registry separately but no test spins up multiple in-process httptest.Server instances to simulate a real federation topology.
- The test patterns from ddx-13e19750 / ddx-e59bff83 (closed tests) established the two-node httptest setup; this bead extends to chaos scenarios.
- ddx-0793ac75 (federated GraphQL resolvers, a dep) must land first as the chaos tests exercise the full resolver + routing stack.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add cli/internal/federation/chaos_test.go (or federation_integration_test.go):
  - Test fixture: spin up N in-process httptest.Server instances (hub + N spokes).
  - Chaos scenarios (each as a t.Run):
    1. Spoke crash: spoke goes offline mid-operation; hub fan-out returns partial results.
    2. Hub crash: spoke loses hub connection; spoke enters degraded mode.
    3. Network partition: simulated by closing httptest.Server; requests timeout gracefully.
    4. Version skew: spoke reports different SpecVersion; hub logs warning and continues.
    5. Duplicate node_id: second spoke with same node_id is rejected at registration.

NON-SCOPE
- Load testing or performance benchmarks.
- External network; httptest only.
title: federation: backend chaos/integration suite

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work` currently reports dependency-ready epics only as structural skips. That is correct for epics with open child work, but stale for epics whose direct children are all closed. Operators then have to manually notice and close completed epics, and the no-ready-work diagnostic keeps showing noise.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1416 appends every ordinary epic container to ReadyExecutionBreakdown.SkippedEpics.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1482 filters ordinary epic containers out of ReadyExecution entirely.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1696 defines isOrdinaryEpicContainer as true for any issue_type `epic`, without considering whether the epic has open children or only closed children.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1940-1942 renders all skipped epics with the same "epics are structural containers; decompose into tasks" message, so completed epics are not surfaced as closure candidates.

PROPOSED FIX
Teach the execution queue to distinguish epics with open children from epics whose direct children are all closed. Resolve the prior product fork conservatively: `ddx work` must not auto-close epics. It should surface completed epics as explicit closure candidates in human and JSON output so an operator can verify and close them intentionally with `ddx bead close`.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/bead/store.go
- cli/internal/bead/gaps_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go and/or cli/cmd/work_plan_test.go for operator-facing output

NON-SCOPE
- Do not execute epics through the normal bead executor. Epics remain structural containers.
- Do not auto-close epics from `ddx work`; closure remains an explicit operator action.
- Do not close epics that still have any direct child whose status is not closed.
- Do not implement recursive grandchild graph closure beyond the existing direct-child/dependency model unless a failing test proves it is required.
- Do not change task queue priority ordering for ordinary executable tasks.
notes: Unblocked 2026-05-08: resolved the human product fork. Completed epics must be surfaced as closure candidates; ddx work must not auto-close them. Worker can implement the conservative reporting path.

description:
PROBLEM
The cleanup manager can delete a registered execute-bead worktree that is still active when singleton run-state no longer points at it. This matches the May 7 vanished-worktree failures where the agent ran for many minutes and spent model budget, but post-execution git rev-parse failed because /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-... no longer existed.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:109-132 defaultExecutionCleanupLivenessProbe only preserves metadata matched by one runState or a local metadata liveness marker.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:253-269 preserves live candidates only after that one-probe result says live.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:271-313 removes registered worktrees when they are registered and not considered live.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:776-783 writes cleanup metadata without an active refreshed liveness marker, and :787-789 only defers removal for the owning process.

PROPOSED FIX
- Make cleanup enumerate all per-attempt live records from the dependency bead and pass the full live set to liveness decisions.
- Preserve registered worktrees when any live attempt record, live pid/session, or fresh heartbeat matches attempt_id or worktree_path.
- Reclaim registered worktrees only when the attempt is terminal/stale and heartbeat expiry/age policy says it is safe.
- Record cleanup observations that distinguish preserved_live_attempt from removed_stale_registered_worktree.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not broaden scratch-prefix cleanup; existing ddx-2df8e946 owns stale DDx scratch roots.
- Do not alter landing/evidence behavior.
- Do not delete published .ddx/executions or .ddx/runs evidence.

DEPS
Depends on per-attempt run-state/liveness and on ddx-2df8e946 for the broader DDx-owned scratch cleanup scope.

description:
PROBLEM
Execute-bead pre-dispatch checkpointing still mutates the parent checkout before the isolated worktree exists. On May 7 this surfaced as failures before execution: one checkpoint ran lefthook go-test with go test -race while cgo was unavailable, and another tripped the large-files hook on a DDx-generated .ddx/backups/*.jsonl artifact. These are parent-checkout hook/index failures, not bead implementation failures.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:740-768 holds the tracker lock across commitTrackerLocked, gitOps.SynthesizeCommit(projectRoot, ...), resolveBase, and WorktreeAdd.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:749-755 intentionally checkpoints caller dirt as a real commit before creating the isolated worktree.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:420-489 RealGitOps.SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes through the normal checkout/index path, which invokes normal git commit hooks.
- .gitignore:424 ignores .ddx/backups/, but staged or force-added DDx backup artifacts can still be present in the parent index and trip hooks.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace parent-checkout hook-running checkpoint commits with an internal temp-index/commit-tree checkpoint path or an equivalent hook-free internal commit path.
- Preserve the intended checkpoint semantics: caller dirt that should be part of the worker base remains reachable as a normal commit/ref without running project hooks.
- Explicitly exclude DDx runtime, evidence, backup, run-state, lock, and cleanup metadata paths from pre-dispatch checkpoint staging.
- Keep commitTrackerLocked behavior for legitimate tracker mutations.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not disable hooks for normal human or implementation commits.
- Do not change worker worktree commit behavior after the agent runs.
- Do not change cleanup/liveness behavior.

DEPS
Depends on incident containment so existing staged evidence deletions and dirty evidence artifacts are resolved before checkpoint behavior is changed.

description:
PROBLEM
Concurrent execute-bead attempts publish liveness through a singleton .ddx/run-state.json file. One active attempt can overwrite another attempt's run-state, and cleanup only sees the last writer as live. Worktree isolation cannot protect active worktrees if live-state tracking is global and single-valued.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/run_state.go:12-14 defines one RunStateFileName, run-state.json.
- cli/internal/agent/run_state.go:37-73 WriteRunState atomically overwrites that single file for every attempt.
- cli/internal/agent/run_state.go:93-100 ClearRunState removes that single file on any completing attempt.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:791-805 writes and clears the singleton state around each attempt.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:116-123 preserves candidates only when their metadata matches the one runState passed to the liveness probe.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add per-attempt run-state records, for example .ddx/run-state/<attempt-id>.json, as the source of truth for live attempts.
- Include bead_id, attempt_id, harness/model, started_at, worktree_path, pid, session_id when available, refreshed_at, and expires_at.
- Refresh attempt liveness while the agent runs and clear only that attempt's record on completion.
- Preserve .ddx/run-state.json as a compatibility aggregate or summary view until existing operator/HELIX consumers migrate.
- Update readers/tests so cleanup and status code can enumerate all live attempts.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement cleanup reclamation policy in this bead beyond making all live attempts visible.
- Do not change queue claim semantics.
- Do not migrate historical execution records.

DEPS
Depends on pre-dispatch checkpoint isolation so new liveness files are not accidentally committed by the parent checkpoint path.

description:

Successful execute-bead work can still collide with the operator checkout during land. The May 7 ddx-377ebf92 attempt completed successfully at result_rev be4fdf4dc1228e1bc25506952c35f41c9fb4dadc, but landing/push recovery tried to merge through a dirty projectRoot and failed because local tracked and untracked files would be overwritten. ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1262-1269 BuildLandRequestFromResult always sets WorktreeDir to projectRoot because the worker worktree has already been removed.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:619-633 landEvidence stages and commits the evidence directory in the same checkout used for landing.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:491-500 CommitStaged commits whatever is already staged, so unrelated operator index entries can be folded into a DDx evidence commit.

- Leave projectRoot available for object/ref operations but not as the staging or merge checkout.

NON-SCOPE

- Do not change cleanup/liveness behavior; sibling beads handle that.
- Do not retry or recover ddx-377ebf92 in this bead.

description:
PROBLEM
After a successful land, DDx attempts to refresh the operator checkout so the files match the new target branch tip. That sync currently mutates the main index and force-checks out changed paths. When the operator checkout has local edits in those same paths, syncing can overwrite or destabilize those edits even though the agent work happened in an isolated worktree.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:338-405 SyncWorkTreeToHead runs git read-tree HEAD and checkout-index -f for files changed between fromRev and HEAD.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:710, :775, :811, :1129, and :1193 call SyncWorkTreeToHead as best-effort after ref updates, merge, rollback, push auto-recovery, or preserved iteration recovery.
- The May 7 ddx-377ebf92 push-conflict event showed dirty projectRoot paths overlapped the landed branch paths, including .ddx/beads.jsonl, AGENTS.md, execute_bead_loop.go, and FEAT docs.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an overlap check before syncing the operator checkout: compare files changed by the landed/recovered commit range with dirty tracked and untracked projectRoot paths.
- If there is no dirty overlap, keep the existing sync behavior.
- If there is dirty overlap, skip checkout sync, leave refs/result/evidence intact, and record an observable checkout_sync_deferred detail/event rather than failing the bead.
- Ensure push auto-recovery and preserved-iteration recovery also use the guard.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change how clean temp landing worktrees merge/push; the dependency bead owns that.
- Do not auto-stash or discard operator changes.
- Do not change retry cooldown policy for real merge conflicts.

DEPS
Depends on clean landing/evidence isolation so sync is the only remaining projectRoot mutation after successful land.

description:
PROBLEM
When an attempt worktree vanishes before DDx can read HEAD, the result is reported as generic execution_failed/transport with ResultRev equal to BaseRev. That hides the root cause and makes the work look like an agent/task failure even when the agent ran to completion and infrastructure lost the worktree.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:940-978 reads gitOps.HeadRev(wtPath) after agent execution; on failure it writes a task_failed result with ResultRev set to baseRev and Reason set to the raw rev-parse error.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go:25-44 defines failure modes but has no worktree_lost class.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1901-1909 derives outcome reason from status/error text and can collapse missing-worktree infrastructure into generic transport/unknown handling.
- The May 7 ddx-c2a66f45 and ddx-b1cf1f6b attempts both failed with git rev-parse HEAD: chdir ... no such file or directory after non-trivial agent runtime/cost.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a stable FailureModeWorktreeLost and classify missing worktree/chdir rev-parse failures into it.
- Write result.json diagnostics that include attempted worktree_path, attempt_id, bead_id, cleanup metadata presence, run-state/liveness state, and whether the path was registered with git worktree.
- Ensure worktree_lost is infrastructure/transport, does not increment no-progress/no_changes budget, and leaves the bead retryable or parked according to existing transport policy.
- Preserve prompt/manifest/result evidence even when the worktree is gone.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not recover lost uncommitted implementation changes.
- Do not change cleanup deletion policy; sibling cleanup bead prevents the loss.
- Do not change model/provider routing.

DEPS
Depends on per-attempt liveness so diagnostics can include the live-state record that should have protected the worktree.
title: agent: classify vanished attempt worktrees as worktree_lost

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work` without flags produces `lint hook: dispatch-failure: ResolveRoute: no viable routing candidate for pins min_power=7 max_power=8: N candidates rejected` because the pre-dispatch lint hook inherits `rcfg.Profile()="default"`, and fizeau's "default" profile pins `min_power=7,max_power=8`. Operators whose providers fall outside that band — local providers (power<=6) or frontier cloud providers via openrouter (power=9) — see every candidate rejected and the agent exits in <1s with no_evidence_produced. The post-attempt triage hook has the same latent bug. Pre-claim intake already sidesteps this with `ClearProfile: true`. Repro: in this repo with no flags, `ddx work --once` will emit the dispatch-failure event on the next claimed bead — see `.ddx/executions/20260508T173611-c6069449/manifest.json` events for a captured instance.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/routing_profile.go:11` normalizes empty profile to `"default"`.
- `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:186-189` flows `rcfg.Profile()` into `ServiceExecuteRequest.Profile` when no `ProfileOverride` is set and `ClearProfile` is false.
- `cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:128-132` constructs the dispatched `AgentRunRuntime` without `ClearProfile` or `ProfileOverride`, so the lint hook's request carries `Profile="default"`.
- `cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go:114-118` has the same omission.
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:96-106` demonstrates the fix shape: `ClearProfile: true`.
- Downstream: fizeau's `internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml` pins the `default` profile to `min_power: 7, max_power: 8`; `service_routing.go` `routePowerPolicyForRequest` overlays that on every request whose `Profile` field is set.

PROPOSED FIX
- In `NewPreDispatchLintHook` (`cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:128-132`), set `ClearProfile: true` on the dispatched `AgentRunRuntime`. Lint is a mechanical scoring task; no profile constraint is needed.
- In `NewPostAttemptTriageHook` (`cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go:114-118`), set `ClearProfile: true` for the same reason.
- Do not change `executeOnService`, `NormalizeRoutingProfile`, or any caller that legitimately uses Profile="default" with explicit `--harness/--model` overrides (those bypass the profile bound via fizeau's `routePowerBoundsForRequest`).
- Do not touch `NewPreClaimIntakeHook`; it already does the right thing.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change `NormalizeRoutingProfile` or the meaning of "default" — execute-bead and `ddx run` callers still rely on it as the default request shape and supply their own `--harness`/`--model`/`--profile` to bypass the bound.
- Do not modify fizeau's catalog.
- Do not add a new global setting; the fix is local to the two hook constructors.

PARENT + DEPS
No parent. No deps.

description:
PROBLEM
Phase events (queueing, running, terminal, bead-result JSONL) are emitted from 4 separate sites in execute_bead_loop.go. Adding a new phase or changing event shape requires coordinated edits across all 4 sites, introducing subtle divergence. Today the JSONL-on-disk shape is only verified by manual inspection against a baseline fixture.

ROOT CAUSE
The 4 emission sites in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:
- :577 — queueing phase event
- :596 — running phase event
- :1076-1091 — terminal phase event (with Disposition/ParkReason)
- :1093 — bead.result JSONL write

Each site constructs and emits events independently with no shared Phase enum or emitPhase abstraction. The C0 fixture diff approach (byte-identical JSONL baseline) is the only correctness gate, but it is not automated.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add cli/internal/agent/work/phases.go defining Phase enum: Queueing | Running | Terminal.
- Add emitPhase(ctx, store, beadID string, phase Phase, outcome Outcome) error called by state transitions.
- Replace the 4 current emission sites with calls to emitPhase.
- Verify JSONL on-disk shape is byte-identical to the C0 baseline (automated fixture diff in test).

NON-SCOPE
- Changing the JSONL event format or adding new event types.
- Renaming execute_bead_loop.go (separate bead ddx-1d867ec1).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: terminal phase emission should fail open rather than silently diverge across call sites.
- P3: the emitted phase stream must stay observable and structured.
- P4: a phase-event bug on one bead must not affect other beads.
- P5: the phase stream supports operator-visible state.
title: C12: single phase-event emission (replaces 4 emit sites with state-machine-driven emitPhase)

acceptance: 1. 429 errors from Fizeau trigger per-bead wait + retry per TD-031 RateLimitRetryContract (per ddx-673833f4). 2. **Per RateLimitRetryContract: NO persisted status change before budget exhaustion; provider stays in rotation. bd/br compatibility (ADR-004) preserved.** 3. Retry-After honored when present; exponential backoff fallback when absent. 4. --rate-limit-max-wait flag bounds per-bead total wait (default 5 min). 5. Beyond budget: bead transitions per TD's outcome→state mapping for 'rate-limited beyond budget' — status stays in canonical 6, additional context conveyed via labels/events. 6. RecordRouteAttempt invoked for transparency per RateLimitRetryContract. 7. Tests cover Retry-After-respected and exponential-backoff paths. 8. Quota-exhaustion path is explicitly NOT this bead's concern (per QuotaPauseContract separation in TD). 9. **schema_compat_test.go round-trip passes after this bead lands.**
description:
Distinct from quota exhaustion (see ddx-aede917d). Rate-limiting (HTTP 429 with Retry-After header — per-second/per-minute throttle) means 'slow down', not 'I'm out for the period'. Provider stays in rotation; current bead retries after waiting.

Per-bead retry policy:
1. On 429 / rate-limit error from Fizeau (NOT NoViableProviderForNow which is quota): wait the Retry-After interval (capped at 60s default, configurable via --rate-limit-max-wait), retry the SAME bead with the SAME provider
2. If no Retry-After header: exponential backoff (1s, 5s, 15s, 30s, 60s) up to the cap
3. Per-bead total rate-limit wait budget (default 5 min); past that, mark execution_failed with reason 'rate-limited beyond budget'
4. Provider availability state unchanged (stays in rotation; this is per-request throttle, not provider-out)

Should use Fizeau's existing RecordRouteAttempt feedback channel for transparency (records the rate-limit event so the routing engine has signal even though it doesn't change provider availability).

description:
CONTEXT
This bead creates the governing feature spec for DDx Prose Quality Support. The desired user outcome is not AI-detection evasion; it is clear, specific, voice-preserving prose in DDx governing artifacts and user-facing docs. The feature must define how DDx targets common AI-writing tropes with deterministic, explainable findings.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md
- docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md only if needed to add a short feature reference

REQUIRED SPEC CONTENT
- Problem: vague/generic prose weakens document-driven development.
- Primary users: DDx maintainers, project maintainers, and agents writing/reviewing docs.
- Content modes: technical, planning, and public prose.
- Product surfaces: default skill, deterministic checker/rules, ddx doc prose --changed, later review integration.
- Non-goals: no AI detector, no detector bypass, no blocking by default, no rewriting authorial voice away.
- Measurable acceptance criteria using structural findings: file, line, rule id, severity, rationale, suggested edit; project vocabulary; advisory default.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Implementing the checker.
- Choosing the final tool boundary beyond naming deterministic prose checks.
- Adding CLI flags or plugin assets.
title: Define FEAT-027 Prose Quality Support

3. TestPreCloseReview_UnanimousApproveCloses verifies close occurs only when both reviewers APPROVE and each approval includes per-AC evidence.
4. TestPreCloseReview_ApproveWithoutEvidenceIsReviewError verifies an evidence-free APPROVE is classified as review_error: unparseable and does not close the bead.
5. TestPreCloseReview_RequestChangesBlocksCloseAndSchedulesRepair verifies REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK with fixable implementation findings records review-fixable-gap, leaves the bead unclosed, and starts the next implementation cycle with repair_context_from_review_group.
6. TestPreCloseReview_TerminalClassificationsBlockOperator verifies review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large at depth cap, review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope, and exhausted review_error block with needs_human and do not schedule unbounded retries.
7. TestPreCloseReview_ReviewErrorRetryBudgetIsPerReviewerSlot verifies review errors retry by result_rev + reviewer_index up to review_max_retries, then emit review-manual-required and block without close.
8. TestPreCloseReview_NoClosedBeadReopen verifies REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK never closes and reopens a bead; the failed review prevents close in the first place.

description:
PROBLEM
ADR-024 and FEAT-010 now require adversarial review to be the default pre-close gate, but current loop/review code still models single post-merge review and reopen behavior. A successful implementation can be closed even though the review path is not modeled as a two-reviewer unanimous pre-close gate with repair-cycle traceability.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:226 documents ReviewVerdict as post-merge review.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:300 documents Reopen as the REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK path after a bead was closed.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:474 builds one reviewer request, without review_group_id or reviewer_index correlation metadata.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:528 runs a single DefaultBeadReviewer.ReviewBead invocation and parses one verdict.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:242 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:279 assert REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK reopen behavior instead of pre-close blocking behavior.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a pre-close review coordinator used by execute-loop after a close-eligible implementation result and before CloseWithEvidence.
- Default behavior runs two no-tool reviewer invocations against the same bounded evidence bundle. Each invocation carries role=reviewer plus bead_id, attempt_id, result_rev, review_group_id, reviewer_index, and implementer route facts.
- Close only when both reviewers return APPROVE with per-AC evidence. Treat APPROVE without required evidence as review_error: unparseable.
- Any evidenced REQUEST_CHANGES or BLOCK prevents close and classifies the result into review_fixable_gap, review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large, review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope, or review_error.
- review_fixable_gap schedules a bounded repair cycle with repair_context_from_review_group. Terminal classifications block the bead with needs_human or decomposition metadata as specified by TD-031.
- Review transport/provider/parse errors retry per result_rev + reviewer_index up to review_max_retries; exhaustion emits review-manual-required and blocks without closing.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL review-session UI mutations.
- Manual human conversation review sessions.
- Changing the evidence assembler beyond adding the review-group fields required by this gate.

description:
PROBLEM
execute_bead_loop.go:875-1036 handles no_changes adjudication, decomposition, push-failed, and push-conflict inline in the loop body. These are distinct outcome behaviors that belong in try.Attempt so the loop only sees Disposition + Cooldown + ParkReason + RecordEvents.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:875-1036: the block that follows a bead attempt contains outcome-specific branches (no_changes, decomposition, push_failed, push_conflict) as raw loop logic rather than Disposition values from try.Attempt.
- ddx-b24e9630 (no_changes verification): adjudicateNoChangesContract function exists in the codebase — confirm it is called from try.Attempt's no_changes branch (not merely defined). If wired: preserve. If unwired: this child must wire it.
- ddx-c6e3db02 (rate-limit retry): EvaluateRetryPolicy (or similar) must be called from try.Attempt's rate-limit detection path — confirm actual wiring before this refactor moves it.
- try/attempt.go:67 is the candidate location for the relocated no_changes adjudication logic.
- The dead-code audit (ddx-09d2990c) must close before this child to establish which functions are genuinely wired vs. only defined.

PROPOSED FIX
- Remove lines 875-1036 from execute_bead_loop.go; replace with: read Disposition + Cooldown + ParkReason + RecordEvents from try.Attempt result.
- Move no_changes adjudication (verification_command runner + triage label logic) into try.Attempt's no_changes branch near try/attempt.go:67.
- Move rate-limit retry (Retry-After honor + exponential backoff + budget cap) into try.Attempt's rate-limit detection.
- Status NEVER changes to needs_investigation on no_changes; stays in canonical state 6; uses triage:no-changes-unverified label per ADR-004.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not modify the Drain loop contract beyond replacing lines 875-1036 with Disposition/Cooldown reads.
- ddx-09d2990c wire-or-delete must close first (hard dep).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: no_changes, decomposition, push-failed, and push-conflict must fail open instead of wedging the loop.
- P2: try.Attempt owns the outcome classification; the drain loop should not own per-outcome policy.
- P3: emit structured events so operators can see the exact degradation instead of a silent retry loop.
- P4: the handling must stay per-bead.
- P6: auto-retry should remain limited to transient classes only.

acceptance:
1. Child beads for FEAT, TD, fixtures, skill, rules, CLI, docs, and review follow-up are closed or explicitly superseded.
2. The shipped docs and CLI help use the canonical feature name Prose Quality Support and do not market detector bypass.
3. The first executable surface is advisory and can be run against changed Markdown files without blocking unrelated DDx workflows.
description:
PROBLEM
DDx is document-driven: PRDs, FEAT specs, technical designs, ADRs, bead descriptions, review prompts, and close notes are governing artifacts. When those documents drift into generic AI-like prose, vague importance language, or terminology inconsistency, agents receive weaker execution prompts and reviewers lose checkable design intent.

GOAL
Add DDx Prose Quality Support: DDx should help users and agents write clear, specific, voice-preserving project prose and flag common AI-writing tropes through advisory guidance plus deterministic checks. This is not an AI detector and must not be framed as detector bypass.

PROPOSED SLICES
1. Define the FEAT product contract for prose quality support.
2. Define the technical design for a deterministic prose quality pipeline.
3. Add fixtures/golden expected findings to keep false positives under control.
4. Ship a default-plugin human-writing-support skill.
5. Add default rules and DDx vocabulary.
6. Add ddx doc prose --changed as the first advisory CLI surface.
7. Document configuration and non-goals.
8. Later, integrate prose findings into bead review after noise is known.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement AI-authorship detection. Do not make prose findings blocking by default. Do not wire this into ddx work or bead review until the advisory doc command has landed and has fixture coverage.
title: EPIC: Add DDx Prose Quality Support

3. Report consolidates causes into bead readiness, system readiness, post-attempt, close-policy, and unknown buckets.
4. Each bucket states whether bead-lifecycle readiness can check it before claim.
5. Report identifies follow-up spec/code surfaces for each bucket.
6. Documentation-only waiver is stated in closing notes; no new Go test is required unless the report adds generated references.

description:
PROBLEM
Recent `ddx work` attempts failed repeatedly, but the causes are mixed together in tracker events and execution bundles. Operators cannot tell which failures are bead-readiness defects versus provider, host resource, git, review, close-policy, or unknown failures.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `.ddx/beads.jsonl` stores execute-bead, bead-quality, routing, review, and cost events in the same per-bead event streams; recent events include provider exhaustion, review transport, ENOSPC, git index lock, no_changes, and tests_red-like checkpoint failures without a durable cross-attempt report.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:670` logs pre-claim intake failures inline and continues claiming, leaving the analysis spread across terminal output and bead events.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:732-751` logs pre-dispatch lint failures separately from intake, so one operator-visible failure taxonomy is not available.

PROPOSED FIX
Create a durable report under `docs/helix/06-iterate/` from recent `.ddx/beads.jsonl` events and `.ddx/executions/*` bundles. The report must group failed beads by estimated cause, include examples, state whether each cause is checkable by bead readiness before claim, and identify the owning follow-up surface.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change specs, skills, Go code, tracker state beyond this bead, or live worker behavior.

acceptance:
1. Files `cli/internal/agent/model_catalog_yaml.go`, `cli/internal/agent/catalog.go`, `cli/internal/agent/agent_catalog_shared.go`, and `cli/internal/server/agent_catalog.go` no longer exist.
2. `rg -n "BuiltinCatalog|ResolveModelTier|LoadModelCatalogYAML|DefaultModelCatalogYAML|DefaultModelCatalogPath|ApplyModelCatalogYAML|ApplyCatalogFromService|CatalogEntry|ModelCatalogYAML|harnessToSurface" cli/` returns zero hits in non-deleted production source (matches in deleted-file diffs and routinglint testdata are expected).
3. `rg -n "GET /api/agent/catalog|/projects/\{project\}/agent/catalog|ddx_agent_catalog" cli/internal/server/` returns zero hits.
4. `cli/internal/agent/runner.go` no longer declares a `Catalog` field on `Runner`, and `NewRunner` constructs without invoking any catalog loader.
5. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_group.go` resolve the reviewer through `ModelRefOverride: "smart"` rather than `ResolveModelTier`. New test `TestReviewerDispatch_UsesModelRefSmartNotResolveModelTier` in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go` captures the dispatched `ServiceExecuteRequest` and asserts `req.ModelRef == "smart"` and `req.Model == ""`.
6. `cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go` forbids the symbols listed in AC 2; new test `TestRoutinglint_ForbidsRetiredCatalogIdentifiers` in `cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer_test.go` (or the existing `analyzer_test.go`) verifies each forbidden identifier triggers a diagnostic in the `violations` testdata.
7. New test `TestDoctor_WarnsOnLegacyModelCatalogFile` in `cli/cmd/doctor_test.go` (or the existing doctor test file) verifies that when `~/.ddx/model-catalog.yaml` exists in a fake home dir, `ddx doctor` output includes a warning naming the file path and pointing at fizeau's location.
8. New test `TestRunner_ConstructsWithoutCatalog` in `cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go` constructs a `NewRunner` and verifies it does not access `~/.ddx/model-catalog.yaml` (use a stub home dir; the file must not be opened).
9. `cli/internal/agent/models.go` `BenchmarkArm.ResolveArm` either is deleted or becomes a no-op; `BenchmarkArm` callers either supply an explicit `Model` or pass `ModelRef`. Existing benchmark tests pass without any tier→model resolution.
10. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... ./internal/server/... ./tools/lint/routinglint/... -run "TestReviewerDispatch_UsesModelRefSmartNotResolveModelTier|TestRoutinglint_ForbidsRetiredCatalogIdentifiers|TestDoctor_WarnsOnLegacyModelCatalogFile|TestRunner_ConstructsWithoutCatalog|TestBenchmark|TestReview" -count=1` passes.
11. `cd cli && go test ./...` green.
12. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
DDx maintains a parallel model catalog: an on-disk override at `~/.ddx/model-catalog.yaml`, a built-in seed catalog (`DefaultModelCatalogYAML`), tier→surface→model mappings (`smart→opus`, `standard→sonnet`, `cheap→haiku`), and HTTP/MCP endpoints that serve it. Fizeau already owns the authoritative model catalog (`~/.config/fizeau/models.yaml` + embedded yaml), exposes models via `svc.ListModels()`, and resolves tier→model via `ModelRef` ("smart"/"standard"/"cheap"). Every tier→model lookup DDx does is duplicate state that drifts from fizeau's truth, violates the routing-boundary contract (CONTRACT-003 / ADR-024 — "DDx forwards opaque pins/power bounds; fizeau owns model selection"), and is the same class of violation already retired by ddx-3bd7396a (`ResolveTierModelRef`). The catalog file at `~/.ddx/model-catalog.yaml` is shadow state that this bead deletes outright; operators with the file see a `ddx doctor` warning pointing at fizeau's location.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/model_catalog_yaml.go` (entire file, 143 lines) — defines `ModelCatalogYAML` schema, `DefaultModelCatalogPath() = ~/.ddx/model-catalog.yaml`, `LoadModelCatalogYAML`, `ApplyModelCatalogYAML`, and the hardcoded seed `DefaultModelCatalogYAML()` with smart/standard/cheap tier→surface→model mappings.
- `cli/internal/agent/catalog.go` (entire file, 207 lines) — defines `Catalog`, `CatalogEntry`, `BuiltinCatalog`, `NewCatalog`, `Resolve(ref, surface)`. Pure DDx routing intelligence.
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_catalog_shared.go` (entire file, 60 lines) — `ApplyCatalogFromService` overlays fizeau's catalog onto DDx's; only exists because DDx insists on holding its own copy.
- `cli/internal/agent/models.go:7-26` — `harnessToSurface` map and `ResolveModelTier(harness, tier)` that wraps `BuiltinCatalog.Resolve`. Tier→model resolution that fizeau owns.
- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:32` — `Runner.Catalog *Catalog` field.
- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:54-66` — `NewRunner` overlays built-in seed + fizeau service catalog + `~/.ddx/model-catalog.yaml` on every Runner construction.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:806` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_group.go:53` — `ResolveModelTier(reviewHarness, SelectReviewerTier(escalation.TierSmart))` resolves a concrete reviewer model client-side instead of asking fizeau.
- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1230-1236` — reads `~/.ddx/model-catalog.yaml` for an `agent` subcommand.
- `cli/internal/server/agent_catalog.go` (entire file, 387 lines) — `handleAgentCatalog` serves the on-disk catalog over HTTP; tier/surface response shapes; same loader paths.
- `cli/internal/server/server.go:853,856,3376,3748-3749` — `GET /api/agent/catalog`, `GET /api/projects/{project}/agent/catalog`, MCP tool `ddx_agent_catalog`.
- `cli/internal/agent/models.go:36-41` `BenchmarkArm.ResolveArm` resolves Model from Tier via `ResolveModelTier`; benchmarks must instead require an explicit Model or pass `ModelRef` through to fizeau.
- Lint anchor: `cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go:75` already forbids `ResolveTierModelRef` per ddx-3bd7396a; that rule needs siblings for `BuiltinCatalog`, `ResolveModelTier`, and the catalog loader symbols so future code cannot reintroduce the violation.

PROPOSED FIX
Aggressive deletion. Do not refactor or reshape; remove.

1. Delete `cli/internal/agent/model_catalog_yaml.go` entirely.
2. Delete `cli/internal/agent/catalog.go` entirely.
3. Delete `cli/internal/agent/agent_catalog_shared.go` entirely.
4. Delete `cli/internal/server/agent_catalog.go` entirely. Remove `GET /api/agent/catalog`, `GET /api/projects/{project}/agent/catalog`, and the MCP `ddx_agent_catalog` tool registration from `cli/internal/server/server.go:853,856,3376,3748-3749`. Frontend/operator consumers that need a catalog view should call fizeau directly (or a thin proxy added in a follow-up if a real consumer surfaces).
5. In `cli/internal/agent/models.go`, delete `harnessToSurface` and `ResolveModelTier`. Keep `BenchmarkArm` struct but change `BenchmarkArm.Model` to be required: `ResolveArm()` becomes a no-op or is deleted; benchmark callers must supply an explicit Model or `ModelRef`. (Audit benchmark callers — likely under `cli/cmd/`, possibly `cli/internal/agent/compare_*.go`.)
6. In `cli/internal/agent/runner.go`, drop the `Catalog *Catalog` field (line 32) and the catalog-construction block (lines 54-66) from `NewRunner`. Drop the `r.catalog()` accessor and any consumers; `BuiltinCatalog` is gone, so leftover references will fail to compile and force callsites to be cleaned in this bead.
7. In `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:806` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_group.go:53`, replace `ResolveModelTier(reviewHarness, SelectReviewerTier(escalation.TierSmart))` with passing `ModelRefOverride: "smart"` to `dispatchViaResolvedConfig` and leaving `Model` empty. Fizeau resolves smart→concrete model. Update the surrounding routing-fact emission to drop the locally-resolved model name; record only what fizeau returns in `final.RoutingActual`.
8. In `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1230-1236`, delete the catalog-loading block and any subcommand it gated. If the subcommand is purely a catalog viewer, delete the subcommand outright.
9. Extend `cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go` forbidden-identifier list with: `BuiltinCatalog`, `ResolveModelTier`, `LoadModelCatalogYAML`, `DefaultModelCatalogYAML`, `DefaultModelCatalogPath`, `ApplyModelCatalogYAML`, `ApplyCatalogFromService`, `CatalogEntry`, `ModelCatalogYAML`. Each entry cites this bead and the routing-boundary rationale.

- Do not delete `escalation.ModelTier` — the type is still used as a request-time hint passed via ModelRef.

PARENT + DEPS
No parent. No deps. Sibling/successor to ddx-3bd7396a (which retired `ResolveTierModelRef`). Independent of ddx-c54b817c and ddx-ea91688b.
