description:
PROBLEM
GraphQL worker dispatch has the same parallel-struct smell as REST, so fixing only the REST handler would leave another transport able to drop future execute-loop fields.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-75 defines a GraphQL-local request struct and cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127-141 manually constructs ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec. It also parses worker-loop mode and idle interval separately from REST, bypassing a shared spec lifecycle and risking a stale public alias.

PROPOSED FIX
- Decode rawArgs directly into executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec.
- Preserve workers.default_spec behavior from cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:83-104 by applying defaults onto the unified spec before server-side ApplyDefaults/Validate.
- Route GraphQL through the same server-side helper used by REST so ApplyDefaults, Validate, SpecVersion handling, ProjectRoot assignment, Mode/IdleInterval semantics, and StartExecuteLoop are not reimplemented.
- Delete the GraphQL-local request struct and the parallel StartExecuteLoop literal.
- Do not accept or emit the removed polling flag alias on the GraphQL worker-dispatch path.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change GraphQL schema or worker kind names beyond the rawArgs decoded into the shared spec.
- Do not change workers.max_count semantics.
- Do not change REST handler behavior; that belongs to the REST child.
notes: Removed the stale GraphQL poll field requirement. GraphQL now follows the same Mode plus IdleInterval contract as REST/server worker dispatch.
title: server: route GraphQL worker dispatch through ExecuteLoopSpec

acceptance:
1. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md explicitly lists the DDx-owned scratch prefixes and `cleanup.json` metadata rule named in this bead.
2. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md defines the default minimum age rule: expired/terminal metadata-backed paths may be removed, and metadata-less recognized-prefix scratch roots require mtime age >= 6 hours plus no live PID/session evidence.
3. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md defines the soft cleanup trigger as below 512 MiB free bytes or below 8192 free inodes before claim, and preserves the current hard stop floor of 64 MiB and 1024 inodes.
4. docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md mirrors the same prefix, age, soft-trigger, and hard-floor values in its roots/ownership and resource-preflight sections.
5. docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md P8 references the explicit FEAT-010/SD-025 cleanup policy so future worker beads do not treat leaked DDx test/e2e scratch roots as out of scope.
6. The bead no longer carries a `needs_human` label after those values are recorded.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The cleanup/resource spec bead was blocked because the governing docs named cleanup scope in broad terms but did not give implementers exact policy values for soft resource pressure, minimum scratch age, and recognized DDx-owned scratch prefixes. Without those choices, cleanup implementation beads can either under-clean leaked DDx roots or overreach into non-DDx temp directories.

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:585-650 defines execution resource cleanup, but lines 605-608 still say `soft high-water threshold` and `hard floor` without concrete values.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:618-623 includes known DDx prefixes and minimum age/mtime rules, but it does not list the prefixes or the default age.
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:224-229 and 277-288 repeat the same policy without values.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_resources.go:13-16 currently defines only a last-resort hard floor of 64 MiB free bytes and 1024 free inodes.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:16-18 defines `cleanup.json` as the ownership metadata file, and cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:212-220 currently scans only entries whose names start with `ExecuteBeadWtPrefix`.

PROPOSED FIX
Update FEAT-010, SD-025, and P8 with explicit policy constants and anchors:
- Recognized DDx-owned scratch prefixes: `ddx-exec-wt`, `ddx-claim-heartbeats`, `ddx-metric-keepalive`, `ddx-test-`, `ddx-e2e-`, and any temp root containing DDx `cleanup.json` metadata with matching project ownership.
- Minimum age: metadata-backed paths may be removed when liveness is expired or the owning attempt is terminal; metadata-less recognized-prefix scratch roots require mtime age >= 6 hours and no live PID/session evidence.
- Hard floor: preserve the current hard stop floor of 64 MiB free bytes and 1024 free inodes until a config override bead changes it.
- Soft high-water cleanup trigger: run cleanup before claim when any checked temp/evidence root is below 512 MiB free bytes or below 8192 free inodes, then re-check before claiming.
- Insertion anchors: FEAT-010 `### Execution Resource Cleanup` after lines 600-608 and 618-623; SD-025 `### Roots and ownership` lines 224-229 and `### Resource preflight and loop-fatal handling` lines 285-288; reliability P8 lines 47-56.
- Remove the `needs_human` blocker once those explicit policy choices are documented.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement cleanup code changes in this spec bead.
- Do not permit deletion of arbitrary non-DDx temp directories.
- Do not change provider quota, routing, review policy, or candidate-cycle behavior.
- Do not delete complete `.ddx/runs/<id>`, complete `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>`, preserved worktrees, active registered worktrees, or `refs/ddx/iterations/...`.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-f3dfde0d. No deps.

description:
PROBLEM
The DDx prose checker needs Vale-backed rules for unsupported claims, AI-slop polish, filler, missing actor/action, repeated openings, token cost, and vocabulary. Without tuned rules, `ddx doc prose` cannot improve documentation clarity or token cost reliably.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:85-107 defines the initial rule pack and implementation notes, but the Vale style files and corpus golden cases are not implemented.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Rule semantics are high-judgment work. The bead must balance catching common model-generated prose against preserving precise technical text, commands, IDs, tables, and legitimate density.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add Vale style files for the initial DDx rule families.
- Add corpus golden cases for unsupported claims, AI slop, filler transitions, missing actor/action, repeated openings, token-cost candidates, and vocabulary substitution.
- Use Vale scopes/token ignores for Markdown structure.
- Keep raw Vale messages short and leave DDx rationale/suggested edits to normalization.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not switch `ddx doc prose` to Vale yet.
- Do not wire workflow hooks.
- Do not make findings blocking.

DEPS
Depends on the corpus harness so rule behavior is measured against positives and negatives.
title: docprose: port initial rules to Vale styles

TestReadyExecutionFilters, TestReadyExecutionBreakdown_SeparatesCooldownFromNonExecutable, TestBlockedAllClassifiesDepAndRetryCooldown, TestBlockedAllOmitsExpiredCooldown, TestBlockedAllPrefersDependencyOverCooldown, and TestProperty_ReadyAndBlockedInvariants are updated for the new derived buckets.

description:
PROBLEM
The core queue queries still implement lifecycle policy with local conditionals: ready filtering excludes lifecycle labels, blocked means unsatisfied dependencies, status counts only cover a subset of statuses, and needs-human has a dedicated query lane. That directly conflicts with the new status-owned lifecycle contract.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1346-1355 exposes Ready and ReadyExecution separately from the new lifecycle contract.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1358-1377 implements NeedsHuman as open plus LabelNeedsHuman.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1379-1452 has ReadyExecutionBreakdown fields for SkippedNeedsInvestigation and filters LabelNeedsInvestigation/LabelNeedsHuman/no-changes triage labels.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1548-1573 defines Blocked as open beads with unclosed deps.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1575-1719 classifies dependency waits, status=blocked, needs_human, needs_investigation, cooldowns, ineligible, superseded, and epics in one store-specific function.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1804-1867 counts Open/Closed/Ready/Blocked/NeedsHuman but not the six lifecycle statuses.

PROPOSED FIX
Refactor store ready, execution-ready, dependency-waiting, externally-blocked, operator-attention, and status-summary queries to call the pure lifecycle state machine. Ready/work eligibility must be status=open with satisfied dependencies, no active claim, and no cooldown/ineligible/superseded/epic-exclusion facts. Dependency waiting must remain status=open and appear as a derived waiting bucket, not status=blocked. status=blocked must represent external recheckable blockers only. status=proposed replaces NeedsHuman for operator-attention queries. closed satisfies dependents; cancelled does not.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change agent parking paths or API/UI names in this bead except where store tests require updated helper names. Agent/API/UI integration beads consume the new store behavior.

DEPS
Depends on the pure lifecycle core and storage status preservation.
title: bead: route ready and blocked queries through lifecycle core

description:
PROBLEM
The escalation ladder (escalation.Ladder.Next) was added in S10_2 but has no production caller in execute_bead_loop.go. Each bead currently gets exactly one singleTierAttempt without any rung escalation on substantive failure. The existing tier→profile mutation in the loop is an ad-hoc workaround that should be replaced by the ladder.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: singleTierAttempt is called exactly once per bead iteration; there is no loop over ladder rungs.
- cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go:30: Ladder.Next is defined but has no call site in execute_bead_loop.go or any other production file (confirmed by dead-code audit ddx-09d2990c).
- The existing tier→profile mutation (a manual MinPower bump) in the executor closure is the only escalation behavior; it is not driven by the ladder contract.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace the single singleTierAttempt call in execute_bead_loop.go with a real loop that:
  1. Calls singleTierAttempt with current rung's MinPower.
  2. On substantive failure (non-park, non-budget-exceeded), calls ladder.Next to get next rung.
  3. Terminates when ladder.Next returns no further rungs or a stop condition triggers.
- Remove the existing tier→profile mutation; ladder.Next drives all MinPower escalation.
- Pass Role='implementer' and CorrelationID='<bead_id>:<attempt_id>' on every ExecuteRequest.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing the ladder definition or rung configuration (S10_2 owns that).
- Cost-cap relocation (belongs to C9 / ddx-9228a484).

acceptance:
1. PreDispatchLintHook fires after RoutePreflight success and before Store.Claim in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go; if RoutePreflight rejects, lint is not invoked for that candidate.
2. TestLoop_LintHook_FiresPreClaim proves hook execution happens before claim by observing hook/claim ordering.
3. TestLintHook_LowScore_WarnDoesNotBlock proves a low score appends a bead-quality.lint event and the loop still claims/dispatches by default.
4. TestLintHook_BlockMode_RefusesDispatchOnLowScore proves bead-quality.lint.block_threshold_score blocks only when the hook returns a valid score below threshold, with no Store.Claim call for that bead.
5. TestLintHook_SkillMissing_ProceedsWithWarning, TestLintHook_BadJSON_ProceedsWithWarning, and TestLintHook_Timeout_ProceedsWithWarning prove hook infrastructure failures append warnings and proceed with claim.
6. WIRED-IN: git grep PreDispatchLintHook cli/internal/agent shows the execute-loop invocation site, not just the runtime field declaration.

8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  ddx try must invoke bead-quality lint after selecting a candidate and before claiming it, but the execute-bead loop currently has no lint hook invocation or warn/block policy at that point.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:640-714 runs PreClaimHook and RoutePreflight before Store.Claim. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:668-710 is the current pre-claim RoutePreflight gate; cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:714 calls Store.Claim. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:29 now exposes ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PreDispatchLintHook from closed contract bead ddx-c8dc1146, but no loop path invokes it and no path emits bead-quality.lint events.

PROPOSED FIX
  - In cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go, invoke runtime.PreDispatchLintHook(ctx, candidate.ID) after RoutePreflight succeeds and before Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee).
  - Default behavior is WARN-ONLY: append a bead event kind bead-quality.lint containing score, rationale, suggested_fixes, waivers_applied, and any warning details, then continue to claim regardless of score.
  - Add opt-in BLOCK behavior from resolved config key bead-quality.lint.block_threshold_score. Only a valid hook response with Score below threshold refuses dispatch; infrastructure errors fail open.
  - On block, emit operator-readable log/loop event text citing bead-lifecycle SKILL.md guidance and skip claiming that bead. The skipped bead must be marked attempted for this Run so the loop moves on instead of spinning on the same candidate.
  - Treat hook errors, missing skill/harness, bad JSON surfaced through the hook, and timeouts as warning events and proceed with claim.

NON-SCOPE
  - Defining hook/result types; closed in ddx-c8dc1146.
  - Implementing the runner-backed lint hook body; covered by ddx-68706ef9.
  - Post-attempt triage and OutcomeReason handling; covered by ddx-4375292b.
  - ddx work integration.

PARENT
  ddx-e1a576a7.

DEPS
  No deps. ddx-c8dc1146 is closed and its hook contract is available in cli/internal/agent/quality_hooks.go and ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.
title: agent: invoke pre-dispatch lint hook before claim

description:
PROBLEM
ADR-024 requires default adversarial pre-close review to use two independent reviewer invocations, but the candidate-cycle refactor intentionally starts with transitional single-slot review to stabilize the pipeline. Once single-slot review/repair is correct, default two-slot candidate review must replace the transitional path.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:283-305 wraps a plain BeadReviewer into a one-slot ReviewGroupResult when the reviewer does not implement reviewGroupReviewer.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:307-352 reduces review groups conservatively, but default production dispatch does not yet guarantee two independent slots.
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md:109-145 requires two independent reviewer invocations and unanimous evidence-backed APPROVE to permit close.

PROPOSED FIX
- Enable two reviewer slots by default in the candidate-cycle reviewer.
- Stamp shared review_group_id and distinct reviewer_index correlation facts.
- Aggregate conservatively: unanimous evidence-backed APPROVE required; any evidenced non-approve enters classification; malformed/empty/transport failures consume per-candidate, per-slot review retry budget.
- Remove the transitional single-slot exception from docs if this bead completes.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the same-worktree repair loop semantics except to feed aggregate review results into the existing classification step.
- Do not force different concrete providers; same-provider review emits pairing-degraded evidence only.

DEPS
Depends on stable single-slot pre-land review/repair and routing power floors.
title: review: enable default two-slot candidate review group

2. TestWorkCleanupEndToEnd_PrunesStaleRegisteredAndUnregisteredWorktrees creates real stale DDx worktrees/dirs under a temp DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR and verifies ddx work or ddx cleanup prunes them safely.
3. TestTryResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_Reclaimable verifies ddx try on a target bead fails with resource_exhausted, exits non-zero, and leaves the bead open/unclaimed without execute-loop-retry-after.

description:
PROBLEM
The cleanup/resource contract needs an end-to-end regression harness, not only isolated unit tests. The May 6 failure crossed command, git worktree, tracker claim, resource classification, and operator-output boundaries: a unit-only suite could still miss the exact behavior where ddx work claims the next bead after ENOSPC.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:686-718 owns real git worktree setup/cleanup, while cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:437-530 owns queue drain loop state; existing tests cover many individual branches but not the full resource-exhausted incident path.
- cli/cmd/try.go:306-342 and cli/cmd/work.go:101 route command contexts into workers, but there is no command-level e2e test proving cleanup/resource failures produce correct operator output and tracker state.
- cli/internal/agent/run_state_test.go:95-145 tests orphan recovery shapes, but not full DDx temp root cleanup plus registered worktree pruning plus claim safety.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:449-498 requires cleanup to be inline, loop-level, background, observable, and loop-fatal on resource exhaustion.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add end-to-end tests that use a temp project, temp DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR, real git worktree commands where feasible, and injectable resource-check failures where filling a filesystem would be unsafe.
- Verify tracker state after failure: no extra bead claimed, failed bead open/re-claimable, no retry-after cooldown for host resource exhaustion.
- Verify cleanup state after failure: stale unregistered dirs and stale registered worktrees are removed; active/preserved evidence survives.
- Verify operator output and loop events contain cleanup summary and resource_exhausted stop reason.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not create a real ENOSPC by filling /tmp or exhausting inodes in CI.
- Do not require live harness/model/provider calls.
- Do not test unrelated production-reachability bead content.

2. TestIntake_UnsafeRewriteRemovedFromReadyExecution asserts Store.ReadyExecution no longer returns the bead after the rejection.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Executable specifications · Audit trail required · Context is king · Work is a DAG · Drift is debt</principles>
<concerns>go-std | security-owasp | testing</concerns>
<practices>Go changes live under cli/ · use focused unit tests with real store/in-memory stubs · run targeted go test plus lefthook · preserve tracker mutations through ddx bead commands</practices>
<governing>FEAT-010 and TD-031 require rejected readiness rewrites to park with needs_human instead of returning to the execution-ready lane. Rejected rewrite evidence must explain why preservation could not be proven.</governing>
</context-digest>

PROBLEM
When readiness returns actionable_but_rewritten but the rewrite validator rejects the proposed mutation, ddx work logs a blocked outcome and unclaims the bead without parking it. In continuous mode, the same bead can be selected again immediately, producing a loop.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:767-783 handles `PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten` validation errors by emitting `pre_claim_intake.blocked`, calling `Unclaim`, and continuing. It does not call `parkBeadPostIntakeRejection`.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:798-821 correctly parks other terminal non-actionable intake outcomes with `needs_human`; the unsafe rewrite rejection branch diverged from that policy.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_test.go:383-433 verifies unsafe rewrites do not execute, but does not assert `needs_human`, `intake.blocked` bead evidence, or removal from `ReadyExecution`.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1508-1511 already excludes `needs_human` beads from `ReadyExecution`, so parking is sufficient to stop the loop.

PROPOSED FIX
- In the unsafe rewrite rejection branch, call `parkBeadPostIntakeRejection` with outcome `ambiguous_needs_human` and the validator detail before unclaiming.
- Change operator wording from `releasing` to `parking for human review` or equivalent.
- Preserve the validator failure detail in the event stream and bead evidence.
- Strengthen tests so rejected rewrites are proven to be open/unclaimed, labeled `needs_human`, recorded with `intake.blocked`, and absent from `ReadyExecution`.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the validated replacement rewrite policy; that is ddx-a41e6fbb.
- Do not change readiness routing or model selection.
- Do not change generic ambiguous_needs_human parking semantics outside this branch.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-5cd555c0. Depends on ddx-5cd555c0 so the spec-evolution bead lands first.

acceptance:
1. A reusable delimiter helper exists for untrusted artifact bodies.
2. Prompt assembly uses the helper at every artifact-body interpolation site in scope.
3. `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` proves the delimiter contract and payload preservation.

description:
PROBLEM
Prompt-injection boundary markers are not yet explicit around artifact bodies that later flow into prompt assembly. The current server-side artifact readers in `cli/internal/server/state_executions.go:205-217` clamp text for transport, but they do not wrap the body in an explicit untrusted-data envelope for downstream prompt renderers. The review prompt path in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1561-1630` only uses XML tags for the bead envelope; it does not mark artifact content with a dedicated boundary contract.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no shared delimiter helper for untrusted artifact bodies. The current prompt builders rely on ad hoc XML wrappers and transport clamping, which are not the same thing as a prompt-injection boundary that clearly distinguishes trusted instructions from untrusted artifact text.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small helper (likely in `cli/internal/evidence` or the prompt-rendering package that owns the assembler) that wraps artifact bodies in a canonical untrusted-data delimiter pair.
- Update the prompt-rendering call sites that inline artifact bodies to use the wrapper before interpolation.
- Add a regression test named `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` that asserts the delimiter pair appears around the artifact payload and that the raw body is still preserved inside the wrapper.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not alter the transport-level clamp behavior or its `truncated` metadata.
- Do not change review verdict parsing or the FEAT-022 telemetry schema in this slice.
- Do not rewrite unrelated server text egress paths.

| ddx-d7aca866 is a rollup/tracking epic for ADR-022 rev 5. Its acceptance criteria are not directly implementable in a single agent attempt: - "All 9 implementation children closed (steps 1-9 from the roadmap)" - "Coordination bead 10 closed (C7 conflict resolved)" - "FEAT-006 reflects rev 5" - "Acceptance bead's soak passed for ≥1 week"  ← calendar-time gate - "CHANGELOG entry merged" Closure is gated on (a) each child bead being independently executed and closed, and (b) a ≥1-week soak window that cannot be compressed by an agent. The epic itself has no code/test deliverable; it is a rollup whose status should flip to closed only after the last child closes and the soak window elapses. Current child inventory (queried via `ddx bead list --json | jq 'parent==epic'`): - ddx-2892f2cf  open   step 1: server ingestion endpoints (register + event + backfill) - ddx-b6ab9c0b  open   step 8: FEAT-006 amendment — replace rev 3 worker-contract with rev 5 The epic description says "The 9 implementation beads + 1 coordination bead are children of this epic," but only 2 of the 10 are filed. Steps 2–7, step 9, and the coordination bead (step 10 / C7 resolution) have not been created. Filing those slices is a triage decision (scope, ordering, owners) that should be done by a human or a dedicated decomposition pass against the ADR roadmap — not auto-generated mid-execution loop, because the ADR-022 rev 5 roadmap sections name specific contracts (auth, schema, retry, ack semantics) that warrant an explicit triage review before bead text is committed. 1. Import and backend code preserve proposed, open, in_progress, blocked, closed, and cancelled status values without defaulting valid values to open.
2. Unknown legacy pseudo-status values such as needs_human and needs_investigation are rejected in normal import/update paths with a clear error that points to the one-way lifecycle migrator; only the lifecycle migrator may convert them.
3. TestImportPreservesAllLifecycleStatuses covers all six statuses.
4. TestImportRejectsLegacyPseudoStatusesOutsideLifecycleMigrator covers needs_human and needs_investigation pseudo-status inputs.
5. TestChaosAllowedStatusesIncludesSixLifecycleValues covers chaos/property fixtures with proposed, blocked, and cancelled included.
6. TestAxonBackend_ListReadyBlocked is updated only as needed to preserve statuses while leaving query semantic changes to the store bead.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestImport.*Lifecycle|TestImportRejectsLegacy|TestChaosAllowedStatuses|TestAxonBackend_ListReadyBlocked|TestSchema|TestSync' -count=1` passes.

9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Storage, import, backend, and schema-adjacent paths do not consistently preserve or validate the six upstream lifecycle statuses. Some paths only recognize open, in_progress, and closed, so proposed/blocked/cancelled can be lost or treated as open.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/import.go:262-263 preserves only open, in_progress, and closed and otherwise defaults to open.
- cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go uses allowed statuses that exclude proposed, blocked, and cancelled.
- cli/internal/bead/types.go:59-75 has the six constants but comments and validation are not governed by the new state-machine contract.
- cli/internal/bead/axon_backend_test.go:TestAxonBackend_ListReadyBlocked currently treats ready/blocked through old store semantics.
- cli/internal/bead/sync_test.go:238 still says non-status needs_investigation should be encoded as label/event, which must be reframed as legacy migration metadata.

PROPOSED FIX
Wire storage-facing validation and import/export/backend preservation to the pure lifecycle status set. Proposed, open, in_progress, blocked, closed, and cancelled must round-trip without being silently rewritten. Unknown historical pseudo-status values such as needs_investigation must route through explicit compatibility/migration handling rather than becoming active lifecycle values. Update schema/sync tests that assert the status vocabulary.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change readiness/blocked queue derivation in this bead except where backend tests need status preservation fixtures. Store query behavior is owned by the store wiring bead.

DEPS
Depends on the pure lifecycle core.
title: storage: preserve six lifecycle statuses across import and backends

description:
PROBLEM
All 21 ddx agent subcommands remain live in cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:67-85 — none are Hidden, none are Deprecated. The migration to ddx run/try/work was supposed to happen but only ddx work was added. Operators still use ddx agent surface, and skill manifests + docs still reference ddx agent.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:67-85: 21 cobra command registrations for ddx agent subcommands (run, execute-bead, execute-loop, benchmark, condense, capabilities, replay, list, doctor, log, etc.) are all live. None set Hidden=true or Deprecated.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: execute-loop and execute-bead are aliases; after C13 (ddx-387a0178), ddx work and ddx try replace these.
- .agents/skills/, .claude/skills/, skills/: skill manifests reference 'ddx agent run' and 'ddx agent execute-bead'.
- docs/: README.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, website/content/docs/cli/_index.md reference ddx agent.
- Depends on C13 (ddx-387a0178) which adds ddx try CLI — cannot retire ddx agent until ddx try exists.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Delete cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go and all cli/cmd/agent_*.go sibling files.
2. Verify: ddx run + ddx try + ddx work cover every prior ddx agent operation:
   - ddx agent run → ddx run
   - ddx agent execute-loop → ddx work
   - ddx agent execute-bead → ddx try
   - ddx agent list → ddx agent list removed (or migrated to ddx harness list)
   - ddx agent doctor → ddx doctor
   - ddx agent log → ddx log (or removed)
3. Update skill manifests to use new commands.
4. Update docs (README.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, website CLI docs).
5. Update lefthook hooks if any reference ddx agent.

NON-SCOPE
- Adding new flags or subcommands to ddx run/try/work (out of scope).
- Deprecation period (user directive: 'completely gone').

CONTEXT
The first executable surface for Prose Quality Support is an advisory command over changed Markdown files. This should give users value without blocking ddx work, ddx bead review, or ddx doc validate by default.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/cmd/doc*.go or existing doc command files
- cli/internal/docprose/ checker integration code
- config schema/types for prose settings if not already landed
- command tests under cli/cmd and checker tests under cli/internal/docprose

COMMAND BEHAVIOR

- Optionally support explicit paths if TD-036 includes them, but changed Markdown is required.
- Findings include file, line, rule id, severity, rationale, and suggested edit.
- Exit behavior is advisory by default; do not break unrelated workflows on findings.
- Respect project config for mode, vocabulary, includes/excludes, and severity threshold.
- If an external checker is selected by TD-036 and missing, emit a clear advisory infrastructure message per the TD rather than panicking.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Bead review integration.
- CI blocking policy.
- AI-authorship scoring.

acceptance:
1. TD-031 contains the authoritative status-owned state machine, including allowed transitions, derived queue buckets, dependency-satisfaction rules, and the rule that labels/events/Extra explain state but do not control lifecycle.
2. FEAT-004, SD-004, FEAT-010, SD-025, ADR-023, ADR-024, docs/triage/decomposition.md, FEAT-008, FEAT-006, and TD-024 no longer describe needs_human or triage:needs-investigation as active queue states.
3. The docs explicitly say dependency waiting stays status=open and is derived from unsatisfied deps; status=blocked is reserved for rare external recheckable blockers.
4. The docs explicitly say proposed is the operator-intervention lifecycle state and replaces needs_human for active routing.
5. The docs explicitly say cancelled is materially different from closed because cancelled does not satisfy dependents unless another explicit dependency policy later says otherwise.
6. `rg -n "needs_human|needs-human|needs investigation|needs-investigation|triage:needs-investigation" docs/helix docs/triage` returns only legacy/migration/backcompat mentions that are locally labeled as such.
7. `rg -n "blocked.*depend|depend.*blocked" docs/helix docs/triage` shows dependency waiting described as derived waiting, not status=blocked.
8. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/graphql/...` passes as a regression gate.
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The governing docs do not define a single authoritative bead lifecycle. They describe upstream status values, derived ready/blocked queues, needs_human labels, needs-investigation labels, cooldowns, and no-changes outcomes as overlapping lifecycle controls. Downstream code and operators cannot know which field owns the bead's current state.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:102 names the upstream status enum, but docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:173,226,253,272,289,367,420 still route lifecycle through no_changes_needs_investigation and needs_human labels.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:39-41 says ready excludes needs_human and status includes a needs_human subcount, while docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:80 calls needs_human an open-label lane.
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-004-beads-tracker.md:187-224 defines Blocked as open beads with unsatisfied deps instead of external blockers only.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:190,408,430,463,489 park execution failures as needs_human.
- docs/triage/decomposition.md:22-23,73,110 defines needs-human escalation as a queue state.

PROPOSED FIX
Edit the governing specs so status is the sole lifecycle field owned by DDx. Define the six statuses exactly: proposed = operator decision required and not autonomous-work eligible; open = accepted active work, including dependency-waiting work; in_progress = claimed/running; blocked = accepted work paused by an external, recheckable blocker; closed = terminal satisfied and satisfies dependents; cancelled = terminal not-doing and does not satisfy dependents. Dependency waits are derived from dependencies, not status=blocked. Replace active needs_human and triage:needs-investigation lifecycle language with status=proposed, keeping old labels only as legacy metadata/migration terms where explicitly marked. Update TD-031 first, then FEAT-004, SD-004, FEAT-010, SD-025, ADR-023, ADR-024, docs/triage/decomposition.md, FEAT-008, FEAT-006, and TD-024 where their wording depends on the old labels.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change Go, GraphQL, Svelte, or skill files in this bead. Do not add a new TD; this is a contract correction across existing governing artifacts.

DEPS
No implementation deps. This bead is the contract input for all code changes in this epic.
title: docs: define status-owned bead lifecycle contract

description:
PROBLEM
The REST worker start endpoint drops execution fields because it decodes into a handler-local request struct that is narrower than the CLI/runtime spec.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 defines a private request struct missing opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power, from_rev, and spec_version. cli/internal/server/server.go:2441-2455 then manually constructs ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec, creating another silent omission point.

PROPOSED FIX
- Decode POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop directly into executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec.
- Resolve ProjectRoot using existing resolveRequestedProject behavior, write it back to the spec, then call ApplyDefaults and Validate before StartExecuteLoop.
- Add a shared server-side start helper if needed so REST and GraphQL use the same ApplyDefaults -> Validate -> StartExecuteLoop lifecycle.
- Return HTTP 400 for unsupported future SpecVersion with a JSON capability list; keep unknown JSON fields tolerated for newer-server/older-client compatibility.
- Preserve existing 403 trust check and 422 unknown project behavior.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not rework HTTP transport or endpoint path.
- Do not modify GraphQL dispatch; a separate child owns that parallel path.
- Do not change worker runtime consumption beyond what is necessary to pass the unified spec to StartExecuteLoop.
title: server: decode REST execute-loop workers from ExecuteLoopSpec

acceptance: 1. Default --poll-interval = 30s in all four call sites: cli/cmd/work.go:66, cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1568, cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:115, cli/internal/server/server.go:2281. 2. execute_bead_loop.go:440-441 no longer returns early when poll-interval > 0; sleeps + retries instead. Per-Run 'attempted'/'hookFailed' maps reset on no-candidate path. 3. execute_bead_loop.go:483-508 preflight rejection becomes per-bead continue, not loop exit. 4. loop.end event includes structured exit_reason field. Recognized values include 'sigterm', 'sigint', 'fatal_config', 'once_complete', 'explicit_poll_zero'; 'providers_exhausted' slot reserved for ddx-aede917d. 5. Server-managed workers report no_ready_work as transient (worker stays in 'running' state with 'idle' substate); existing 'exited' status reserved for true exits per AC #4. 6. Tests: TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue (poll-interval > 0; verify loop stays alive across N empty polls); TestLoop_PreflightRejectionContinues (one bead's preflight failure doesn't abandon siblings); TestLoop_OnceFlagStillExits (back-compat); TestLoop_ExplicitPollZeroExits (back-compat). 7. Manual verification: spawn worker via 'ddx work --harness claude'; let queue drain; verify worker stays running > 5 min after queue empty (vs current behavior of immediate exit). 8. Update FEAT-006 / FEAT-010 docs noting the new long-running default + the explicit opt-out flags.
description:
Workers exit prematurely even when ready beads remain in the queue. Operators have to manually relaunch, defeating the point of a long-running drain.

## Root causes (from sub-agent investigation)

1. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:440-441**: when nextCandidate returns no eligible bead AND runtime.PollInterval <= 0, the loop returns immediately. Combined with the per-Run 'attempted' map (line 388, 476), once each currently-ready bead has been *attempted once* the loop exits even though those same beads (or new arrivals) are still queue-ready.

2. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:483-508**: routing-preflight rejection on a single bead exits the entire loop ('return result, nil'), abandoning all other ready beads.

3. **Default --poll-interval=0 everywhere**: cli/cmd/work.go:66, cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1568, cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:115, cli/internal/server/server.go:2281. All three recent server-spawned workers in .ddx/workers/ show poll_interval: '0s', so cause #1 fires the first empty poll.

## Fix shape (locked decisions baked in)

- Flip default --poll-interval from 0 → **30s** everywhere (CLI + server adapter). Operator can override with --once for one-shot or explicit --poll-interval=N.
- Reset per-Run 'attempted'/'hookFailed' maps when the loop falls through to 'no candidate', before sleeping. Prevents the 'attempted-once = exit' trap.
- Convert preflight early-exit (483-508) to per-bead 'continue' so one bead's routing failure doesn't abandon the rest.
- Add structured exit_reason to loop.end event; reserve 'providers_exhausted' slot for ddx-aede917d (quota-pause work).
- Server-managed workers expose 'no_ready_work' as a **transient 'running (idle)' substate**, not terminal. Worker stays alive; UI shows running (idle).
- Preserve --once and explicit --poll-interval=0 for back-compat (legacy 'drain-and-exit' semantics).

Acceptable exit conditions remain (per existing beads):
- SIGTERM / SIGINT (operator-driven)
- All providers durably out (per ddx-aede917d quota pause/resume — workers pause+probe, not exit)
- No providers configured at all (fatal config error)
- --once explicitly requested
- --poll-interval=0 explicitly requested

title: ddx work / agent execute-loop: stay alive while queue has ready work and viable providers exist

acceptance:
1. Human/default `ddx work` output no longer prints an extra bare `route:` summary after scoped phase route lines have already been emitted.
2. Terminal human summary uses explicit labels for attempts, closed, changed, already-satisfied, and failures; it does not rely on ambiguous `completed:` wording.
3. Terminal human summary includes stop reason / mode context from `ExecuteBeadLoopResult`.
4. Terminal human summary includes remaining queue-state buckets from the queue snapshot: execution-ready, blocked, needs-human/investigation, cooldown/deferred, execution-ineligible, superseded, epics, and epic closure candidates.
5. Terminal human summary includes human-review dependency pressure when present: `N beads blocked behind M needs-human blockers`, with blocker ID/title/count entries.
6. `TestWorkDefaultOutput_DoesNotPrintBareRouteSummaryAfterScopedRoutes` covers the duplicate-summary case.
7. `TestWorkDefaultOutput_PrintsCompactTerminalSummary` covers changed vs already-satisfied counts and stop reason.
8. `TestWorkDefaultOutput_PrintsQueueStateAndHumanBlockers` covers a queue snapshot with 30 beads blocked behind 3 needs-human blockers.
9. Existing `TestWorkJSONOutput_IncludesRouteEconomicsWithoutHumanLines` remains green and asserts JSON output still includes route economics fields.
10. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run 'TestWorkDefaultOutput|TestWorkJSONOutput|TestFormatAttemptRouteEconomics'` passes.
11. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Even after route/progress lines are scoped, `ddx work` final human summaries can still emit bare `route:` lines and underspecified completion counts. Operators seeing `completed: 3 | successes: 3 | failures: 0` cannot tell whether work changed code, was already satisfied, why the worker exited, or whether many beads remain blocked behind human review.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1871-1937 formats `ddx work` human/default summaries independently from the live work stream.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1925 prints only `completed`, `successes`, and `failures`, which hides changed vs already-satisfied outcomes.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1927-1928 prints final route economics as bare `route: %s` lines in human mode.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1885-1923 prints `No execution-ready beads.` only for zero-attempt no-ready runs and does not summarize remaining blocked/human/cooldown queue state after a drain that processed work.
- ddx-7dcbd40b will expose stop reason and queue snapshot fields needed for a better final summary.

PROPOSED FIX
Align final summaries with the scoped stdout contract:
- Replace `completed:` wording with explicit counts: attempts, closed, changed, already-satisfied, failures.
- Include stop reason / mode context, for example `worker exited: drained current execution-ready queue` or `worker stopped: once complete`.
- Include remaining queue-state summary using the queue snapshot: execution-ready, blocked, needs-human/investigation, cooldown/deferred with next retry, execution-ineligible, superseded, epics, and epic closure candidates.
- If open work is transitively blocked behind human-review blockers, include a concise explanation such as `30 beads blocked behind 3 needs-human blockers:` followed by blocker ID/title/count entries.
- Avoid duplicate bare route lines after scoped phase route output; any retained terminal route/economics information must use compact wording consistent with the renderer.
- Preserve JSON output and route economics fields for machine consumers.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go
- cli/cmd/work_test.go
- cli/cmd/agent_output_test.go only if existing output helper tests need adjustment

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove route economics from JSON output.
- Do not change result status classification, close/retry behavior, or cost accounting.
- Do not alter structured execution records.
- Do not implement queue snapshot collection; ddx-7dcbd40b owns that.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-eeced112. Depends on lifecycle stdout, Fizeau progress stdout, and loop-result queue snapshot work.
title: cmd: align ddx work final summaries with scoped stdout

description:
PROBLEM
Review currently runs as a post-merge/pre-close gate from project root, which prevents efficient same-worktree repair and can leave rejected implementation commits already landed. Candidate-cycle review should run before final land in the still-live attempt worktree, after candidate refs and checks are available.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1086-1104 calls RunPostMergeReview after the loop has a success report from the existing attempt/landing path.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:53-77 names the current state machine post-merge and pre-close.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:715-812 ReviewBead builds a prompt from project root git show and dispatches reviewer with WorkDir set later from ProjectRoot, not the attempt worktree.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:765-767 currently removes the worktree before a post-loop reviewer could use it.

PROPOSED FIX
- Move single-slot review into the candidate-cycle coordinator before final land.
- Dispatch the reviewer with workdir set to the still-live attempt worktree and the read-only tool reviewer profile from the dependency bead.
- Review canonical bounded evidence for base_rev..candidate_rev while allowing supplemental read-only worktree inspection.
- Approval permits final land; review failure prevents land and records candidate-cycle evidence.
- Make the old post-land review path unreachable for the new coordinator, but leave final removal to the later retirement bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not enable default two-slot review yet; the spec bead allows transitional single-slot review.
- Do not implement repair classification or repair loops in this bead.

DEPS
Depends on read-only reviewer enforcement, coordinator interfaces, candidate refs, and checks-before-review.
title: review: run pre-land candidate review in same worktree

acceptance:
1. A pure lifecycle module exists under cli/internal/bead and has no store, filesystem, git, process, config, or wall-clock side effects.
2. TestLifecycleTransitionMatrix covers allowed and rejected transitions among proposed, open, in_progress, blocked, closed, and cancelled, including open/in_progress/blocked to proposed for operator-required outcomes.
3. TestLifecycleQueueDerivation covers execution-ready open beads, dependency-waiting open beads, proposed operator-attention beads, in_progress claimed beads, externally blocked beads, closed terminal beads, and cancelled terminal beads.
4. TestLifecycleDependencySatisfactionClosedOnly proves closed satisfies dependents and cancelled/proposed/open/in_progress/blocked do not.
5. TestLifecycleBlockedRequiresExternalReason proves dependency waits are not converted into status=blocked and blocked requires an external recheckable blocker reason.
6. TestLifecycleLegacyLabelsDoNotControlLifecycle proves needs_human and triage:needs-investigation labels do not by themselves remove an open bead from worker eligibility; only status/provided compatibility migration output does.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestLifecycle' -count=1` passes.
8. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/...` passes.
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Lifecycle semantics are scattered through store queries, agent parking paths, no-changes parsers, GraphQL resolvers, and frontend filters. Each surface can reinterpret ready, blocked, proposed/operator-required, terminal, and dependency-satisfied differently.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/types.go:59-75 defines status constants with comments that diverge from the new contract, especially blocked and proposed.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1473-1541 embeds readiness filtering directly in readyFiltered.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1575-1719 embeds blocked/needs_human/needs-investigation classification in BlockedAll.
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:333-348 hard-codes ambiguous_needs_human outcome names.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2017-2036 treats needs_human as an implementation-attempt outcome reason.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a pure functional lifecycle module under cli/internal/bead, for example lifecycle.go or statemachine.go, that exports the canonical status constants, transition validation, dependency-satisfaction rules, worker-eligibility derivation, operator-attention derivation, and queue-bucket derivation. The API must accept plain inputs (status, dependency states, claimed/stale claim facts, cooldown facts, external-blocker facts, terminal facts, labels/events as explanatory metadata) and return deterministic derived state without reading the store, clock globals, git, config, or files. Labels such as needs_human and triage:needs-investigation must not control lifecycle except through an explicit legacy-compat input that emits migration warnings/results.

NON-SCOPE
Do not wire store, CLI, agent, GraphQL, or frontend callers in this bead. This is the reusable core and its tests.

DEPS
Depends on the updated specs so function names and transition names match the contract.
title: bead: add pure lifecycle state machine core

description:
PROBLEM
Fizeau now owns transcript, progress, routing-service event semantics, and session rendering for agent execution. DDx still has cleanup debt that parses Claude streams, renders Fizeau session logs, infers runtime status from Fizeau internals, and calls route/service APIs from DDx execution paths.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go still drains and interprets Fizeau service events for DDx-specific runtime policy.
- cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go still contains legacy direct stream handling.
- cli/internal/agent/session_log_format.go and cli/internal/agent/session_log_tailer.go still render/tail inner agent logs.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go still mixes DDx worker lifecycle output with inner Fizeau progress/session surfaces.

PROPOSED FIX
Coordinate child cleanup beads so DDx keeps only worker/bead lifecycle, opaque Fizeau event forwarding, artifact links, and final DDx Result projection. Fizeau owns inner transcript/progress/session semantics.

NON-SCOPE
- Fizeau schema or API changes.
- DDx queue lifecycle state, bead state transitions, worker watchdog policy, or final Result storage outside the specific boundary cleanup children.

description:
PROBLEM
After the C13 refactor (ddx-387a0178), shim typedefs of the form ExecuteBeadWorker, ExecuteBeadReport, ExecuteBeadResult, ExecuteBeadStatus, ExecuteBeadOutcome remain in the codebase as temporary aliases. These shims prevent callers from adopting the canonical names and leave dead identifier surface that grep/tooling cannot distinguish from active usage.

ROOT CAUSE
C13 (ddx-387a0178) introduces the canonical type names as part of the file/identifier rename step and adds shim typedefs for backward compat during the migration window. The shim typedefs live in the files renamed by C13 (exact file:line determined after C13 lands). The CLI aliases ddx agent execute-loop and ddx agent execute-bead continue to register live cobra commands after C13, pointing at logic now accessible via ddx work and ddx try.

PROPOSED FIX
- Sweep all callsites off the shim names (grep for ExecuteBeadWorker | ExecuteBeadReport | ExecuteBeadResult | ExecuteBead\b | ExecuteBeadStatus | ExecuteBeadOutcome in cli/).
- Delete the shim typedefs from C13.
- Remove cobra command registrations for ddx agent execute-loop and ddx agent execute-bead; replace with a cobra RunE that prints a deprecation message pointing at ddx work / ddx try.
- Verify: grep returns 0 hits in non-deleted, non-test files.

NON-SCOPE
- Deleting the entire ddx agent command group (that is ddx-d8474e0e, a separate bead).
- Changing execute-loop/try behavior.
title: C14: delete ExecuteBead* shims and aliases; verify all callers ported

description:
PROBLEM
No GraphQL mutations or subscription exist for review sessions. The review panel (Story 18) cannot start, respond to, or cancel a review session through the GraphQL API, and there is no way to stream review response deltas to the frontend.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql: no reviewSessionStart, reviewSessionRespond, reviewSessionCancel mutations defined.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql: no reviewSessionEvents subscription defined.
- cli/internal/server/graphql_resolver.go: no resolver implementations for the above.
- ddx-f7c3d512 (turn dispatcher, a dep) must land first — it provides the dispatch path that these mutations invoke.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add to schema.graphql:
  - Mutation.reviewSessionStart(sessionId, artifactId, artifactSha, templateRef, promptRef, maxBillableUSD): ReviewSession
  - Mutation.reviewSessionRespond(sessionId, turn): ReviewSession
  - Mutation.reviewSessionCancel(sessionId): Boolean
  - Subscription.reviewSessionEvents(sessionId): ReviewSessionEvent (streaming deltas optional; final-message required)
- Add resolver implementations in graphql_resolver.go for each mutation + subscription.
- Streaming deltas: optional (loosened per codex from strict streaming AC); final-message is required.

NON-SCOPE
- Reviewer routing and cost cap (ddx-f1e12904).
- Turn dispatcher implementation (ddx-f7c3d512, a dep).
title: review: GraphQL mutations + reviewSessionEvents (status + optional deltas)

acceptance:
1. ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go ~line 30) gains PreDispatchLintHook + PostAttemptTriageHook closure fields with the documented signatures. LintResult + TriageResult + FollowupBead types defined in same file or sibling cli/internal/agent/quality_hooks.go.

2. PreDispatchLintHook fires at the documented insertion point (after nextCandidate, before Claim, sibling to RoutePreflight at execute_bead_loop.go:662). Verified by integration test TestLoop_LintHook_FiresPreClaim.

3. PostAttemptTriageHook fires after outcome adjudication, before cooldown. Verified by TestLoop_TriageHook_FiresPostOutcome.

4. cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go + triage_hook.go implement the hooks via runner library (NOT subprocess to ddx agent run). Verified by TestLintHook_UsesRunnerLibrary asserting no os/exec call.

5. WARN-ONLY default: tests TestLintHook_LowScore_WarnDoesNotBlock + TestTriageHook_RecordsButDoesNotMutateOutcome.

6. BLOCK mode opt-in: config flag bead-quality.lint.block_threshold_score. Test TestLintHook_BlockMode_RefusesDispatchOnLowScore.

7. FAIL-OPEN: tests TestLintHook_SkillMissing_ProceedsWithWarning + TestLintHook_BadJSON_ProceedsWithWarning + TestLintHook_Timeout_ProceedsWithWarning + TestTriageHook_HookError_FallsThroughToLegacyCooldown.

8. ExecuteBeadReport gains OutcomeReason string field (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go ~line 1317 event serialization). Disrupted bool unchanged. Test TestReport_OutcomeReason_Persists_BesideDisrupted.

9. shouldSuppressNoProgress (execute_bead_loop.go:1522) extends to short-circuit on OutcomeReason ∈ {transport, quota, routing, timeout, merge_conflict}. Test TestSuppressNoProgress_HonorsTransientReasons.

10. cli/cmd/try.go wires both hooks into Runtime construction at try.go:327. Test TestTry_HooksWired.

11. WIRED-IN: 'git grep PreDispatchLintHook cli/cmd/' shows the wire site; 'git grep PostAttemptTriageHook cli/cmd/' shows same.

12. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook run pre-commit passes.

13. Manual verification: 'ddx try <retrofit-bead-id>' (after epic child #2 ships sub-skill) emits bead-quality.lint event in the bead's event stream; 'ddx try <bead-with-bad-AC>' under BLOCK mode refuses dispatch with operator-readable message.

14. Conventional commit ending [<this-bead-id>]. Stage cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go + cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go + cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go + cli/internal/agent/quality_hooks.go + cli/cmd/try.go + cli/internal/agent/*_test.go + cli/cmd/try_test.go only.
description:
PROBLEM
Per epic ddx-a9d130d0 + ADR-023 + sub-skill bead-lifecycle/: ddx try needs to invoke the sub-skill at two points (pre-dispatch lint, post-attempt triage) and act on the structured output. Today ddx try has hook precedents (PreClaimHook, RoutePreflight) but no LLM-invoking hook + no outcome classification carrier.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:24-38 defines ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime with PreClaimHook + RoutePreflight closures; no slot for lint or triage hooks.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:657 RoutePreflight runs between pick and claim — exactly where lint should fire.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:45 ExecuteBeadResult already has reason + failure_mode; ExecuteBeadReport (loop event payload) does NOT — only status/detail/rationale at execute_bead_loop.go:1317.
- shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1522 already respects Disrupted bool. Codex amendment: KEEP Disrupted; ADD OutcomeReason beside it (do not replace).
- ddx try at cli/cmd/try.go:327 wires PreClaimHook today; sibling fields will go through the same Runtime construction.

PROPOSED FIX
1. ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime additions (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go ~line 30):
   PreDispatchLintHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)
   PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)
   LintResult struct { Score int; Rationale string; SuggestedFixes []string; WaiversApplied []string }
   TriageResult struct { Classification string; RecommendedAction string; Rationale string; SuggestedAmendments string; SuggestedFollowupBeads []FollowupBead }

2. PreDispatchLintHook fires AFTER nextCandidate, BEFORE Claim (sibling to RoutePreflight at execute_bead_loop.go:662).
   - Hook implementation in cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go: composes runner library to invoke bead-lifecycle skill in lint mode (per FEAT-001/FEAT-010 layer-1 invocation; NOT subprocess via ddx agent run per codex).
   - WARN-ONLY behavior (default): record lint result as bead event (kind=bead-quality.lint); proceed with claim regardless of score.
   - BLOCK mode (opt-in via config flag in .ddx/config.yaml): refuse claim when score < threshold; emit operator-readable explanation + cite bead-lifecycle SKILL.md guidance for fixes.
   - FAIL-OPEN per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES P1: infrastructure failure (skill missing, harness unavailable, bad JSON, timeout) → emit warning event + proceed. Only valid low-score response blocks (and only in BLOCK mode).

3. PostAttemptTriageHook fires AFTER outcome adjudication, BEFORE cooldown decision (read execute_bead_loop.go ~line 880-1100 for the outcome class chain).
   - Hook implementation in cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go: composes runner library to invoke bead-lifecycle skill in triage mode with (bead + outcome event + session log excerpt).
   - Triage classification populates new ExecuteBeadReport.OutcomeReason field (string).
   - shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1522 extends to also short-circuit when OutcomeReason ∈ {transport, quota, routing, timeout, merge_conflict} (transient classes per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES P6). Disrupted check stays in place beside it.
   - FAIL-OPEN: triage hook failure → no classification → fall through to existing cooldown logic (today's behavior, no regression).

4. cli/cmd/try.go integration: wire PreDispatchLintHook + PostAttemptTriageHook into ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime construction at try.go:327. Operator can disable via --no-lint / --no-triage flags (defer if not needed; defaults are warn-only so explicit disable is rare).

5. Hook invocation cost: lint runs once per dispatch attempt (cheap sonnet call); triage runs once per non-clean outcome. Both invocations cite the bead-lifecycle skill by name in the prompt; harness auto-discovers from filesystem (no special skill-loading code needed).

NON-SCOPE
- ddx work integration (this bead is ddx try only; ddx work integration is a separate follow-up if desired)
- BLOCK mode threshold tuning (operator chooses post-baseline; no code change)
- ddx work plan quality column (deferred per epic non-scope)
- GraphQL state.OutcomeReason exposure (defer; today's GraphQL maps status only at state_graphql.go:493; if exposure needed, separate bead per codex)
- --force --reason CLI flag (add only if BLOCK mode rollout reveals need; defer)

description:
PROBLEM
executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText (1078 words) and executeBeadInstructionsAgentText (1020 words) duplicate ~250 words across Step 0, evidence steering, review-gate, bead-override, and constraints tail. Maintaining two parallel prompt texts means changes to shared logic require dual edits and risk divergence.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or prompts package): two large string constants define the Claude and Agent harness instructions. Approximately 250 words appear verbatim in both, including the Step 0 investigation guidance, evidence steering instructions, review-gate checklist, bead-override pattern, and constraints tail.
- No shared constant or template mechanism exists; both strings are inline.
- ddx-cfedee8e (Story 10 prompt edits, a dep) must land first to avoid merge conflict.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extract the shared ~250 words into named constants (e.g., sharedStep0Block, sharedEvidenceBlock, sharedReviewGateBlock, sharedBeadOverrideBlock, sharedConstraintsTailBlock).
- Reassemble both Claude and Agent variants as: per-harness preamble + sharedStep0Block + process body (harness-specific) + shared tail blocks.
- Byte-equivalent: output bytes of the assembled prompts must match the prior output exactly (golden snapshot test).

NON-SCOPE
- Semantic changes to any prompt instruction.
- Changing prompt selection logic or harness routing.

description:
PROBLEM
System failures can look like bead failures, causing repeated retries, bad no-progress accounting, or misleading operator guidance.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:698-710` treats intake errors as warnings but does not expose a unified system-readiness reason.

description:
PROBLEM
BLOCKING for any non-localhost write enablement in coordinator topology. When the hub forwards a mutation to a spoke, there is no signed attestation of origin identity, and the spoke cannot distinguish a legitimate coordinator-forwarded request from a hub-originated forgery. Without dual authorization on both origin and coordinator identity, any compromised hub can submit mutations on behalf of any operator.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/federation/fanout.go: no attestation metadata structure defined; no signing of identity claims.
- cli/internal/server/server.go: requireTrusted checks only immediate transport peer (hub); does not validate origin identity independently.
- wake-on-approve routing (the 'wake-on-approve targets owning client node's loop' requirement) has no implementation path in the current federation layer.
- ddx-f171a715 (federation foundation, a dep) and ddx-0793ac75 (federated GraphQL resolvers, a dep) must land first.

PROPOSED FIX
- Define attestation metadata structure: signed structured metadata carrying origin identity (WhoIs peer at coordinator edge) and coordinator identity (hub's own attested ID). Signing mechanism: HMAC or similar per plan-locked decisions.
- Spoke validates BOTH: origin identity (verified against tailnet WhoIs / loopback policy) AND hub identity (verified against registered federation peers).
- wake-on-approve: when spoke receives an approve mutation, it routes wake signal to the owning client node's execute-loop (project-ownership authoritative on the spoke).

NON-SCOPE
- Transport-level encryption (handled by tailnet).
- Key management or certificate infrastructure beyond HMAC shared secret.
- Story 14 query fan-out behavior.
title: operator-prompts: multi-node trust attestation forwarding (gates non-localhost write)

description:
PROBLEM
New lifecycle code cannot safely start on top of an old bead queue. If legacy needs_human / triage:needs-investigation labels remain in active rows, the new semantics may treat those beads as runnable open work or leave stale labels as hidden control-plane state.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/command_factory.go:110-120 constructs the root command without a project-local bead queue migration gate.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:67-92 creates stores without checking lifecycle queue version or legacy label state.
- cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go:10-108 exposes migration commands but not a lifecycle migration mode that startup can point to.
- cli/internal/bead/migrate.go:23-40 migrates archive/event layout only, so there is no startup-enforced one-way lifecycle migration path.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a lifecycle queue detector and startup gate. For repositories with `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, DDx scans active beads for legacy lifecycle labels/pseudo-statuses and/or a missing lifecycle schema marker. Normal commands fail before reading/mutating queue state when the detector reports an unmigrated queue. The error must list counts and tell the operator to run the lifecycle migration dry-run/apply command. Allow only help, version, doctor/read-only diagnostics, and the lifecycle migrator itself to run. Once migration succeeds, record a schema/version marker so startup can proceed without rescanning expensive history.

NON-SCOPE
Do not maintain runtime compatibility for old labels. Do not auto-apply migration without operator command. Do not block commands outside a DDx project with no bead queue.

DEPS
Depends on the one-way lifecycle migrator and transition write API.
title: startup: block DDx on unmigrated lifecycle queues

description:
PROBLEM
The candidate-cycle design requires reviewers to inspect the live attempt worktree efficiently without being able to mutate it. Current reviewer dispatch is not enforced as read-only at the runtime boundary, so moving review into the attempt worktree would depend on prompt wording rather than a hard capability.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:623-673 builds reviewer AgentRunRuntime with Role=reviewer and MinPower metadata, but no read-only permission profile or tool restriction is set.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:826-842 sets implementation dispatch PermissionsOverride to unrestricted because implementation runs in an isolated worktree; reviewer dispatch needs the opposite boundary.
- cli/internal/agent/service_run.go and related dispatch code are the likely boundary where PermissionsOverride / Role / tool access must be translated into Fizeau execution config.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an enforceable read-only tool reviewer profile to the agent runtime/dispatch boundary.
- Ensure reviewer role can read files and run read-only inspection commands if supported, but cannot write files, stage, commit, delete, or run mutating shell commands.
- Fail closed if a harness cannot enforce read-only reviewer mode, with an operator-visible review-error class rather than silently running unrestricted.
- Add tests with a fake reviewer that attempts mutation and verify the mutation is rejected or impossible.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not move review into the attempt worktree in this bead; that is a dependent bead.
- Do not implement repair cycles.
- Do not choose concrete provider/model fallback logic.

DEPS
Depends on the spec bead under this epic so the read-only tool reviewer contract is defined before implementation.

AC3. acceptancePreservesCriteria treats reformatting (numbering, whitespace, ordering) as preserving criteria when the underlying verifiable assertions are unchanged.

description:
PROBLEM: The pre-claim intake hook (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go + preclaim_intake_rewrite.go) flags well-formed beads as ambiguous_needs_human with the generic detail "acceptance criteria dropped or altered", blocking them from execution.

SYMPTOM: Two beads in axon, 2026-05-08, both got the same generic intake.blocked event:
{"detail":"acceptance criteria dropped or altered","intake_outcome":"ambiguous_needs_human"}

- axon-044a5b5b: original AC was a single sentence (legitimately weak — refinement was warranted, but intake should have produced a concrete suggested rewrite, not just a needs_human outcome).
- axon-11508cde: 7 numbered ACs with file:line refs, in-scope/out-of-scope sections, and a rollback plan. This is a strongly-formed bead and should pass intake; it was flagged anyway.

LIKELY ROOT CAUSE (per source orientation):
- preclaim_intake_rewrite.go:256 validateAndApplyPreClaimIntakeRewrite returns "acceptance criteria dropped or altered".
- preclaim_intake_rewrite.go:289 acceptancePreservesCriteria validates whether a rewrite preserves AC.
- The intake LLM is rewriting beads as part of intake; the rewrite validator (acceptancePreservesCriteria) is firing when the rewrite is structurally different even when the AC content is preserved or improved. So strong beads get false-positive blocked because the rewriter can not improve them and the validator catches a non-substantive rewording.

EXPECTED BEHAVIOR:
- For weak ACs, intake should produce concrete suggested_fixes pointing at exactly what is missing (named test, file:line, etc.), not a needs_human outcome.
- For strong ACs, the intake rewriter should leave them alone (or recognize "no rewrite needed" as a pass), not generate a no-op rewrite that the validator flags.
- The validator should compare AC at a structural/semantic level (numbered points, named tests, verifiable claims) rather than literal-text equivalence.

PROPOSED FIX DIRECTION:
1. acceptancePreservesCriteria should be tolerant of formatting/whitespace/numbering changes that preserve the same set of verifiable claims; it should only fail when a CRITERION was demonstrably dropped or weakened.
2. The intake prompt should be updated to declare "if the existing acceptance criteria are already verifiable per the lint rubric, output an empty rewrite (no changes) and classification=ready" rather than always producing a rewrite.
3. The needs_human classification should require concrete unresolved questions, not just a validation failure on the rewrite itself.

NON-SCOPE: changing the bead-lifecycle SKILL.md contract; that contract is correct.
title: bug: pre-claim intake emits ambiguous_needs_human false positives on well-formed beads

acceptance:
1. library/checks/go-production-reachability/ exists with: README.md (usage), check.yaml (template), implementation (binary or script).
2. Implementation reads DIFF_BASE/DIFF_HEAD/PROJECT_ROOT/EVIDENCE_DIR from env; computes new symbols from diff; runs deadcode-rooted reachability; writes result.json conforming to REACH-PROTO schema.
3. // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation parsed; bead existence + open-status verified against .ddx/beads.jsonl; invalid annotation = violation.
4. _test.go files excluded from both root selection and reachability calculation (so a unit-test-only reference does NOT satisfy the check).
5. Self-test: synthetic project with one unwired exported function returns status=block with that symbol in violations.
6. Self-test: same project with the function wired into cmd handler returns status=pass.
7. Self-test: same project with // wiring:pending annotation citing an open bead returns status=pass; annotation citing a closed/missing bead returns status=block.
8. This project installs it: .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml committed; ddx ac run on any current bead succeeds (or BLOCKs with the known unwired functions — see REACH-BACKFILL for resolution).
9. cd cli && go test green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
10. Evidence file at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/production-reachability.json shows the check ran and its findings.
description:
Ship the Go-specific production-reachability check as a library resource conforming to the REACH-PROTO check protocol. NOT installed by default for arbitrary projects (Go-opinion would leak through ddx init). This project (ddx itself) opts in by adding .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml.

DESIGN
- Location: library/checks/go-production-reachability/ (alongside library/personas/, library/patterns/)
- Implementation: a small Go binary or shell script invoking golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode rooted at configurable entry packages.
- Default entry roots for ddx self-use: cli/cmd/..., cli/internal/server/graphql/..., cli/internal/server/http/...
- Logic:
  1. Read DIFF_BASE..DIFF_HEAD from env. Compute set of new Go symbols (functions, methods, types) added by the diff via AST parse of changed files.
  2. Run deadcode (or callgraph) rooted at the configured entry packages, EXCLUDING _test.go from roots and from reachability.
  3. For each new symbol not reachable from any entry root → violation { file, line, symbol, kind: "unreachable", detail: "no path from entry roots: <list>" }
  4. Recognize // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation as escape hatch for individual symbols. The check verifies the named bead exists and is open via local beads.jsonl read; otherwise the annotation is rejected.
  5. Write ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/production-reachability.json with the protocol-defined schema.
- Config: library/checks/go-production-reachability/check.yaml template that operators copy to .ddx/checks/ and customize entry_roots for their project.

DOGFOOD
- This project installs it: write .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml referencing the library check + cli's entry roots.
- The next ddx try on any bead touching cli/**/*.go will exercise the check.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Auto-install during ddx init (would leak Go-opinion). Operators opt in manually or via ddx install check (future, separate bead).
- Coverage-based "exercised via CLI" check (entered_via flavor). Static reachability is sufficient for the immediate problem.
- Python/TS/Rust analogues. Future contributions; protocol supports them.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (above)

title: checks: ship Go production-reachability check (library/checks/go-production-reachability)

description:
PROBLEM
DDx synthesizes a `default` routing profile for any operator who does not pass `--profile`, then sends `Profile="default"` to fizeau. Fizeau's `default` profile (`fizeau/internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml:438-443`) hard-pins `min_power: 7, max_power: 8`. For operators whose model catalog has no power-7-or-8 entries — e.g., local providers (power 5–6) plus frontier cloud (power 9) — `routePowerPolicyForRequest` overlays those bounds and the routing engine rejects every candidate as `below_min_power` or `above_max_power`. Result: zero-config `ddx work` claims a bead, dispatches with empty intent, fizeau returns "no viable routing candidate," and the agent exits in <1s with `no_evidence_produced`. Power was meant to be a hint, not a gate; synthesizing a constraint the operator never asked for inverts that policy. Repro: in this repo with no flags, `ddx work --once` triggers the dispatch-failure on hooks today and would trigger it on the implementation dispatch too if explicit `--harness/--model` flags weren't bypassing the bound via fizeau's exact-pin escape (`routePowerBoundsForRequest`).

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/routing_profile.go:9-15` returns the constant `"default"` whenever the operator-supplied profile is empty.
- `cli/cmd/work.go:70` registers the `--profile` flag with default value `agent.DefaultRoutingProfile`, so cobra fills in `"default"` even when the operator omits the flag.
- `cli/cmd/try.go:86` does the same for `ddx try`.
- `cli/cmd/run.go:137`, `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:298`, `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1613`, `cli/cmd/try.go:215`, and `cli/internal/server/workers.go:{303,409,705}` all call `NormalizeRoutingProfile`, inheriting the synthesis.
- Downstream fizeau gating: `routePowerPolicyForRequest` (`fizeau/service_routing.go:1117-1141`) returns zero bounds only when `req.Profile == ""`; any non-empty profile string causes a catalog lookup that overlays its `min_power`/`max_power` onto the request.

PROPOSED FIX
- Change `NormalizeRoutingProfile` to return the trimmed input as-is; an empty operator value stays empty. Keep the function (callers want trim+normalize), drop the synthesis branch.
- Remove the `DefaultRoutingProfile = "default"` constant, or repurpose it strictly for the help-text label rather than a runtime default. Update `cli/cmd/work.go:70` and `cli/cmd/try.go:86` so the cobra flag's default is `""` and the help string reads "Routing profile: cheap, fast, smart (empty = unconstrained, let the agent service choose)".
- Audit every caller of `NormalizeRoutingProfile` to confirm they tolerate `""` (today they all forward the value into `CLIOverrides.Profile` or a workers spec — empty is already valid downstream).
- After this change, zero-config `ddx work` sends `Profile=""` → fizeau applies no profile-derived power bound → routing picks any available model regardless of power tier, honoring the "power is a hint" policy.
- The pre-dispatch lint and post-attempt triage hooks (`lint_hook.go`, `triage_hook.go`) still need `ClearProfile: true` so they ignore an explicitly operator-set `--profile fast`/`--profile standard` (those profiles also pin power 7-8); that defensive change is tracked in ddx-c54b817c and remains correct.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change fizeau's `default` profile or its power-gating semantics; making power bounds advisory rather than hard is a fizeau-side change and out of scope here.
- Do not change `--min-power`/`--max-power` flag handling. When the operator explicitly asks for a power bound, honor it; this bead only stops DDx from inventing one.
- Do not remove `cheap`/`fast`/`smart`/`standard` profile values — they remain available as explicit operator opt-ins.
- Do not modify ddx-c54b817c's hook-level fix; it is independently defensive against operator-set profiles.

PARENT + DEPS
No parent. No deps. ddx-c54b817c is sibling/defensive; both can land in either order.
title: agent: stop synthesizing default routing profile from empty input

acceptance:
1. Fixture-based test: 2 workers + server up + restart server mid-flight; assert workers continue + reconnect + backfill in <60s.
2. Same scenario with NO server; assert identical bead outcomes.
3. Operator-cancel test: cancel issued; preserved_for_review reported within 30s with reason operator_cancel.
4. LIVE OBSERVABILITY: start a real ddx work worker against fixture repo with server running; observe via workers panel that worker appears within 30s, freshness state transitions to 'connected', current_bead updates within 60s of bead pickup. Kill worker process; observe transition to 'disconnected' within 2× probe interval.
5. Soak: ≥7 days of operator use without manual intervention; summary at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/soak-summary.md.
6. SOAK ABORT CRITERIA: rollback if any of: (a) any worker's mirror_failures_count exceeds 5 in a 24h window; (b) any silent dropped backfill (had_dropped_backfill flag set on any worker); (c) any operator-initiated SIGTERM of a worker process during soak (workers should not need killing); (d) any bead requires manual git-reset to recover; (e) any 'preserved_orphaned' outcome on a bead the worker actually finished. If any of these fire during soak, file a regression bead, halt the soak clock, and resume after fix lands.
7. CHANGELOG entry merged describing the worker-server architecture change.
description: Uses ddx-50da9674 clean fixture repo. Multi-worker drain WITH server up; restart server mid-flight; verify worker continues + reconnects + backfills. Then drain with NO server; verify identical bead outcomes. Operator-cancel test (cancel issued; preserved_for_review with operator_cancel reason within 30s). Soak: ≥1 week of normal operator use. Final bead.
title: ADR-022 step 9: acceptance + 1-week soak using clean fixture repo

1. RouteMutationToProjectOwner(registry, projectID) returns correct SpokeRecord or error when no owner registered.
2. ForwardMutation(ctx, target, body, headers) issues POST to <spoke>/graphql with supplied headers preserved verbatim.
3. Hub handler that accepts mutation forwarding requests exists and routes by project.
4. TestRouteMutation_CorrectSpoke verifies routing to the correct spoke.
5. TestRouteMutation_NoOwner_ReturnsError verifies error when project owner unknown.
6. TestForwardMutation_HeadersReachSpoke verifies headers arrive at spoke unchanged.
7. No behavior change to existing query fan-out tests.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/federation/... green.
