description:
PROBLEM
Federation today supports query fan-out only (cli/internal/federation/fanout.go). For the multi-node operator-prompt path, the coordinator (hub) must route a mutation to a single spoke that owns the project, NOT fan out. No mutation routing envelope, no project-based spoke selection, and no forwarding client exist.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/federation/fanout.go: FanOut function sends read queries to all spokes; has no concept of routing to a single owning spoke for mutations.
- FederationRegistry (from ddx-90f48012): stores spoke records by node_id but no lookup-by-project-id helper exists.
- No HTTP client method ForwardMutation exists in the federation package.
- Plan-locked decision (point 6 from story-15 planning): hub picks single owning spoke by project ID from FederationRegistry; no broadcast of mutations; mutation envelope is HTTP POST to spoke /graphql with X-DDx-Origin-Identity and X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity headers preserved verbatim.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add RouteMutationToProjectOwner(registry FederationRegistry, projectID string) (*SpokeRecord, error): looks up the spoke that owns the project; returns error when no owner registered.
- Add federation client method ForwardMutation(ctx context.Context, target *SpokeRecord, body []byte, headers map[string]string) (*http.Response, error): issues HTTP POST to <spoke>/graphql with supplied headers preserved verbatim.
- Add hub HTTP handler that accepts forwarding requests and routes by project (the handler that S15-7c and S15-7d build on top of).
- Identity header values are NOT populated in this bead (that is S15-7c / ddx-ba31fec8); this bead lays the envelope and routing.

NON-SCOPE
- Dual authorization on the spoke side (ddx-ba31fec8).
- Identity attestation structure (ddx-e2c217d1).
- Changing existing query fan-out behavior.
title: S15-7b: Coordinator→client mutation routing envelope (foundation)

acceptance: 1. TD-<NNN>-axon-bead-backend.md exists with all sections. 2. depends_on: [ADR-004, SD-004, FEAT-004]. 3. All five locked decisions reflected exactly. 4. Wire transport explicitly GraphQL-only with rationale. 5. Cross-references the plan at /tmp/ddx-axon-backend-plan.md as 'background' AND axon-82b6f7b2 as the cross-repo coordination tracker. 6. Adoption gates (alpha/beta/GA) documented with which axon-side beads gate each. 7. ddx doc audit clean. 8. TD picks a concrete TD-NNN ID.
description:
Author docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-<NNN>-axon-bead-backend.md.

Source material (consume in order):
1. /tmp/ddx-axon-backend-plan.md — full integration plan with Axon's external interface mapped + GraphQL pivot + locked decisions
2. Cross-repo tracking bead axon-82b6f7b2 — full DDx-on-axon adoption checklist (links to all required axon-side capabilities)

LOCKED DECISIONS (do not relitigate; bake into TD):
1. Deployment: separate axon-server, operator-managed. DDx does NOT spawn or supervise Axon.
2. Auth: localhost-only by default; ts-net for remote. Axon already supports ts-net per its serve.rs (Tailscale mode + LocalAPI whois + role tags). Reuses ADR-006.
3. Events storage: separate ddx_bead_events collection with event_of links (Option B). Two collections in Axon.
4. Offline posture: refuse all bead ops when Axon unreachable. No local cache, no WAL.
5. Wire transport: GraphQL only via genqlient-generated Go client. NOT gRPC. Subscriptions over WebSocket for live updates.

Cross-repo dependencies (tracked in axon-82b6f7b2):
- axon-05c1019d (P2): GraphQL pattern-query support — needed for single-query ddx bead ready/blocked. Day 1 ships with two-phase fallback.
- FEAT-013 secondary indexes (P1 Draft in axon): needed for status-field query performance at >1000 beads.
- FEAT-017 schema evolution (P1 Draft in axon): needed for clean DDx schema upgrades over time. Day 1 ships with DDx-side lazy migration on read.
- ts-net auth: ALREADY SHIPPED in axon. No coordination needed.
- GraphQL CRUD + subscriptions: ALREADY SHIPPED in axon.
- Multi-namespace/database: ALREADY SHIPPED.

Adoption gates (per axon-82b6f7b2):
- Alpha: requires axon-05c1019d + everything already-shipped. Acceptable to ship with table-scan perf.
- Beta: requires FEAT-013 (indexes) for >1000-bead performance.
- GA: requires FEAT-013 + FEAT-017 + axon-05c1019d all landed.

Defaults the TD can adopt without rechecking with operator (revisit only if implementation surfaces a problem):
- Schema versioning: DDx-side lazy migrate on read until FEAT-017 lands; switch to axon-native then.
- ddx bead ready Day 1: two-phase GraphQL query.
- Backup/DR: operator-managed via Axon's own backing-store DR.

Output: TD-<NNN>-axon-bead-backend.md with sections for: GraphQL schema mapping (DDx bead → Axon entity); operation mapping (DDx CLI → GraphQL queries/mutations/subscriptions); deployment + config; concurrency model (OCC via expectedVersion); migration plan; test plan; cross-repo coordination notes (links to axon-82b6f7b2 + FEAT-013/017).

acceptance:
1. CandidateCheckRunner runs deterministic checks against a pinned candidate before reviewer dispatch.
2. TestCandidateChecks_RunBeforeReview verifies reviewer is not called when candidate checks fail.
3. TestCandidateChecks_FinalLandChecksStillRun verifies final land still invokes SubmitWithPreMergeChecks or equivalent after review approval.
4. TestCandidateChecks_ArtifactsRecorded verifies check output is attached to candidate-cycle evidence.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/checks/... -run "TestCandidateChecks" green.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Pre-land review must not approve code that deterministic checks would reject, and final land must still guard against race/drift after review. The candidate-cycle plan therefore needs checks before every review cycle and final land checks before closing.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:133-180 defines SubmitWithPreMergeChecks as the canonical land-back gate today, but review currently happens later in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1086-1104.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:150 calls RunPreMergeChecks against baseRev/resultRev as part of landing, not as an explicit pre-review candidate check.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:337-342 requires review before close, but without pre-review checks the reviewer can waste cycles on red candidates.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add CandidateCheckRunner that runs existing pre-merge/check gates against a pinned candidate before reviewer dispatch.
- Keep final land checks in SubmitWithPreMergeChecks or equivalent to catch concurrent target-branch drift after review.
- Attach check artifacts to the candidate cycle evidence.
- On check failure, classify the candidate as candidate-checks-failed and do not call the reviewer for that candidate.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement repair for failed checks yet; this bead may only surface a repair-eligible status/event.
- Do not remove final land checks.

DEPS
Depends on candidate refs so checks can run against a project-root reachable candidate.
notes: Unblocked 2026-05-08: intake.blocked detail claimed the description dropped cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:133, but the current ROOT CAUSE cites cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:133-180 and related review/land lines. This is a valid implementation bead, not an operator decision block.
title: checks: run candidate checks before review and final land

acceptance:
1. WorkerManager.runWorker resolves the ddx binary (os.Executable by default, with a test-only override) and executes ddx work as a child process for bead execution.
2. Spawned ddx work receives MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, OpaquePassthrough, FromRev, profile/model/power, and bead selection fields from the validated worker spec.
3. Production runWorker no longer calls agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig, worker.Run, agent.ExecuteBeadWorker, or a production BeadWorkerFactory execution seam; rg -n 'ExecuteBeadWithConfig|ExecuteBeadWorker|worker.Run\(|BeadWorkerFactory' cli/internal/server/workers.go has no production hits except tests or non-execution docs.
4. TestRunWorker_ExecsDDxWorkSubprocess proves the server launches the subprocess with the expected args/env or spec file.
5. TestRunWorker_ProgressBridge_FromChild proves child progress/status output updates worker state without parsing Fizeau inner transcript logs.
6. TestStopExecuteLoopWorker_KillsSubprocess proves stop/watchdog cancellation signals the child process and records terminal worker state.
7. TestServerSpawnWorker_HonorsAllSpecFields verifies all ExecuteLoopSpec fields reach the subprocess.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run 'TestRunWorker|TestStopExecuteLoopWorker|TestWorkerManager|TestServerSpawnWorker' -count=1 passes.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
DDx server workers still execute bead work in-process instead of spawning the canonical ddx work command. That leaves an alternate execution path inside the server process, duplicates worker lifecycle behavior, and weakens both ADR-022's worker/server boundary and the DDx/Fizeau execution boundary.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:735-969 builds server-local execution configuration and runs bead execution inline instead of delegating to a ddx work subprocess.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go uses BeadWorkerFactory as a production seam around the in-process executor, so server worker behavior can diverge from the installed ddx work CLI.
- Previous attempts parked on missing unified ExecuteLoopSpec wiring; the cleanup still needs to make the subprocess contract explicit and preserve MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, OpaquePassthrough, and FromRev.

PROPOSED FIX
Make WorkerManager.runWorker spawn a ddx subprocess for worker execution, using the current ddx executable by default and a test override for deterministic tests. Translate the validated ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec/ExecuteLoopSpec into ddx work arguments or a spec file, stream child output/events into existing worker status channels, and ensure stop/watchdog behavior signals the child process.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing Fizeau internals.
- Changing bead state semantics.
- Rewriting REST/GraphQL request decoding except as needed to preserve the validated worker spec into the subprocess.

## Why this attempt declined to commit

The bead asks for an end-to-end migration of the server-spawn path from in-process execution to subprocess `exec ddx work`. Six tightly-coupled ACs touch the most intricate file in the server package.

### Actual surface (vs stated estimate)

cli/internal/server/workers.go is 1,959 lines. The current `runWorker` (lines 612–926, ~315 lines) is a dense in-process orchestrator that knits together:

- in-process `agent.ExecuteBeadWorker` construction
- per-project `LandCoordinator` single-flight serialization
- in-process post-merge `DefaultBeadReviewer`
- in-process `escalation.CostCapTracker` (per-worker billed cost accumulation)
- in-process `RoutePreflight` (typed-incompatibility gate via FizeauService)
- per-attempt `singleTierAttempt` closure with config resolution + gate evaluation + leftover-evidence safety net
- progress channel piped to SSE subscribers via `drainProgress`
- watchdog phase-stall tracking via shared workerHandle.lastPhaseTS
- terminal-state preservation (stopped/reaped) merged with loop result
- BeadWorkerFactory injection point for test fakes

Replacing the in-process call with `exec ddx work` collapses all of those in-process couplings — they now run inside the child process — but the parent server still needs equivalent observability/control via:

- /api/workers/{id}/event ingestion (already exists from step 1) for progress
- PID + signal escalation for watchdog/Stop (PID field exists; signal plumbing is partial)
- worker-events.jsonl tail or HTTP-mirrored events for SSE bridging
- Cross-process Stop / wakeCh equivalent

### Test surface

cli/internal/server/workers_test.go alone is 1,107 lines, plus 9 sibling workers_*_test.go files and graphql_subscription_worker_test.go that all assert in-process semantics. Many tests inject `BeadWorkerFactory` for control over the in-process worker; with subprocess execution, those tests need to be replaced by either:

- a built-in subprocess-stub binary (e.g. `go test -test.run … -ddx-fake-work-stub` reentry pattern) the parent execs in tests, or
- deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now belong to ddx work's own test surface).

This is the bulk of the work, not "test updates."

### AC #5 (real-harness end-to-end)

"server-spawned worker (via the new exec path) registers via the new endpoints AND successfully invokes the claude harness (real claude binary, not a stub) on a fixture bead AND completes the attempt with a commit"

Even with the fall-back to a scripted-claude-stub, this requires: a clean fixture repo with a real ready bead, a stub or real claude binary on $PATH inside the test harness, a server bound to a free port, the new exec path wired in, the worker registering against the running server's /api/workers/register, and an end-to-end commit landing through the LandCoordinator (which has moved out of the parent process). That is a substantial new integration test — comparable to the workers_e2e_test.go suite — distinct from the refactor proper.

### Size-check verdict

- More than ~6 ACs across unrelated subsystems? 6 ACs, but several (in-process-replacement refactor, struct deletion, full test sweep, real-harness integration) are independently sized engineering deliverables.
- AC mixes implementation, integration tests, and end-to-end scenarios? Yes (AC #1–4 = refactor, AC #5 = new integration test, AC #6 = full lefthook gate).
- More than ~500 LOC across more than ~5 files? Yes — workers.go rewrite (~400), server.go handler (~70), workers_test.go sweep (~600+), sibling test sweep (~500+), new exec/stub plumbing (~200), new e2e integration test (~300). Estimated 1,500–2,500 LOC of changes.

The bead's "~200 LOC + test updates" estimate (echoing the ADR-022 step 7 roadmap line) materially understates the work.

## Proposed decomposition (orchestrator should create from main repo)

Create the following children of ddx-eccc6efb from the **main repo** (not from an execution worktree — IDs created from a worktree get the worktree dirname as the project prefix and become unusable):

### 7a — runWorker exec rewrite + parent-side subprocess plumbing
Title: ADR-022 step 7a: workers.go runWorker → exec ddx work subprocess (progress via /api/workers/{id}/event, watchdog via PID)
Scope:
- Replace the in-process `worker.Run(...)` body in cli/internal/server/workers.go with `exec.CommandContext` of `ddx work` (resolved via os.Executable() + arg-vec flags + DDX_PROJECT_ROOT env).
- Record child PID on WorkerRecord; ensure watchdog SIGTERM/SIGKILL via PID still works.
- Bridge child-emitted progress events into the existing parent SSE subscriber path — either by POST to /api/workers/{id}/event being multiplexed back to the in-memory progressCh, or by tailing the child's worker-events.jsonl.
- Stop()/cancel/wake plumbing: Stop signals the PID; wakeCh is replaced by sending a SIGUSR1 (or equivalent) handled by ddx work, OR documented as no-op when subprocess.
- Drop in-process LandCoordinator coupling, in-process DefaultBeadReviewer, in-process CostCapTracker, in-process RoutePreflight from this code path.
- Update workers_test.go, workers_costcap_test.go, workers_gates_test.go, workers_watchdog_test.go, workers_wake_test.go, workers_reap_unix.go, workers_stop_test.go, workers_stop_propagation_test.go, workers_e2e_test.go, graphql_subscription_worker_test.go to use a subprocess-stub binary (or test-binary reentry) instead of BeadWorkerFactory injection.
- handleStartExecuteLoopWorker untouched at this point — still uses ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec.
Acceptance: subprocess exec path works; existing test scenarios pass against subprocess-stub; lefthook pre-commit green.
Estimated: ~800–1,000 LOC.

### 7b — handler migration + ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec deletion
Title: ADR-022 step 7b: handleStartExecuteLoopWorker → exec ddx work; delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec
Scope:
- handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes HTTP request → builds argv/env → calls a thinner StartExecuteLoop(opts) that no longer takes the legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct.
- Delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct and its JSON marshalling.
- Update server_test.go, bounded_egress_test.go, federation_*_test.go, and any other call sites that constructed an ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal.
Acceptance: spec struct gone; handler tests assert exec form; full ./internal/server/... + ./cmd/... green.
Estimated: ~300–500 LOC.

### 7c — End-to-end integration test (scripted-claude-stub or opt-in real claude)
Title: ADR-022 step 7c: end-to-end test — server-spawned worker via exec path registers, invokes harness on fixture bead, lands commit
Scope: Build the AC #5 evidence the parent bead requires:
- Spin up a server.Server in-test bound to an ephemeral port.
- Use scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh (federated profile) for a clean ddx-initialized git repo with one ready bead.
- POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop with --once.
- Assert the child ddx work process registers via POST /api/workers/register, claims the bead, the scripted-claude-stub (matching the same exec args + env-var path real claude uses) emits an evidence file, the attempt commits, and the bead closes (or preserves with a known-good status).
- Optional: gate a separate test on `DDX_E2E_REAL_CLAUDE=1` that runs the same scenario against a real `claude` binary on $PATH.
Acceptance: green CI run with scripted-claude-stub end-to-end; opt-in real-claude variant documented.
Estimated: ~400–600 LOC + fixture infra.

### Dependencies
- 7b dep 7a (handler migration assumes runWorker already speaks exec)
- 7c dep 7b (end-to-end via the new exec path requires the new handler shape)

## What blocked this attempt from a clean partial commit

The 6 ACs are tightly interlocking — partial implementation (e.g. just runWorker rewrite without handler migration, or rewrite without test sweep) leaves the package in a non-compiling or red-test state (BeadWorkerFactory injection points either still exist and are dead, or are removed and break tests). A clean intermediate slice requires the explicit decomposition above so each child commits a green tree.

## Bookkeeping

A child bead was inadvertently created from this worktree before the orchestrator-must-be-main-repo issue was identified; its ID `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eccc6efb-20260503T065047-61e4ed20-c4de2d6e` is malformed and the bead has been closed. The orchestrator should disregard it and create the three children above from the main repo where the project prefix resolves to `ddx-`.

acceptance:
1. review_fixable_gap prevents CloseWithEvidence, records a review-fixable-gap event, and schedules one bounded repair cycle with repair_context_from_review_group.
2. TestPreCloseReviewFixableGap_PreventsCloseAndSchedulesRepair covers the close-prevention, event body, repair context, and one-cycle scheduling behavior.
3. TestRetryPolicy_ReviewFixableGapRaisesMinPowerAndPreservesPins proves the repair attempt raises MinPower above the implementer actual power while preserving explicit harness/model/provider/profile pins and leaving MaxPower unset unless the operator explicitly set it.
4. TestRetryPolicy_ReviewFixableGapStopsAfterOneRepairCycle proves the policy cannot loop indefinitely on the same review_group_id/result_rev.
5. Terminal review classes from ddx-c2a66f45 do not schedule repair; TestReviewTerminalClassifications_BlockWithoutNoProgress still passes.
6. Existing TestEscalationLadder_WiredIntoExecutor and TestZeroConfigEscalationIntegration_RealLadderAdvancesMinPower still pass.
7. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestPreCloseReviewFixableGap|TestRetryPolicy_ReviewFixableGap|TestReviewTerminalClassifications|TestEscalationLadder|TestZeroConfigEscalation" -count=1 passes.
8. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... passes.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Fixable review findings currently stop the bead after review instead of scheduling a bounded repair cycle. The old escalation bead ddx-cfedee8e is marked superseded because this scope belongs in C9, but C9 was too broad. This split slice owns exactly one review_fixable_gap repair cycle plus the MinPower bump needed for that repair attempt.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:233-277 records REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK and applies review triage, but it does not schedule a repair attempt with the review context.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:283-353 reduces the review group to one verdict but does not return a repair_context_from_review_group payload to the attempt path.
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go:40-68 escalates MinPower for repeated implementation failures, not for review_fixable_gap repair attempts.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1699-1715 already rebuilds per-attempt config with a requested MinPower while preserving explicit harness/model/provider/profile pins; the repair path needs to reuse that property instead of mutating pins.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_group.go:69-94 records reviewer slots and review_group_id, which is the source data the repair context should reference.

PROPOSED FIX
- Introduce a review_fixable_gap classification distinct from terminal review classes.
- When review_fixable_gap is returned, prevent close, append review-fixable-gap evidence, and schedule exactly one bounded repair cycle using repair_context_from_review_group.
- Raise MinPower for the repair attempt above the implementer's actual power using the available ladder/model catalog, but preserve explicit harness/model/provider/profile pins and do not add a MaxPower cap.
- Ensure spec_gap, missing_acceptance, too_large at depth cap, unsafe/out_of_scope, and exhausted review_error remain terminal operator-required states from ddx-c2a66f45 rather than repair attempts.
- Stop after one repair cycle per candidate/result unless a later bead explicitly extends the policy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement candidate refs or same-worktree repair persistence; candidate ref retention is tracked by ddx-7cb3153d and the candidate-cycle epic.
- Do not change the reviewer prompt beyond schema fields needed to classify fixable vs terminal findings.
- Do not change general implementation-failure escalation beyond the repair attempt.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-c2a66f45 for terminal review classification. This bead supersedes the executable scope of ddx-cfedee8e.

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx doc prose` must run with DDx defaults even when a project has no `.vale.ini`. DDx needs temporary Vale config generation so the public command stays `ddx doc prose` instead of making users manage Vale config.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:109-130 specifies temporary `.vale.ini` generation, packaged `StylesPath`, explicit paths, JSON output, and `--no-global`, but no config generator exists.

PROPOSED FIX
- Generate a temporary `.vale.ini` from DDx defaults and project config.
- Point `StylesPath` at packaged DDx Vale styles.
- Render project vocabulary into Vale vocab/config.
- Keep temp files scoped to the command invocation and clean them up.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not invoke Vale in this bead.
- Do not parse Vale JSON.
- Do not change user-facing findings.

DEPS
Depends on the style-pack skeleton because config generation needs packaged style paths.

acceptance:
1. Child spec bead defines public `once` / `drain` / `watch` / `idle interval` semantics and the `ddx work` stdout grammar, including idle queue status, next-ready-bead transitions, terminal summaries, and human-review blocker explanation.
2. Child implementation beads cover watch/idle CLI semantics with `--poll-interval` removed, loop stop + queue snapshot fields, renderer foundation, DDx lifecycle lines, Fizeau route/progress lines, watch idle/next-ready transition output, and final summaries without exceeding ~3 in-scope files each.
3. `ddx bead list --label plan-2026-05-08-work-stdout` shows this epic plus the spec, watch-mode, queue-snapshot, renderer, lifecycle, Fizeau progress, watch-idle, and final-summary child beads.
4. `ddx bead dep tree ddx-8b7ed960` shows watch-idle rendering depends on the spec, renderer, watch-mode, and queue-snapshot beads.
5. `ddx bead dep tree ddx-dc300be9` shows final summary cleanup depends on lifecycle, Fizeau-progress, and queue-snapshot implementation.
6. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestWork|TestFormatServiceProgress|TestDrainServiceEvents|TestExecuteBeadWorker|TestQueueSnapshot'` passes after children land.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes after children land.
description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work` stdout and mode behavior currently mix DDx lifecycle messages, Fizeau route/progress rows, and final summaries without one human-facing contract. Operators see repeated bead ids, unlabeled model/route lines, vague `pre-claim intake` wording, ambiguous `--poll-interval` semantics, and terminal output that does not explain whether work changed code, why the worker exited, or what queue state remains.

CURRENT STATE / ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:251-265 says layer 3 owns queue-drain records and stop disposition, but it does not define the human stdout grammar for a live drain.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:685-702 describes long-running behavior in terms of `--poll-interval`, conflating mode selection with idle sleep duration.
- cli/cmd/work.go:75 exposes `--poll-interval` as the public control for long-running behavior, but the intended operator concepts are `watch` and `idle interval`.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:656-699 emits idle/active progress signals but does not print human idle queue status or next-ready-bead transitions.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:722-737 writes bead headers and readiness-start text directly with `fmt.Fprintf`, including repeated bead IDs and legacy `pre-claim intake` wording.
- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:185-195 writes Fizeau route/progress lines directly to the same writer without DDx phase context.
- cli/internal/agent/session_log_format.go:331-349 prefixes tool progress with task_id, which often equals the already-scoped bead id.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1885-1937 formats terminal summaries without stop reason, changed/already-satisfied breakdown, queue-state buckets, or human-review blocker explanation.

PROPOSED FIX
Break the work into a spec update plus focused implementation beads:
- Define public `once`, `drain`, `watch`, and `idle interval` semantics.
- Add explicit `--watch` / `--idle-interval` CLI semantics while preserving `--once`.
- Remove `--poll-interval`; do not retain it as a compatibility alias in CLI help, generated docs, or new server/API worker-start inputs.
- Expose stop reason and queue snapshot data, including human-review dependency pressure.
- Add a reusable work-log renderer.
- Route DDx lifecycle lines through it.
- Render Fizeau service events with scoped phase context.
- Render watch idle queue status and next-ready-bead transitions.
- Clean up final summaries.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the structured JSONL event schema or RFC3339 timestamps stored in logs.
- Do not change Fizeau routing policy, ResolveRoute boundaries, retry policy, or actual model selection.
- Do not complete broader terminology cleanup covered by ddx-1c0de0af except where required for `ddx work` stdout.

PARENT / DEPS
Root epic. Child beads carry the executable details and dependency order.
title: EPIC: improve ddx work stdout sequence

acceptance:
1. TestCleanupCommand_DryRunReportsWithoutDeleting verifies dry-run lists stale DDx resources and leaves them on disk.
2. TestCleanupCommand_ApplyRemovesStaleDDxResources verifies apply mode invokes the shared cleanup manager and removes stale temp worktrees/liveness files.
3. TestCleanupCommand_JSONShape verifies JSON output includes removed counts, warnings, bytes/inodes when available, and blocked errors.
4. TestCleanupCommand_DoesNotRemovePreservedEvidence verifies preserved attempts and complete evidence bundles survive command execution.
5. The command is registered in the root CLI and documented in docs/agent-execute.md or adjacent operator docs.
6. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestCleanupCommand|TestExecutionCleanup" -count=1 passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Operators currently have no first-class command to inspect and clean DDx-owned stale execution resources. During the May 6 incident, cleanup required manual shell inspection of /tmp/ddx-exec-wt, git worktree list, and inode usage, which is error-prone and can accidentally delete non-DDx or preserved state.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/command_factory.go:561-562 registers top-level work/try commands but there is no ddx cleanup or doctor cleanup command that invokes the execution cleanup manager.
- cli/cmd/doctor.go:111-202 checks general repo health but does not expose DDx execution temp worktree/liveness cleanup.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:257-279 defines DDx temp worktree locations, but operators must discover and prune them manually.
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:240-244 requires an explicit operator command such as ddx cleanup or ddx doctor cleanup that runs the same manager and reports what it removed.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an operator command, preferably ddx cleanup unless existing CLI conventions strongly favor ddx doctor cleanup.
- Support dry-run and apply behavior if consistent with existing command style; at minimum produce a clear human summary and JSON output.
- Wire the command to the shared cleanup manager, not a separate shell/script implementation.
- Report removed registered worktrees, unregistered dirs, liveness files, warnings, and resource observations; never scan outside configured DDx roots.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add destructive broad system cleanup outside DDx-owned roots.
- Do not remove complete .ddx/runs or .ddx/executions evidence.
- Do not require the command to fix provider quota/auth/network failures.

acceptance:
1. New file `cli/internal/agent/profile_select.go` defines `ProfileSnapshot`, `LoadProfileSnapshot`, `SelectCheapestProfile`, `SelectStrongestProfile`, `SelectStrongestProfileAbove` with the semantics described.
2. New test `TestSelectCheapestProfile_LowestBandWithAvailableModel` in `cli/internal/agent/profile_select_test.go` builds a snapshot with multiple profiles and asserts the cheapest profile whose band has an available model is returned.
3. New test `TestSelectStrongestProfile_HighestBandWithAvailableModel` does the same for strongest.
4. New test `TestSelectStrongestProfileAbove_RespectsFloor` asserts profiles whose band MinPower is below `floor` are excluded.
5. New test `TestSelectProfile_ReturnsEmptyWhenNothingSatisfies` covers all three selectors and asserts empty string with no error when no profile fits.
6. New test `TestLoadProfileSnapshot_MemoizesAndStaleOnError` asserts the snapshot is reused within the cache window and that an error after a successful load returns the last good snapshot rather than failing.
7. New test `TestPreDispatchLintHook_DispatchesWithCheapestProfile` in `cli/internal/agent/lint_hook_test.go` stubs `ListProfiles`/`ListModels` and a service stub that captures `ServiceExecuteRequest`; asserts `req.Profile` equals the cheapest profile selected from the stub's snapshot, `req.Model == ""`, `req.ModelRef == ""`, `req.Harness == ""`, `req.Provider == ""`.
8. New test `TestPostAttemptTriageHook_DispatchesWithCheapestProfile` mirrors AC 7 for the triage hook.
9. New test `TestPreClaimIntakeHook_DispatchesWithStrongestProfileNoStrongPowerTrick` asserts (a) `req.Profile` equals the strongest profile selected from the stub snapshot, (b) `req.MinPower == 0` (no DDx-injected floor), (c) `req.Model == "" && req.ModelRef == "" && req.Harness == "" && req.Provider == ""`, (d) the deleted symbols `resolveStrongSplitterMinPower`/`isStrongPowerUnsatisfiedError`/`isSmartRouteUnavailableError` cannot be referenced (compile-time check via routinglint or a `rg`-based assertion in the test).
10. New test `TestPostMergeReviewer_DispatchesWithStrongestAboveImplPowerAndNoModelPin` in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go` asserts (a) `req.Profile` equals `SelectStrongestProfileAbove(snap, impl.ActualPower+1)`, (b) `req.MinPower >= impl.ActualPower + 1`, (c) `req.Model == "" && req.Harness == ""` from the reviewer dispatch (operator-supplied review-harness flag exception is preserved if explicitly set).
11. `rg -n "ResolveModelTier|resolveStrongSplitterMinPower|isStrongPowerUnsatisfiedError|isSmartRouteUnavailableError" cli/` returns zero hits in non-deleted production source (matches in deletion diffs and routinglint testdata are expected).
12. `rg -n '"smart"|"cheap"|"standard"|"default"' cli/internal/agent/ | grep -v _test.go | grep -v '^[^:]*://'` returns zero hits where the literal is assigned to `ProfileOverride`, `ProfileName`, `Profile:`, or used as a fizeau routing tier name. (Documentation strings and unrelated literals are fine.)
13. `cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go` forbids `ResolveModelTier`, `resolveStrongSplitterMinPower`, `isStrongPowerUnsatisfiedError`, `isSmartRouteUnavailableError`. New test `TestRoutinglint_ForbidsRetiredProfileShadowingSymbols` covers each.
14. New structural test `TestNoDDxInternalCallSiteInventsRoutingPins` (in `cli/internal/agent/`) asserts that across the lint, triage, intake, reviewer, and reviewer-group hook constructors, the dispatched `AgentRunRuntime` has empty `HarnessOverride`, `ModelOverride`, and `ModelRefOverride` (allowing only `ProfileOverride`, `MinPowerOverride`, `MaxPowerOverride`, `ClearProfile`, `ClearMaxPower`).
15. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestSelectCheapestProfile|TestSelectStrongestProfile|TestSelectStrongestProfileAbove|TestSelectProfile_ReturnsEmpty|TestLoadProfileSnapshot|TestPreDispatchLintHook_DispatchesWithCheapestProfile|TestPostAttemptTriageHook_DispatchesWithCheapestProfile|TestPreClaimIntakeHook_DispatchesWithStrongestProfileNoStrongPowerTrick|TestPostMergeReviewer_DispatchesWithStrongestAboveImplPowerAndNoModelPin|TestRoutinglint_ForbidsRetiredProfileShadowingSymbols|TestNoDDxInternalCallSiteInventsRoutingPins" -count=1` passes.
16. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... ./internal/server/... ./tools/lint/routinglint/...` green.
17. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Five DDx call sites talk to fizeau in mixed dialects: hardcoded profile names ("smart", "default", "cheap"), DDx-side model catalog lookups (`ResolveModelTier`), homemade power-floor tricks (`MinPowerOverride = max(all model powers)`), and brittle string-matching retries (`isSmartRouteUnavailableError`). Fizeau is realigning around a single concept — **profile** — defined and enumerated by fizeau's catalog and exposed via `svc.ListProfiles`. DDx-internal callers (lint, triage, intake, reviewer) need to talk to fizeau in that one vocabulary: select a profile by criteria against fizeau's live list, send it as `Profile`. The other knobs (`Harness`, `Provider`, `Model`, `ModelRef`) are **operator-supplied pins for fallback only** — DDx-internal code must never invent them. Power bounds (`MinPower`, `MaxPower`) are a numerical refinement on top of a profile, legitimately used only by the reviewer's `MinPower = impl.actualPower+1` bump.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:91-105` pins `ProfileOverride: "smart"` plus `MinPowerOverride = max(all model powers)` plus `ClearMaxPower: true`. The MinPower trick is a hard floor masquerading as a hint; the "smart" string is brittle to fizeau's policy realignment.
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:122-133` `dispatchPreClaimIntakePayload` retries with empty ModelRef when `isSmartRouteUnavailableError` matches a substring of the dispatch error — exists only because the hardcoded "smart" reference is fragile.
- `cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:128-132` and `cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go:114-118` set no profile at all; both inherit `rcfg.Profile()` (currently broken under default-profile synthesis, addressed structurally by ddx-ea91688b — but the *positive* signal "this is a cheap auxiliary call" is never expressed).
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:806` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_group.go:53` resolve a concrete reviewer model via `ResolveModelTier(reviewHarness, SelectReviewerTier(escalation.TierSmart))`. That call hits the DDx-side `BuiltinCatalog` (deleted by ddx-cd641910) and pins `Model` directly — DDx-internal code inventing a pin, which is the boundary violation this bead also closes.
- Fizeau introspection that DDx is not using: `fizeau/service.go:28-29` (`ListProfiles`, `ResolveProfile`) and `svc.ListModels`. The single existing `ListProfiles` caller (`cli/internal/agent/agent_catalog_shared.go:26`) is being deleted by ddx-cd641910.

PROPOSED FIX
Profile is the only signal DDx-internal code sends. Pins are fallback only. Migrate every auxiliary call site to talk profiles, sourced from fizeau's live list.

1. New file `cli/internal/agent/profile_select.go`:
   - `type ProfileSnapshot struct { Profiles []agentlib.ProfileInfo; Models []agentlib.ModelInfo }`.
   - `func LoadProfileSnapshot(ctx context.Context, svc agentlib.FizeauService) (ProfileSnapshot, error)` — calls `svc.ListProfiles` and `svc.ListModels`, returns both. Per-process 30s memoization keyed on the service identity; stale-on-error (return last good snapshot when fizeau is briefly unreachable).
   - `func SelectCheapestProfile(snap ProfileSnapshot) string` — returns the profile name with the lowest `MaxPower` whose `[MinPower, MaxPower]` band contains at least one `Available && AutoRoutable` model. Empty string if none satisfy. Tie-break: prefer `provider_preference == local-first`, then alphabetical.
   - `func SelectStrongestProfile(snap ProfileSnapshot) string` — symmetric: highest `MinPower` whose band has an available model. Empty string if none.
   - `func SelectStrongestProfileAbove(snap ProfileSnapshot, floor int) string` — same as Strongest but additionally requires the band's `MinPower >= floor`. Empty if none.
   - These are pure functions over the snapshot; they make no service calls.

2. Migrate `cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:128-132`:
   - Load snapshot. Set `runtime.ProfileOverride = SelectCheapestProfile(snap)`. Set `runtime.ClearProfile = true` so any rcfg-level profile from operator flags does not leak into the lint dispatch. Empty result → unconstrained dispatch (still ClearProfile=true).

3. Migrate `cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go:114-118` identically.

4. Migrate `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go`:
   - Replace lines 91-105 with: load snapshot; `runtime.ProfileOverride = SelectStrongestProfile(snap)`; `runtime.ClearProfile = true`. Delete `MinPowerOverride`, `ClearMaxPower`, and any references to `resolveStrongSplitterMinPower`.
   - Delete `resolveStrongSplitterMinPower` (lines 255-271). Delete `isStrongPowerUnsatisfiedError` and `isSmartRouteUnavailableError` (lines 273-297). Delete the retry seam in `dispatchPreClaimIntakePayload` (lines 122-133); collapse to a single dispatch.
   - The "system_unready" / readiness-error outcome path stays: if dispatch returns an error, classify as `PreClaimIntakeError` with the underlying error's text. The resolver's empty-string fallback handles the case where fizeau has no satisfiable profile — the dispatch then runs unconstrained, which is the correct degradation.

5. Migrate `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_group.go`:
   - Reviewer `runtime.ProfileOverride = SelectStrongestProfileAbove(snap, impl.ActualPower+1)`. `runtime.ClearProfile = true`. Keep `runtime.MinPowerOverride = impl.ActualPower+1` as the numerical floor in case the resolved profile's lower bound is below the impl's actual power (defense in depth — fizeau's profile bound and DDx's explicit MinPower are both enforced).
   - Delete every `ResolveModelTier(...)` callsite. Reviewer `Model` is left empty; fizeau picks within the resolved profile.
   - Reviewer routing-fact emission already reads `final.RoutingActual.Model`/`Harness`/`Provider`/`Power` — no change.

6. Boundary invariant: after this bead lands, no DDx-internal call site sets `Harness`, `Provider`, `Model`, or `ModelRef` on `AgentRunRuntime` or `ServiceExecuteRequest`. Those fields flow only from operator CLI flags through `rcfg.Passthrough()` into the implementation dispatch (operator-driven). The pre-claim intake hook's `ProfileOverride: "smart"` literal goes away; the reviewer's `Model` literal goes away.

7. Untouched paths:
   - Implementation dispatch (`execute_bead.go`, the `ddx run`/`try`/`work` operator-flag flow). Operator pins remain opaque passthrough per CONTRACT-003.
   - Benchmark arms (`models.go BenchmarkArm`, `compare_*.go`). Benchmarks legitimately pin `Harness`+`Model` per arm; that is operator-specified config, not DDx-internal invention.

8. Lint anchor: extend `cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go` to forbid identifiers `ResolveModelTier`, `resolveStrongSplitterMinPower`, `isStrongPowerUnsatisfiedError`, `isSmartRouteUnavailableError`. Also add a structural rule (or document the test that enforces it): files in `cli/internal/agent/` other than `service_run.go`, `dispatch.go`, `runner.go`, `agent_runner_service.go`, and the operator-flag-flow set must not assign string literals to `runtime.HarnessOverride`, `runtime.ModelOverride`, or `runtime.ModelRefOverride`. Hardcoded profile names in DDx-internal code (`"smart"`, `"cheap"`, `"default"`, etc. assigned to `ProfileOverride`) are likewise forbidden — the resolver is the only path.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not rename DDx field `ProfileOverride` → `PolicyOverride`. When fizeau renames the wire concept, the resolver gains a one-line update; every call site already routes through it.
- Do not change the `--profile` operator CLI flag surface; ddx-ea91688b owns the empty-default fix on the impl path.
- Do not delete the DDx-side model catalog here; ddx-cd641910 owns that. Whichever lands second drops the now-obsolete touch-points in the other.
- Do not change fizeau's catalog or APIs.

PARENT + DEPS
No parent. No deps. Sibling to:
- ddx-c54b817c (defensive ClearProfile on lint/triage hooks) — fully superseded by this bead's lint/triage migration. Close ddx-c54b817c when this bead lands.
- ddx-ea91688b — independent, impl-path scope.
- ddx-cd641910 — independent, catalog-deletion scope.
title: agent: profile is the only routing concept — migrate DDx-internal calls and forbid pin-invention

description:
PROBLEM
The runs/[runId] page is a single-panel flat view with no tab navigation, no URL-driven tab state, and no separation between Overview, Prompt, Response, Tools, and Session data. Users cannot deep-link to a specific view. Shared sub-components between the runs/[runId] and executions/[executionId] pages are duplicated.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/runs/[runId]/+page.svelte is a monolithic flat page without tab structure.
- Subcomponents for prompt/response/tools/session panels are inlined or missing; no lib/runDetail/ shared library exists.
- URL query parameter ?tab= is not implemented; tab state is not preserved on navigation.
- Depends on ddx-9228a484 (C9) to stabilize the StopCondition / run record shape before building the Session tab.

PROPOSED FIX
- Promote shared subcomponents from cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/executions/[executionId]/+page.svelte into cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/runDetail/ (tab shell, prompt panel, response panel, tools panel, session panel).
- Rebuild cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/runs/[runId]/+page.svelte as tabbed: Overview / Prompt / Response / Tools / Session.
- Implement URL-driven tab state via ?tab=<name> query parameter; deep-link preserves tab on refresh.
- Overview tab: status + last-update timestamp + run/try/work layer chip + base/result rev + link to worker page for running runs.
- Tools tab: paginated 50/page with Load more; lazy-loads on activation.
- Session tab: tokens (prompt/completion/total/cached), billingMode, base/result revs, stderr/stderrPath link.

NON-SCOPE
- Evidence tab (ddx-4cd64068, a child bead).
- New subscription infrastructure (reuse existing workerProgress).

description:
PROBLEM
DDx has overlapping work queued for `ddx agent` removal, try/work refactors, Fizeau transcript cleanup, and routinglint guardrails, but several existing beads still preserve deprecated aliases or target `cli/internal/agent` as the future package. That conflicts with the operator decision: remove, do not deprecate; DDx owns task execution; Fizeau owns routing; and `agent` must disappear from DDx-owned internals unless it literally refers to an external AI agent.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
FEAT-001 currently still has an `Agent Passthrough` section describing `ddx agent` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-001-cli.md:70-84. FEAT-006 still names the upstream boundary as `agentlib.DdxAgent` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md:19-23. FEAT-010 still describes layer 1 as consuming upstream `ddx-agent` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:16-19. Open beads ddx-387a0178 and ddx-3ee4ddcf still preserve deprecated aliases or target `cli/internal/agent`, while upstream Fizeau changes are now tracked in ../fizeau as fizeau-06524f43, fizeau-61668fd3, and fizeau-400b57fe.

PROPOSED FIX
Use this epic as the governing DDx-side sequence: update specs first, wait for/consume the upstream Fizeau contract cleanup release, remove the `ddx agent` public surface with no alias, rename DDx-owned execution packages/types away from `agent`, update skills/generated docs, then add lint guardrails that prevent routing or vocabulary regressions.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement Fizeau model matching or native harness rename in the DDx repo. Do not retain compatibility aliases for `ddx agent`. Do not add DDx-side model/provider/harness defaulting or fuzzy matching. Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`; all sequencing changes go through `ddx bead` commands.

UPSTREAM REFERENCES
- fizeau-06524f43: Align Fizeau contract with DDx routing-boundary cleanup
- fizeau-61668fd3: Rename Fizeau native harness identity from agent to fiz
- fizeau-400b57fe: Define Fizeau-owned fuzzy model constraint resolution

PARENT AND DEPENDENCIES
Parent: ddx-e34994e2. Dependencies: child beads define the execution order.
title: Finalize DDx/Fizeau no-agent routing-boundary cleanup

description:
PROBLEM
This bead was scoped around preferred_reviewer, downgrade routing, and Story 10 ladder fallback, but ADR-024 removed that configuration-heavy behavior from the default path. The still-valid gap is narrower: review turns, including adversarial pre-close review and server review sessions, need bounded budget/refusal behavior and structured errors that do not silently skip review or accidentally close work.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/review_session.go:22 stores review session state but does not define a per-turn refusal/error contract for budget or prompt overflow.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:474 builds reviewer dispatch runtime but does not carry review_group_id/reviewer_index budget/refusal context.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:780 classifies parse/provider-empty errors in the reviewer path, but server review-session refusal codes remain separate and incomplete.
- The previous bead text required preferred_reviewer and downgrade behavior that is no longer part of the default adversarial review policy.

PROPOSED FIX
- Remove preferred_reviewer/downgrade routing from this bead's implementation scope.
- Enforce max_billable_usd for server review sessions and adversarial review turns with structured refusal body when a new turn would exceed the cap.
- Return stable refusal codes PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED, COST_CAP_EXCEEDED, and REVIEWER_UNAVAILABLE where those conditions are truly terminal for a review turn.
- For the pre-close gate, budget/refusal outcomes must feed the review_error retry/exhaustion path rather than being treated as successful review or silently skipped.
- Emit review-pairing-degraded only as telemetry when pairing is weaker than intended; do not use it to downgrade or mutate route pins.

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing the pre-close two-reviewer coordinator; tracked by ddx-c851c3dd.
- GraphQL display of refusal bodies.
- Preferred reviewer policy or configurable reviewer downgrade ladder.

acceptance:
1. `TestFormatLoopResultLine_SuccessUsesSuccessMarker` proves success and already_satisfied statuses render with the success marker and unchanged concise result text.
2. `TestFormatLoopResultLine_FailuresDoNotUseSuccessMarker` proves review malfunction, execution_failed, post_run_check_failed, no_evidence_produced, and preserved-error statuses do not render with `✓`.
3. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_LogLineDoesNotUseSuccessMarkerForReviewError` covers a loop result with `Detail="pre-close review: review-error: transport"` and asserts the emitted log line does not start with `✓`.
4. Existing `formatLoopResult` behavior remains covered for `merged`, `already_satisfied`, `no_changes`, `preserved: ...`, and `error: ...` text.

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work` prints a success-looking terminal line for failed iterations. Operators see output such as `✓ ddx-8fa7ecf6 → error: pre-close review: review-error: transport`, where the leading checkmark contradicts the error detail and makes it unclear whether the bead closed successfully.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1214-1215` always prints `✓ <bead> → <formatted-result>` whenever an iteration finishes, regardless of `ExecuteBeadReport.Status`. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1590-1616` formats failed statuses as `error: ...`, but the caller still prefixes them with the success glyph.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small terminal result-line formatter near `formatLoopResult` that returns both a status marker and the existing concise result text.
- Use a success marker only for `success` and `already_satisfied` results.
- Use a neutral or failure marker for `no_changes`, review malfunction, execution_failed, post-run check failures, and other non-closing statuses so the first token no longer implies success.
- Keep persisted `bead.result` events and status enums unchanged; this bead is CLI/log presentation only.

NON-SCOPE
- Renaming `pre-claim intake` / `pre-dispatch lint` readiness-hook terminology; that is owned by `ddx-1c0de0af`.
- Review retry/manual-required policy; that is owned by `ddx-639af4ef` and related review beads.
- Changing status taxonomy or persisted event schemas; classification work is owned by `ddx-e2479b81`.

description:
PROBLEM
The factory workflow needs to improve or split poor beads before implementation is attempted. Current queue work only covers a Step 0 prompt hint inside the implementer prompt, which is too late: an agent can claim an ambiguous or feature-sized bead, fail, and burn retry budget before the system improves the input.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:680 invokes PreDispatchLintHook before claim, but the hook is lint-oriented and does not expose the required intake outcomes actionable_atomic, actionable_but_rewritten, too_large_decomposed, ambiguous_needs_human, or intake_error.
- cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:86 constructs NewPreDispatchLintHook, which emits lint results but does not safely rewrite bead text or create child beads.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1386 embeds Step 0 decomposition instructions in the implementer prompt, so decomposition happens after dispatch rather than before claim.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1399 still says auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2, while docs/triage/decomposition.md now defines the intake gate and default max_decomposition_depth policy.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a PreClaimIntakeHook/guard that runs after ready selection and routing preflight but before Claim.
- Intake returns one of: actionable_atomic, actionable_but_rewritten, too_large_decomposed, ambiguous_needs_human, intake_error.
- For actionable_but_rewritten, mutate the bead only when the rewrite preserves intent, narrows wording, adds missing file/test references already inferable from repo evidence, and does not invent behavior or scope.
- For too_large_decomposed, create child beads with standalone descriptions/AC, dependency wiring, and a parent triage-decomposed event containing child_ids, rationale, and ac_map. The parent moves to blocked/decomposed instead of being executed.
- For ambiguous_needs_human, block with needs_human/triage:spec-gap labels and an operator-readable explanation.
- Intake errors fail open only when the hook infrastructure fails; a valid ambiguous/too-large decision must block/decompose, not proceed.
- No intake-only outcome counts as no-progress implementation failure.

NON-SCOPE
- Implementer prompt Step 0 cleanup beyond removing contradictions after the pre-claim gate exists.
- Human UI for editing rewritten beads.
- Full skill rubric redesign beyond the outcomes required here.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx now specifies task execution cleanup in FEAT-010 and SD-025, but the implementation still treats execution resource cleanup as incidental worktree removal. The May 6 incident showed stale /tmp/ddx-exec-wt directories and registered orphan worktrees exhausting /tmp inodes, after which ddx work kept claiming more beads and repeated the production-reachability family.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:686-718 creates $TMPDIR/ddx-exec-wt worktrees and defers WorktreeRemove only after WorktreeAdd succeeds; failed setup can leave partial directories and there is no resource preflight before claim/worktree creation.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:711-714 says orphan recovery is the parent responsibility, but ExecuteBeadWorker.Run at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:437-530 does not run startup, loop, or background cleanup.

PROPOSED FIX
Coordinate child beads that implement the cleanup manager, try/work resource preflight, loop-fatal resource exhaustion handling, background cleanup cadence, an operator cleanup command, and an end-to-end regression suite. Align with the existing graceful interrupt epic ddx-99a3be32 so signal shutdown uses the same cleanup manager rather than a separate path.

NON-SCOPE

description:
CONTEXT
DDx should guide agents while they write or edit prose, separate from deterministic post-write checks. This bead ships a default-plugin skill named human-writing-support that adapts the stronger advisory guidance from human-writing-style approaches to DDx: preserve voice, prefer specific detail, avoid generic AI-like tropes, and do not trade away technical precision.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- library/.agents/skills/ddx/human-writing-support/SKILL.md or the default-plugin skill path established by FEAT-011/TD-036
- skills/ddx/human-writing-support/SKILL.md and .agents/.claude materialized copies only if this repo maintains generated checked-in mirrors
- skill validation/eval fixtures under the existing skills validation surface

REQUIRED SKILL BEHAVIOR
- Trigger for writing, rewriting, editing, or reviewing DDx prose, specs, ADRs, beads, docs, website copy, and public prose.
- Explicitly says it is not an AI detector or detector-bypass tool.
- Preserves user voice and technical precision.
- Applies different expectations for technical, planning, and public prose.
- Encourages concrete/checkable detail over generic importance language.
- Directs agents to run deterministic prose checks when available.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- CLI checker implementation.
- Rule pack implementation.
- Bead review integration.

description:
PROBLEM
Plain `ddx work` must run decomposition automatically and it must use a strong model/power floor. Today the typed intake hook exists in agent code, but the production `ddx work` loop does not wire PreClaimIntakeHook or PostAttemptTriageHook, and the existing bead-lifecycle hook helpers dispatch through the resolved worker config without a MinPowerOverride. That means decomposition either does not run at all under `ddx work`, or would inherit the worker's cheap/standard route instead of using a strong splitter.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1919 constructs ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime for the CLI work/execute-loop path but sets only PreClaimHook; it does not set PreClaimIntakeHook, PreDispatchLintHook, or PostAttemptTriageHook.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:908 constructs ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime for server-managed workers but also omits PreClaimIntakeHook, PreDispatchLintHook, and PostAttemptTriageHook.
- cli/cmd/try.go:335 wires lint and triage hooks for `ddx try`, but that does not cover `ddx work`.
- cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:115 and cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go:111 dispatch via AgentRunRuntime without MinPowerOverride, so quality/decomposition prompts inherit rcfg.MinPower().
- cli/internal/agent/types.go:90 and cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:180 show MinPowerOverride is available and reaches ServiceExecuteRequest.MinPower when set.

PROPOSED FIX
- Wire production `ddx work` / execute-loop paths to construct and pass PreClaimIntakeHook, PreDispatchLintHook, and PostAttemptTriageHook by default, matching the reliable factory policy.
- Add a strong-model/power policy for decomposition and other splitter decisions: the decomposition runner must set MinPowerOverride to the configured strong splitter floor, defaulting to the smart/top-power tier floor when no project override is set.
- Preserve passthrough constraints: do not mutate operator-supplied harness/provider/model, but if the strong MinPower cannot be satisfied under those constraints, record agent_power_unsatisfied and block for operator rather than falling back to weak decomposition.
- Server-managed workers and CLI `ddx work` must behave the same way.

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing the child-bead AC-map splitter itself; ddx-008d288f owns that behavior.
- Review adversarial pre-close gate wiring.

description:
PROBLEM
The server-managed worker pre-claim hook still treats every `FetchOriginAncestryCheck` error as an ignorable fetch failure. After the main-worktree safety changes, that call can also fail because the main checkout is on the wrong branch, has staged changes, or is otherwise unsafe for a claim. Swallowing those local safety errors lets server workers claim beads while the coordinator knows the main tree is not safe.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:1617-1628 calls `gitOps.FetchOriginAncestryCheck(projectRoot, branch)` and returns nil for all errors with the comment `Fetch failure is non-fatal`.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:677-691 now runs `FetchOriginAncestryCheck` under the main git lock and calls `ensureLandingWorktreeReady` before remote fetch, so not every error from that method is a remote fetch failure.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1970-1975 already distinguishes local safety errors from `git fetch origin` errors in the CLI pre-claim hook, so the server path is inconsistent.

PROPOSED FIX
- Share or duplicate a small error-classification helper so both CLI and server pre-claim hooks ignore only actual `git fetch origin` failures.
- Propagate local main-worktree safety errors from the server hook, including staged changes, uncommitted tracked changes, unexpected branch, detached HEAD where unsafe, and local git lock/readiness failures.
- Keep the air-gap behavior: a genuine origin fetch failure remains non-fatal and only skips the ancestry check.
- Add tests at the server hook boundary; do not rely only on CLI hook tests.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the land/merge locking implementation.
- Do not make remote fetch mandatory in air-gapped/offline projects.
- Do not change queue ranking or bead claim semantics beyond blocking unsafe local pre-claim state.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-5cb6e6cd. No deps.

description:
PROBLEM
A malformed `.ddx/.git-tracker.lock` path can stop `ddx work` before it claims any bead. The observed incident was a zero-byte regular file at `.ddx/.git-tracker.lock`; `ddx work` then printed repeated lines like `pre-claim hook: tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, owner pid: unknown) (skipping ddx-...)` for every ready candidate.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:130 calls `os.Mkdir(lockDir, 0o755)` and treats every failure as ordinary lock contention.
- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:139-149 only calls `breakStaleTrackerLock(lockDir)` and then sleeps/retries. That stale path only understands a lock directory with `pid` and `acquired_at` sidecars, so a regular file at the lock path is never classified or removed.
- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:156-161 reports `owner pid: unknown` by trying to read `lockDir/pid`, which is impossible when `lockDir` is a regular file.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:499-522 now specifies the required filesystem-shape contract: `.ddx/.git-tracker.lock` is a lock directory; malformed non-directories must be classified before retry/backoff and must not masquerade as `owner pid: unknown` contention.

PROPOSED FIX
- In cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go, classify `os.Mkdir(lockDir)` failures immediately using `os.Lstat(lockDir)`.
- Only enter retry/backoff after the existing path is confirmed to be a directory that represents real lock contention.
- Preserve existing directory stale-lock recovery for old `pid`/`acquired_at` sidecars.
- If `Lstat` shows the lock path disappeared after the failed mkdir, retry acquisition immediately.
- If `Lstat` shows an exact regular file older than `trackerLockStaleAge`, remove it with `os.Remove` only, then retry acquisition immediately.
- If `Lstat` shows a fresh regular file, fail fast with a malformed-lock diagnostic that names `.ddx/.git-tracker.lock` and says a lock directory was expected.
- If `Lstat` shows a symlink, socket, device, or other special file, fail fast and do not remove it.
- Update owner diagnostics so real lock-directory timeouts report `owner pid: <pid>` or `owner pid: missing`; malformed paths must not fall through to `owner pid: unknown`.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the lock path.
- Do not add a new bead status, label, worker state, or event kind.
- Do not change claim semantics, queue ranking, or pre-claim hook policy.
- Do not use `os.RemoveAll` for malformed non-directory paths.
- Do not remove symlinks or special filesystem objects.
- Do not mutate the active server pre-claim hook bead ddx-f6fd75e7; this is a sibling lock primitive hardening bead.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-5cb6e6cd. No deps.
INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-da11a34a introduced the main-git/tracker lock and retry policy; this bead hardens that primitive against malformed ignored lock paths.
- ddx-f6fd75e7 is an active sibling about server pre-claim error propagation. This bead must not expand into that server policy work.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx still tails and renders Fizeau/agent session-log files into human transcript lines. That duplicates Fizeau-owned session rendering and keeps DDx coupled to inner session-log schemas.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/session_log_format.go defines FormatSessionLogLines and related inner-log formatting logic.
- cli/internal/agent/session_log_tailer.go tails agent session logs for display.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:1655-1704 reads agent-*.jsonl files and calls agent.FormatSessionLogLines for active worker output.

PROPOSED FIX
Remove DDx production code that parses Fizeau session-log JSONL or tails those files for live rendering. Worker/server surfaces should use canonical Fizeau ServiceEvents, Fizeau-rendered progress.message fields, and artifact links/pointers without interpreting session-log schemas.

NON-SCOPE
- DDx-owned worker lifecycle progress events.
- Historical artifact retention.
- Fizeau session rendering implementation.

acceptance:
1. `ddx work --help` documents `--watch` and `--idle-interval` as the primary long-running worker surface, with examples for `ddx work`, `ddx work --once`, `ddx work --watch`, and `ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s`.
2. `ddx work --help` and generated CLI docs no longer contain `--poll-interval`; `ddx work --poll-interval=30s` fails with an unknown-flag error.
3. `ddx work` with no watch flag keeps drain-and-exit behavior on an empty execution-ready queue; `TestWorkDefaultDrainModeExitsOnEmptyQueue` covers the default.
4. `ddx work --watch` keeps running across an empty queue until context cancellation or signal; `TestWorkWatchModeStaysAliveWithEmptyQueue` covers the watch path.
5. `ddx work --once` still processes at most one ready bead and exits; existing `TestLoop_OnceFlagStillExits` remains green.
6. `ddx work --once --watch` returns a usage error explaining that `--once` and `--watch` are mutually exclusive.
7. `ddx work --idle-interval 15s` without `--watch` returns a usage error explaining that `--idle-interval` only applies in watch mode.
8. Server-managed workers created through REST and GraphQL default to watch mode with a 30s idle interval unless the request explicitly selects once/drain behavior. New request/input shapes use `mode` and `idle_interval`, not `poll_interval`.
9. The loop/runtime no longer relies on raw `PollInterval > 0` as the only semantic mode test; mode and idle interval are represented explicitly at the command/runtime boundary.
10. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... -run 'TestWork(DefaultDrain|Watch|Once|IdleInterval|PollIntervalRemoved)|TestLoop_(StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue|OnceFlagStillExits|ExplicitDrainExits)|TestStartExecuteLoop'` passes.
11. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work` currently uses `--poll-interval` as the public way to request a long-running worker. That name is ambiguous because drain mode also scans the queue between attempts; the real operator choice is whether the process exits when execution-ready work dries up or keeps watching for newly arriving work.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/work.go:75 defines `--poll-interval` with default 0 and documents it as the continuous scanning control.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:649-682 treats `PollInterval > 0` as the semantic switch from drain-and-exit to idle/watch behavior.
- cli/internal/server/server.go:2425-2437 and cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:112-124 default server-managed workers to a 30s poll interval, but the persisted/request vocabulary still names only the interval, not the worker mode.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md will define the intended vocabulary in ddx-7d9e1e82: `once`, `drain`, `watch`, and `idle interval`, with no `--poll-interval` compatibility alias.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement explicit watch/drain semantics in the CLI and server worker entry points:
- Add `ddx work --watch` for long-running behavior: drain ready work, then remain idle and rescan for newly arriving execution-ready beads.
- Add `ddx work --idle-interval <duration>` for the sleep between empty-queue scans in watch mode, defaulting to 30s when watch mode is active.
- Keep direct CLI `ddx work` as drain-and-exit unless `--watch` is supplied. `ddx work --once` remains the one-bead mode.
- Treat `--once` and `--watch` as incompatible and return a usage error when both are supplied.
- Treat `--idle-interval` without `--watch` as a usage error. The interval tunes watch mode; it does not implicitly select a mode.
- Remove `--poll-interval` from `ddx work` entirely. It is not retained as a compatibility alias. Help and generated docs must not list it.
- Replace new server-managed worker request/input vocabulary with `mode` (`once`, `drain`, `watch`) and `idle_interval`; do not accept `poll_interval` for new worker starts. Existing persisted worker records may be read best-effort if necessary, but this bead must not expose `poll_interval` as a supported input alias.
- Keep server-managed workers in watch mode by default with a 30s idle interval unless the request explicitly asks for once/drain semantics.
- Use one internal representation for mode + idle interval so callers do not branch directly on raw `PollInterval > 0`.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not redesign route/provider/model passthrough.
- Do not change success/failure classification, bead closing policy, cost accounting, or retry cooldown rules.
- Do not implement final human summary wording; ddx-dc300be9 consumes this once loop state is explicit.
- Do not perform ExecuteLoopSpec unification; that remains owned by ddx-29058e2a and its server children.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-5cb6e6cd. Depends on ddx-7d9e1e82 for the documented public semantics.

acceptance:
1. Audit report at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/parallel-struct-audit.md exists, covers at minimum: ddx agent run (CLI + REST + MCP variants), ddx work passthrough behavior, GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter (and any other GraphQL adapters), server worker variants, ddx bead create.
2. For each command: programmatically-generated field tables for each layer (cobra / REST request struct / GraphQL adapter / spec / consumer); diff between layers; explicit drop count per command.
3. Drop-risk score (count of fields dropped between any two layers) explicit per command.
4. Summary table at top of report.
5. For each high-drop-risk command: a follow-up bead filed with ExecuteLoopSpec refactor as template; bead ID listed in the report.
6. For commands judged clean: explicit rationale ("only 1 layer" or "fields collapse via X" — not just "looks fine").
7. ddx try and ddx run, if not shipped, recorded as N/A with one-line rationale; not chased.
8. Report committed to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ and bead closed once review confirms coverage.
description:
The ExecuteLoopSpec unification (ddx-29058e2a) addresses one instance of the parallel-struct smell. The same anti-pattern likely exists in other ddx commands where flags travel cobra → function param → loose map → HTTP request struct → spec struct → consumer. Find them and either:
- File unification beads following the ExecuteLoopSpec pattern, OR
- Document them as already-clean (and why).

COMMANDS TO AUDIT (per codex review — expanded)

1. ddx agent run (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go ~395-414 cobra flags) PLUS its server endpoint /api/agent/run (cli/internal/server/server.go:2150-2188) AND its MCP equivalent (cli/internal/server/server.go:4537-4553). The server endpoint accepts only Harness/Prompt/Model/Effort while cobra has many more flags — same drop risk as ExecuteLoop, narrower fields.
2. ddx work passthrough (alias for ddx agent execute-loop with implicit OpaquePassthrough — covered by ddx-29058e2a but verify the implicit behavior is documented in the spec)
3. GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter (cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137) — covered by ddx-29058e2a explicitly, but audit any OTHER GraphQL adapters with similar patterns
4. Server-side worker variants in cli/internal/server/workers.go — anything else that takes a Spec struct
5. ddx bead create / update — flag set is small but check whether server endpoint mirrors
6. ddx server commands (ddx server workers list/show/log) — read-only, less risk but verify
7. ddx try (cli/cmd/try.go) — SHIPPED. INCLUDE in audit. The runTry function constructs config.CLIOverrides and ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime in parallel to runWork's runAgentExecuteLoopImpl (see comment at try.go:155 acknowledging the duplication). Audit whether the same parallel-struct smell that plagues ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec also exists at the cobra-→-spec boundary for try, and if so file a follow-up unification bead. (Stale 2026-05-04 note "ddx try NOT shipped" was corrected 2026-05-07.)
8. ddx run (cli/cmd/run.go) — SHIPPED. INCLUDE in audit. Layer-1 invocation primitive; flags forward to Fizeau Execute. Lower drop-risk than try/work but verify cobra → service request shape parity.

AUDIT METHOD (per codex — generated diff, not gut-feel)

For each command:
1. Enumerate cobra flag list (programmatic: parse cobra command struct or grep for `cmd.Flags().*Var`).
2. Enumerate corresponding server JSON request struct fields (programmatic: AST parse the request struct in handler).
3. Enumerate the spec/persisted struct fields (programmatic).
4. Enumerate the final consumer fields (programmatic).
5. Diff each layer's field set; classify each field as: execution / control-plane / output-only / unsupported / dropped.
6. Drop-risk score: count of fields that exist in cobra but NOT in subsequent layers.

OUTPUT

Report at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/parallel-struct-audit.md:
- One section per command audited
- For each: layer count, list of fields per layer, classification table, drop-risk score, recommendation (unify / clean / N/A)
- Summary table: command | layer count | drop-risk count | recommendation

If unification candidates found, file follow-up beads using the ExecuteLoopSpec refactor (ddx-29058e2a) as the template.

DEP CHANGE (per codex)
- DEP on ddx-29058e2a is RELAXED to "may begin read-only command discovery + diff generation in parallel; only the implementation-pattern recommendations need ddx-29058e2a as reference." Update dep semantics so this can start now.
title: audit: parallel-struct smell — find other ddx commands with multi-layer flag wiring

acceptance:
1. Execute-loop calls the review-group pre-close gate before CloseWithEvidence for close-eligible implementation results.
2. TestPreCloseReview_UnanimousApproveCloses verifies both evidence-backed APPROVE results are required to close.
3. TestPreCloseReview_ApproveWithoutEvidenceIsReviewError verifies evidence-free APPROVE does not close and records review_error: unparseable.
4. TestPreCloseReview_RequestChangesPreventsClose verifies REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK leave the bead unclosed and do not invoke Reopen.
5. Stale reopen assertions in execute_bead_review_test.go are updated to pre-close blocking semantics.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestPreCloseReview" -count=1 passes.
7. TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FiveConcurrentBeads_AllLanded either passes or is confirmed pre-existing via git-log evidence documented in the commit message.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
After review-group dispatch exists, execute-loop must use it as a pre-close gate. Today review REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK semantics are still expressed as reopen behavior rather than preventing close.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:294 exposes CloseWithEvidence without a required pre-close review decision boundary.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:300 documents Reopen for post-close REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:242 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:279 assert reopen behavior for REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK (line numbers may have shifted after prior attempts; verify before editing).

PRIOR ATTEMPT CONTEXT
- result_rev 3b03e5a: implementation present and review-gate tests written; review step itself was malformed (review_error: unparseable), merge never landed.
- result_rev aca87ab: TestPreCloseReview suite passes; commit blocked by TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FiveConcurrentBeads_AllLanded (4 of 5 beads closed, missing HEAD:bead-0005.txt). Prior agent confirmed this failure is not introduced by the review-gate change.

FIRST ACTION
Before writing any code, run `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestPreCloseReview' -count=1`. If all three TestPreCloseReview_* cases pass, skip re-implementation and proceed directly to the integration-test verification step below.

PROPOSED FIX
- Invoke the review-group coordinator before CloseWithEvidence for every close-eligible implementation result.
- Close only when both reviewers APPROVE and each approval includes per-AC evidence.
- Treat APPROVE without evidence as review_error: unparseable.
- REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK prevent close and record the appropriate review event without closing/reopening the bead.

INTEGRATION TEST GATE
After the review-gate implementation is verified, run `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FiveConcurrentBeads_AllLanded' -count=1 -v`. If it fails, determine via `git log --oneline` and targeted blame whether the failure predates this bead's changes. If pre-existing: document the git-log evidence in the commit message and proceed. If introduced by this bead: fix it before committing.

NON-SCOPE
- Repair-cycle escalation and review-error retry budget.
- Root-cause fix of the integration test if failure is confirmed pre-existing.
